units.text: Enter KENT, GLOUCESTER, and EDMUND

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
I thought the king had more affected the Duke of Albany
than Cornwall.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
It did always seem so to us. But now in the division of
the kingdom, it appears not which of the dukes he
values most, for equalities are so weighed that
curiosity in neither can make choice of either’s moiety.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: [indicating EDMUND ] Is not this your son, my lord?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
His breeding, sir, hath been at my charge. I have so
often blushed to acknowledge him that now I am brazed to
it.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: I cannot conceive you.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Sir, this young fellow’s mother could, whereupon she
grew round-wombed, and had indeed, sir, a son for her
cradle ere she had a husband for her bed. Do you smell a
fault?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
I cannot wish the fault undone, the issue of it being
so proper.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
But I have, sir, a son by order of law, some year older
than this, who yet is no dearer in my account. Though
this knave came something saucily to the world before he
was sent for, yet was his mother fair, there was good
sport at his making, and the whoreson must be
acknowledged.— Do you know this noble gentleman, Edmund?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: No, my lord.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
My lord of Kent. Remember him hereafter as my
honorable friend.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: My services to your lordship.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: I must love you and sue to know you better.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Sir, I shall study deserving.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: He hath been out nine years, and away he shall again.
Sennet.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: A trumpet call announces the arrival of the king.
Enter one bearing a coronet, then King LEAR, then the Dukes of CORNWALL and ALBANY, next GONERIL, REGAN, CORDELIA, and attendants

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Attend the lords of France and Burgundy, Gloucester.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: I shall, my lord.
Exit GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Meantime we shall express our darker purpose.—
Give me the map there.— Know that we have divided
In three our kingdom, and ’tis our fast intent
To shake all cares and business from our age,
Conferring them on younger strengths while we
Unburdened crawl toward death.— Our son of Cornwall,
And you, our no less loving son of Albany,
We have this hour a constant will to publish
Our daughters' several dowers, that future strife
May be prevented now.
The two great princes, France and Burgundy,
Great rivals in our youngest daughter’s love,
Long in our court have made their amorous sojourn,
And here are to be answered.— Tell me, my daughters,
(Since now we will divest us both of rule,
Interest of territory, cares of state)
Which of you shall we say doth love us most
That we our largest bounty may extend
Where nature doth with merit challenge?— G oneril,
Our eldest born, speak first.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Sir, I do love you more than words can wield the
matter,
Dearer than eyesight, space, and liberty,
Beyond what can be valued, rich or rare,
No less than life, with grace, health, beauty, honor,
As much as child e'er loved or father found—
A love that makes breath poor and speech unable.
Beyond all manner of so much I love you.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
[Aside] What shall Cordelia speak? Love, and be
silent.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Of all these bounds, even from this line to this,
With shadowy forests and with champains riched,
With plenteous rivers and wide-skirted meads,
We make thee lady. To thine and Albany’s issue
Be this perpetual.—What says our second daughter,
Our dearest Regan, wife of Cornwall? Speak.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Sir, I am made of that self mettle as my sister,
And prize me at her worth. In my true heart,
I find she names my very deed of love—
Only she comes too short, that I profess
Myself an enemy to all other joys,
Which the most precious square of sense possesses.
And find I am alone felicitate
In your dear highness' love.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
[Aside] Then poor Cordelia!
And yet not so, since I am sure my love’s
More ponderous than my tongue.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
To thee and thine hereditary ever
Remain this ample third of our fair kingdom,
No less in space, validity, and pleasure
Than that conferred on Goneril.— But now, our joy,
Although our last and least, to whose young love
The vines of France and milk of Burgundy
Strive to be interessed. What can you say to draw
A third more opulent than your sisters? Speak.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: Nothing, my lord.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Nothing?

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: Nothing.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: How? Nothing will come of nothing. Speak again.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
Unhappy that I am, I cannot heave
My heart into my mouth. I love your majesty
According to my bond, no more nor less.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
How, how, Cordelia? Mend your speech a little,
Lest you may mar your fortunes.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
Good my lord,
You have begot me, bred me, loved me. I
Return those duties back as are right fit—
Obey you, love you, and most honor you.
Why have my sisters husbands if they say
They love you all? Haply when I shall wed
That lord whose hand must take my plight shall carry
Half my love with him, half my care and duty.
Sure, I shall never marry like my sisters,
To love my father all.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: But goes thy heart with this?

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: Ay, good my lord.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: So young and so untender?

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: So young, my lord, and true.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Let it be so. Thy truth then be thy dower.
For by the sacred radiance of the sun,
The mysteries of Hecate and the night,
By all the operation of the orbs
From whom we do exist and cease to be—
Here I disclaim all my paternal care,
Propinquity, and property of blood,
And as a stranger to my heart and me
Hold thee from this for ever. The barbarous Scythian,
Or he that makes his generation messes
To gorge his appetite, shall to my bosom
Be as well neighbored, pitied, and relieved
As thou my sometime daughter.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Good my liege—

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Peace, Kent.
Come not between the dragon and his wrath.
I loved her most and thought to set my rest
On her kind nursery. [To CORDELIA] Hence, and avoid my
sight!—
So be my grave my peace as here I give
Her father’s heart from her.— Call France. Who stirs?
Call Burgundy.—
Exeunt several attendants

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Cornwall and Albany,
With my two daughters' dowers digest this third.
Let pride, which she calls plainness, marry her.
I do invest you jointly with my power,
Preeminence, and all the large effects
That troop with majesty. Ourself, by monthly course,
With reservation of an hundred knights
By you to be sustained, shall our abode
Make with you by due turns. Only shall we retain
The name, and all th' additions to a king.
The sway, revenue, execution of the rest,
Belovèd sons, be yours; which to confirm,
This coronet part between you.
[Gives CORNWALL and ALBANY the coronet]

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Royal Lear,
Whom I have ever honored as my king,
Loved as my father, as my master followed,
As my great patron thought on in my prayers—

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: The bow is bent and drawn. Make from the shaft.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Let it fall rather, though the fork invade
The region of my heart. Be Kent unmannerly
When Lear is mad. What wouldst thou do, old man?
Think’st thou that duty shall have dread to speak
When power to flattery bows? To plainness honor’s bound
When majesty falls to folly. Reserve thy state,
And in thy best consideration check
This hideous rashness. Answer my life my judgment,
Thy youngest daughter does not love thee least,
Nor are those empty-hearted whose low sound
Reverbs no hollowness.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Kent, on thy life, no more.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
My life I never held but as a pawn
To wage against thy enemies , nor fear to lose it,
Thy safety being motive.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Out of my sight!

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
See better, Lear, and let me still remain
The true blank of thine eye.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Now, by Apollo—

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Now, by Apollo, King,
Thou swear’st thy gods in vain.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: O vassal! Miscreant!

units.speaker: ALBANY CORNWALL
units.text: Dear sir, forbear!

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Do, kill thy physician, and the fee bestow
Upon thy foul disease. Revoke thy gift,
Or whilst I can vent clamor from my throat,
I’ll tell thee thou dost evil.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Hear me, recreant! On thine allegiance hear me.
That thou hast sought to make us break our vows,
Which we durst never yet, and with strained pride
To come betwixt our sentence and our power,
Which nor our nature nor our place can bear,
Our potency made good, take thy reward:
Five days we do allot thee for provision
To shield thee from diseases of the world.
And on the sixth to turn thy hated back
Upon our kingdom. If on the next day following
Thy banished trunk be found in our dominions,
The moment is thy death. Away! By Jupiter,
This shall not be revoked.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Why, fare thee well, King. Sith thus thou wilt appear,
Freedom lives hence, and banishment is here.
[To CORDELIA]
The gods to their dear shelter take thee, maid,
That justly think’st and hast most rightly said!
[To REGAN and GONERIL]
And your large speeches may your deeds approve,
That good effects may spring from words of love.—
Thus Kent, O princes, bids you all adieu.
He’ll shape his old course in a country new.
Exit KENT
Flourish. Enter GLOUCESTER with the King of FRANCE, the Duke of BURGUNDY, and attendants

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Here’s France and Burgundy, my noble lord.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
My lord of Burgundy.
We first address towards you, who with this king
Hath rivaled for our daughter. What in the least
Will you require in present dower with her
Or cease your quest of love?

units.speaker: BURGUNDY
units.text:
Most royal majesty,
I crave no more than hath your highness offered.
Nor will you tender less.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Right noble Burgundy,
When she was dear to us we did hold her so,
But now her price is fallen. Sir, there she stands.
If aught within that little seeming substance,
Or all of it, with our displeasure pieced
And nothing more, may fitly like your grace,
She’s there, and she is yours.

units.speaker: BURGUNDY
units.text: I know no answer.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Sir, will you, with those infirmities she owes—
Unfriended, new adopted to our hate,
Dowered with our curse and strangered with our oath—
Take her or leave her?

units.speaker: BURGUNDY
units.text:
Pardon me, royal sir.
Election makes not up in such conditions.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Then leave her, sir, for by the power that made me,
I tell you all her wealth.
[To FRANCE] For you, great King,
I would not from your love make such a stray
To match you where I hate. Therefore beseech you
T' avert your liking a more worthier way
Than on a wretch whom Nature is ashamed
Almost t' acknowledge hers.

units.speaker: FRANCE
units.text:
This is most strange,
That she that even but now was your best object—
The argument of your praise, balm of your age,
Most best, most dearest— should in this trice of time
Commit a thing so monstrous to dismantle
So many folds of favor. Sure, her offense
Must be of such unnatural degree
That monsters it (or your fore-vouched affection
Fall into taint), which to believe of her
Must be a faith that reason without miracle
Could never plant in me.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
[To LEAR] I yet beseech your majesty,
If for I want that glib and oily art
To speak and purpose not— since what I well intend,
I’ll do ’t before I speak— that you make known
It is no vicious blot, murder, or foulness,
No unchaste action or dishonored step
That hath deprived me of your grace and favor,
But even for want of that for which I am richer:
A still-soliciting eye and such a tongue
As I am glad I have not, though not to have it
Hath lost me in your liking.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Go to, go to. Better thou
Hadst not been born than not t' have pleased me
better.

units.speaker: FRANCE
units.text:
Is it no more but this—a tardiness in nature
Which often leaves the history unspoke
That it intends to do?— My lord of Burgundy,
What say you to the lady? Love’s not love
When it is mingled with regards that stands
Aloof from th' entire point. Will you have her?
She is herself a dowry.

units.speaker: BURGUNDY
units.text:
[To LEAR] Royal King,
Give but that portion which yourself proposed,
And here I take Cordelia by the hand,
Duchess of Burgundy.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Nothing. I have sworn. I am firm.

units.speaker: BURGUNDY
units.text:
[To CORDELIA] I am sorry then. You have so lost a
father
That you must lose a husband.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
Peace be with Burgundy.
Since that respects and fortunes are his love,
I shall not be his wife.

units.speaker: FRANCE
units.text:
Fairest Cordelia, that art most rich being poor,
Most choice forsaken, and most loved despised!
Thee and thy virtues here I seize upon,
Be it lawful I take up what’s cast away.
Gods, gods! 'Tis strange that from their cold’st
neglect
My love should kindle to inflamed respect.—
Thy dowerless daughter, King, thrown to my chance,
Is queen of us, of ours, and our fair France.
Not all the dukes of waterish Burgundy
Can buy this unprized precious maid of me.—
Bid them farewell, Cordelia, though unkind.
Thou losest here, a better where to find.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Thou hast her, France. Let her be thine, for we
Have no such daughter, nor shall ever see
That face of hers again. [To CORDELIA] Therefore be
gone
Without our grace, our love, our benison.—
Come, noble Burgundy.
Flourish
Exeunt all but FRANCE, GONERIL, REGAN, and CORDELIA

units.speaker: FRANCE
units.text: Bid farewell to your sisters.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
The jewels of our father, with washed eyes
Cordelia leaves you. I know you what you are,
And like a sister am most loath to call
Your faults as they are named. Love well our father.
To your professèd bosoms I commit him.
But yet, alas, stood I within his grace,
I would prefer him to a better place.
So farewell to you both.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Prescribe not us our duty.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Let your study
Be to content your lord, who hath received you
At fortune’s alms. You have obedience scanted,
And well are worth the want that you have wanted.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
Time shall unfold what plighted cunning hides,
Who covers faults at last with shame derides.
Well may you prosper.

units.speaker: FRANCE
units.text: Come, my fair Cordelia.
Exeunt FRANCE and CORDELIA

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Sister, it is not a little I have to say of what most
nearly appertains to us both. I think our father will
hence tonight.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: That’s most certain, and with you. Next month with us.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
You see how full of changes his age is. The observation
we have made of it hath not been little. He always
loved our sister most, and with what poor judgment he
hath now cast her off appears too grossly.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
'Tis the infirmity of his age. Yet he hath ever but
slenderly known himself.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
The best and soundest of his time hath been but rash.
Then must we look from his age to receive not alone the
imperfections of long-engrafted condition, but
therewithal the unruly waywardness that infirm and
choleric years bring with them.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Such unconstant starts are we like to have from him as
this of Kent’s banishment.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
There is further compliment of leave-taking between
France and him. Pray you, let’s sit together. If our
father carry authority with such dispositions as he
bears, this last surrender of his will but offend us.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: We shall further think on ’t.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: We must do something, and i' th' heat.
Exeunt
Enter EDMUND the bastard, with a letter

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
Thou, nature, art my goddess. To thy law
My services are bound. Wherefore should I
Stand in the plague of custom and permit
The curiosity of nations to deprive me
For that I am some twelve or fourteen moonshines
Lag of a brother? Why “bastard?” Wherefore “base?”
When my dimensions are as well compact,
My mind as generous, and my shape as true
As honest madam’s issue? Why brand they us
With “base,” with “baseness,” “bastardy,” “base,”
“base”—
Who in the lusty stealth of nature take
More composition and fierce quality
Than doth within a dull, stale, tirèd bed
Go to th' creating a whole tribe of fops
Got ’tween a sleep and wake? Well then,
Legitimate Edgar, I must have your land.
Our father’s love is to the bastard Edmund
As to the legitimate.— Fine word, “legitimate!”—
Well, my legitimate, if this letter speed
And my invention thrive, Edmund the base
Shall top th' legitimate. I grow, I prosper.
Now, gods, stand up for bastards!
Enter GLOUCESTER. EDMUND looks over his letter

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Kent banished thus? And France in choler parted?
And the king gone tonight, prescribed his power
Confined to exhibition? All this done
Upon the gad?—Edmund, how now? What news?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: [pocketing the letter] So please your lordship, none.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Why so earnestly seek you to put up that letter?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: I know no news, my lord.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: What paper were you reading?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Nothing, my lord.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
No? What needed, then, that terrible dispatch of it
into your pocket? The quality of nothing hath not such
need to hide itself. Let’s see. Come, if it be nothing,
I shall not need spectacles.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
I beseech you, sir, pardon me. It is a letter from my
brother that I have not all o'er-read. And for so much
as I have perused, I find it not fit for your
o'erlooking.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Give me the letter, sir.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
I shall offend, either to detain or give it. The
contents, as in part I understand them, are to blame.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: [taking the letter] Let’s see, let’s see.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
I hope, for my brother’s justification, he wrote this
but as an essay or taste of my virtue.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
[reads] “This policy and reverence of age makes the
world bitter to the best of our times, keeps our
fortunes from us till our oldness cannot relish them. I
begin to find an idle and fond bondage in the oppression
of aged tyranny, who sways not as it hath power but as
it is suffered. Come to me, that of this I may speak
more. If our father would sleep till I waked him, you
should enjoy half his revenue forever, and live the
beloved of your brother,
Edgar.”
Hum, conspiracy? “'Sleep till I wake him, you should
enjoy half his revenue”—my son Edgar? Had he a hand to
write this, a heart and brain to breed it in? When came
this to you? Who brought it?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
It was not brought me, my lord. There’s the cunning of
it.
I found it thrown in at the casement of my closet.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: You know the character to be your brother’s?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
If the matter were good, my lord, I durst swear it
were his.
But in respect of that, I would fain think it were not.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: It is his.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
It is his hand, my lord, but I hope his heart is not in
the contents.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Hath he never heretofore sounded you in this business?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
Never, my lord. But I have heard him oft maintain it to
be fit that, sons at perfect age and fathers declined,
the father should be as ward to the son, and the son
manage his revenue.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
O villain, villain! His very opinion in the letter!
Abhorred villain! Unnatural, detested, brutish
villain—worse than brutish! Go, sirrah, seek him. I’ll
apprehend him. Abominable villain! Where is he?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
I do not well know, my lord. If it shall please you to
suspend your indignation against my brother till you
can derive from him better testimony of his intent, you
shall run a certain course— where if you violently
proceed against him, mistaking his purpose, it would
make a great gap in your own honor and shake in pieces
the heart of his obedience. I dare pawn down my life for
him that he hath wrote this to feel my affection to
your honor and to no other pretense of danger.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Think you so?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
If your honor judge it meet, I will place you where you
shall hear us confer of this and by an auricular
assurance have your satisfaction—and that without any
further delay than this very evening.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: He cannot be such a monster—

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Nor is not, sure.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
To his father, that so tenderly and entirely loves
him. Heaven and earth! Edmund, seek him out, wind me
into him, I pray you. Frame the business after your own
wisdom. I would unstate myself to be in a due
resolution.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
I will seek him, sir, presently, convey the business as
I shall find means, and acquaint you withal.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
These late eclipses in the sun and moon portend no good
to us. Though the wisdom of nature can reason it thus
and thus, yet nature finds itself scourged by the
sequent effects. Love cools, friendship falls off,
brothers divide, in cities mutinies, in countries
discord, in palaces treason, and the bond cracked ’twixt
son and father. This villain of mine comes under the
prediction—there’s son against father. The king falls
from bias of nature—there’s father against child. We
have seen the best of our time. Machinations,
hollowness, treachery, and all ruinous disorders follow
us disquietly to our graves. Find out this villain,
Edmund. It shall lose thee nothing. Do it carefully.—And
the noble and true-hearted Kent banished, his offense
honesty! 'Tis strange, strange.
Exit GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
This is the excellent foppery of the world that when we
are sick in fortune—often the surfeit of our own
behavior—we make guilty of our disasters the sun, the
moon, and the stars, as if we were villains by
necessity, fools by heavenly compulsion, knaves,
thieves, and treachers by spherical predominance,
drunkards, liars, and adulterers by an enforced
obedience of planetary influence, and all that we are
evil in by a divine thrusting-on. An admirable evasion
of whoremaster man, to lay his goatish disposition to
the charge of a star! My father compounded with my
mother under the dragon’s tail and my nativity was under
Ursa Major, so that it follows I am rough and
lecherous. Fut, I should have been that I am, had the
maidenliest star in the firmament twinkled on my
bastardizing. Edgar—
Enter EDGAR

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
and pat on ’s cue he comes like the catastrophe of the
old comedy. My cue is villainous melancholy, with a sigh
like Tom o' Bedlam. Oh, these eclipses do portend these
divisions! Fa, sol, la, mi.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
How now, brother Edmund? What serious contemplation are
you in?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
I am thinking, brother, of a prediction I read this
other day, what should follow these eclipses.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Do you busy yourself about that?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
I promise you, the effects he writes of succeed
unhappily — as of unnaturalness between the child and
the parent, death, dearth, dissolutions of ancient
amities, divisions in state, menaces and maledictions
against king and nobles, needless diffidences,
banishment of friends, dissipation of cohorts, nuptial
breaches, and I know not what.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: How long have you been a sectary astronomical?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Come, come. When saw you my father last?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Why, the night gone by.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Spake you with him?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Ay, two hours together.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
Parted you in good terms? Found you no displeasure in
him by word or countenance?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: None at all.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
Bethink yourself wherein you may have offended him.
And at my entreaty forbear his presence till some little
time hath qualified the heat of his displeasure, which
at this instant so rageth in him that with the mischief
of your person it would scarcely allay.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Some villain hath done me wrong.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
That’s my fear. I pray you, have a continent
forbearance till the speed of his rage goes slower. And
as I say, retire with me to my lodging, from whence I
will fitly bring you to hear my lord speak. Pray ye, go.
There’s my key. If you do stir abroad, go armed.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Armed, brother?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
Brother, I advise you to the best. Go armed. I am no
honest man if there be any good meaning towards you. I
have told you what I have seen and heard—but faintly,
nothing like the image and horror of it. Pray you, away.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Shall I hear from you anon?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: I do serve you in this business.
Exit EDGAR

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
A credulous father, and a brother noble—
Whose nature is so far from doing harms
That he suspects none, on whose foolish honesty
My practices ride easy. I see the business.
Let me, if not by birth, have lands by wit.
All with me’s meet that I can fashion fit.
Exit
Enter GONERIL and her steward OSWALD

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Did my father strike my gentleman
For chiding of his fool?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Ay, madam.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
By day and night he wrongs me. Every hour
He flashes into one gross crime or other
That sets us all at odds. I’ll not endure it.
His knights grow riotous, and himself upbraids us
On every trifle. When he returns from hunting,
I will not speak with him. Say I am sick.
If you come slack of former services,
You shall do well. The fault of it I’ll answer.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: He’s coming, madam. I hear him.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Put on what weary negligence you please,
You and your fellow servants. I’ll have it come to
question.
If he distaste it, let him to our sister,
Whose mind and mine I know in that are one,
Not to be overruled. Idle old man
That still would manage those authorities
That he hath given away! Now by my life,
Old fools are babes again and must be used
With checks as flatteries, when they are seen abused.
Remember what I have said.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Very well, madam.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
And let his knights have colder looks among you.
What grows of it, no matter. Advise your fellows so.
I would breed from hence occasions, and I shall,
That I may speak. I’ll write straight to my sister
To hold my very course. Go, prepare for dinner.
Exeunt severally
Enter KENT disguised

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
If but as well I other accents borrow,
That can my speech diffuse, my good intent
May carry through itself to that full issue
For which I razed my likeness. Now, banished Kent,
If thou canst serve where thou dost stand condemned,
So may it come thy master, whom thou lovest,
Shall find thee full of labors.
Horns within. Enter LEAR with attendant knights

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Let me not stay a jot for dinner. Go get it ready.
Exit attendant

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: [To KENT] How now, what art thou?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: A man, sir.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: What dost thou profess? What wouldst thou with us?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
I do profess to be no less than I seem—to serve him
truly that will put me in trust, to love him that is
honest, to converse with him that is wise and says
little, to fear judgment, to fight when I cannot choose,
and to eat no fish.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: What art thou?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: A very honest-hearted fellow, and as poor as the king.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
If thou beest as poor for a subject as he’s for a king,
thou'rt poor enough. What wouldst thou?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Service.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Who wouldst thou serve?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: You.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Dost thou know me, fellow?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
No, sir. But you have that in your countenance which I
would fain call master.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: What’s that?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Authority.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: What services canst thou do?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
I can keep honest counsel, ride, run, mar a curious
tale in telling it, and deliver a plain message bluntly.
That which ordinary men are fit for, I am qualified in.
And the best of me is diligence.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: How old art thou?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Not so young, sir, to love a woman for singing, nor so
old to dote on her for anything. I have years on my back
forty- eight.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Follow me. Thou shalt serve me. If I like thee no worse
after dinner, I will not part from thee yet.—Dinner,
ho, dinner! Where’s my knave, my fool?—Go you, and call
my fool hither.
Exit attendant
Enter OSWALD the steward

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: You, you, sirrah, where’s my daughter?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: So please you—
Exit OSWALD

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: What says the fellow there? Call the clotpoll back.
Exit FIRST KNIGHT

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Where’s my fool, ho? I think the world’s asleep.
Enter FIRST KNIGHT

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: How now? Where’s that mongrel?

units.speaker: FIRST KNIGHT
units.text: He says, my lord, your daughter is not well.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Why came not the slave back to me when I called him?

units.speaker: FIRST KNIGHT
units.text:
Sir, he answered me in the roundest manner he would
not.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: He would not?

units.speaker: FIRST KNIGHT
units.text:
My lord, I know not what the matter is, but to my
judgment your highness is not entertained with that
ceremonious affection as you were wont. There’s a great
abatement of kindness appears as well in the general
dependants as in the duke himself also, and your
daughter.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Ha! Sayest thou so?

units.speaker: FIRST KNIGHT
units.text:
I beseech you pardon me, my lord, if I be mistaken—for
my duty cannot be silent when I think your highness
wronged.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Thou but rememberest me of mine own conception. I have
perceived a most faint neglect of late, which I have
rather blamed as mine own jealous curiosity than as a
very pretense and purpose of unkindness. I will look
further into ’t. But where’s my fool? I have not seen
him this two days.

units.speaker: FIRST KNIGHT
units.text:
Since my young lady’s going into France, sir, the fool
hath much pined away.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
No more of that. I have noted it well. Go you and tell
my daughter I would speak with her.
Exit an attendant

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Go you, call hither my fool.
Exit another attendant
Enter OSWALD

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: O you sir, you, come you hither, sir. Who am I, sir?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: My lady’s father.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
“My lady’s father?” My lord’s knave, your whoreson dog!
You slave, you cur!

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: I am none of these, my lord. I beseech your pardon.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Do you bandy looks with me, you rascal?
[He strikes OSWALD ]

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: I’ll not be strucken, my lord.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
[tripping OSWALD]
Nor tripped neither, you base football player.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
[To KENT] I thank thee, fellow. Thou servest me, and
I’ll love thee.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
[To OSWALD] Come, sir, arise, away! I’ll teach you
differences. Away, away. If you will measure your
lubber’s length again, tarry. But away, go to. Have you
wisdom? So.
Exit OSWALD

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Now, my friendly knave, I thank thee.
Enter FOOL

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: [gives KENT money] There’s earnest of thy service.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Let me hire him too.—Here’s my coxcomb.
[offers KENT his cap]

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: How now, my pretty knave? How dost thou?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: [To KENT] Sirrah, you were best take my coxcomb.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Why, Fool?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Why? For taking one’s part that’s out of favor. Nay,
an thou canst not smile as the wind sits, thou'lt catch
cold shortly. There, take my coxcomb. Why, this fellow
has banished two on ’s daughters, and did the third a
blessing against his will. If thou follow him, thou must
needs wear my coxcomb.— How now, nuncle? Would I had two
coxcombs and two daughters.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Why, my boy?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
If I gave them all my living, I’d keep my coxcombs
myself.
There’s mine. Beg another of thy daughters.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Take heed, sirrah—the whip.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Truth’s a dog that must to kennel. He must be whipped
out, when Lady Brach may stand by th' fire and stink.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: A pestilent gall to me!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Sirrah, I’ll teach thee a speech.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Do.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Mark it, nuncle.
Have more than thou showest,
Speak less than thou knowest,
Lend less than thou owest,
Ride more than thou goest,
Learn more than thou trowest,
Set less than thou throwest,
Leave thy drink and thy whore
And keep in-a-door,
And thou shalt have more
Than two tens to a score.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: This is nothing, Fool.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Then ’tis like the breath of an unfee’d lawyer. You
gave me nothing for ’t.—Can you make no use of nothing,
nuncle?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Why no, boy. Nothing can be made out of nothing.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[To KENT] Prithee, tell him so much the rent of his
land comes to. He will not believe a fool.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: A bitter fool.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Dost thou know the difference, my boy, between a bitter
fool and a sweet fool?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: No, lad. Teach me.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
That lord that counseled thee
To give away thy land,
Come place him here by me.
Do thou for him stand.
The sweet and bitter fool
Will presently appear—
The one in motley here,
The other found out there.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Dost thou call me fool, boy?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
All thy other titles thou hast given away that thou
wast born with.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: This is not altogether fool, my lord.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
No, faith, lords and great men will not let me. If I
had a monopoly out, they would have part on ’t. And
ladies too— they will not let me have all fool to
myself; they’ll be snatching. Give me an egg, nuncle,
and I’ll give thee two crowns.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: What two crowns shall they be?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Why—after I have cut the egg i' th' middle and eat up
the meat—the two crowns of the egg. When thou clovest
thy crown i' th' middle, and gavest away both parts,
thou borest thy ass o' th' back o'er the dirt. Thou
hadst little wit in thy bald crown when thou gavest thy
golden one away. If I speak like myself in this, let him
be whipped that first finds it so.
[Sings]
Fools had ne'er less wit in a year,
For wise men are grown foppish.
They know not how their wits to wear,
Their manners are so apish.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: When were you wont to be so full of songs, sirrah?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
I have used it, nuncle, ever since thou madest thy
daughters thy mothers. For when thou gavest them the
rod, and put’st down thine own breeches,
[Sings]
Then they for sudden joy did weep
And I for sorrow sung,
That such a king should play bo-peep
And go the fools among.
Prithee, nuncle, keep a schoolmaster that can teach thy
fool to lie. I would fain learn to lie.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: An you lie, sirrah, we’ll have you whipped.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
I marvel what kin thou and thy daughters are. They’ll
have me whipped for speaking true, thou'lt have me
whipped for lying, and sometimes I am whipped for
holding my peace. I had rather be any kind o' thing than
a fool. And yet I would not be thee, nuncle. Thou hast
pared thy wit o' both sides and left nothing i' th'
middle. Here comes one o' the parings.
Enter GONERIL

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
How now, daughter? What makes that frontlet on?
Methinks you are too much of late i' th' frown.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[To LEAR] Thou wast a pretty fellow when thou hadst no
need to care for her frowning. Now thou art an O without
a figure. I am better than thou art now. I am a fool.
Thou art nothing.
[To GONERIL] Yes, forsooth, I will hold my tongue. So
your face bids me, though you say nothing.
Mum, mum,
He that keeps nor crust nor crumb,
Weary of all, shall want some.
[indicates LEAR] That’s a shelled peascod.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
[To LEAR] Not only, sir, this your all-licensed fool,
But other of your insolent retinue
Do hourly carp and quarrel, breaking forth
In rank and not-to-be-endurèd riots. Sir,
I had thought by making this well known unto you
To have found a safe redress, but now grow fearful
By what yourself too late have spoke and done
That you protect this course and put it on
By your allowance— which if you should, the fault
Would not ’scape censure, nor the redresses sleep
Which in the tender of a wholesome weal
Might in their working do you that offense,
Which else were shame, that then necessity
Will call discreet proceeding.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
For you know, nuncle,
The hedge-sparrow fed the cuckoo so long,
That it’s had it head bit off by it young.
So out went the candle and we were left darkling.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Are you our daughter?

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Come, sir,
I would you would make use of that good wisdom
Whereof I know you are fraught, and put away
These dispositions that of late transform you
From what you rightly are.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
May not an ass know when the cart draws the horse?
Whoop, Jug! I love thee.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Does any here know me? Why, this is not Lear.
Doth Lear walk thus? Speak thus? Where are his eyes?
Either his notion weakens, or his discernings
Are lethargied. Ha, sleeping or waking?
Sure, ’tis not so.
Who is it that can tell me who I am?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Lear’s shadow.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
I would learn that. For by the marks
Of sovereignty, knowledge, and reason,
I should be false persuaded I had daughters.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Which they will make an obedient father.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: [to GONERIL] Your name, fair gentlewoman?

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
This admiration, sir, is much o' th' savor
Of other your new pranks. I do beseech you
To understand my purposes aright.
As you are old and reverend, should be wise.
Here do you keep a hundred knights and squires,
Men so disordered, so debauched and bold
That this our court, infected with their manners,
Shows like a riotous inn. Epicurism and lust
Make it more like a tavern or a brothel
Than a graced palace. The shame itself doth speak
For instant remedy. Be then desired
By her that else will take the thing she begs,
A little to disquantity your train,
And the remainder that shall still depend
To be such men as may besort your age,
Which know themselves and you.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Darkness and devils!
Saddle my horses. Call my train together.—
Degenerate bastard, I’ll not trouble thee.
Yet have I left a daughter.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
You strike my people, and your disordered rabble
Make servants of their betters.
Enter ALBANY

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Woe that too late repents!—
[To ALBANY] O sir, are you come?
Is it your will? Speak, sir.—Prepare my horses.
Exit attendant

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Ingratitude, thou marble-hearted fiend,
More hideous when thou show’st thee in a child
Than the sea monster.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: Pray, sir, be patient.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
[To GONERIL] Detested kite, thou liest!
My train are men of choice and rarest parts
That all particulars of duty know
And in the most exact regard support
The worships of their name. O most small fault,
How ugly didst thou in Cordelia show,
That, like an engine, wrenched my frame of nature
From the fixed place, drew from heart all love,
And added to the gall! O Lear, Lear, Lear!
[strikes his head]
Beat at this gate that let thy folly in
And thy dear judgment out!—Go, go, my people.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
My lord, I am guiltless, as I am ignorant,
Of what hath moved you.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
It may be so, my lord.
Hear, Nature, hear, dear goddess, hear!
Suspend thy purpose if thou didst intend
To make this creature fruitful.
Into her womb convey sterility.
Dry up in her the organs of increase,
And from her derogate body never spring
A babe to honor her. If she must teem,
Create her child of spleen, that it may live
And be a thwart disnatured torment to her.
Let it stamp wrinkles in her brow of youth,
With cadent tears fret channels in her cheeks,
Turn all her mother’s pains and benefits
To laughter and contempt, that she may feel—
That she may feel
How sharper than a serpent’s tooth it is
To have a thankless child.— Away, away!
Exeunt LEAR, FOOL, KENT, FIRST KNIGHT and the other attendants

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: Now gods that we adore, whereof comes this?

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Never afflict yourself to know more of it,
But let his disposition have that scope
That dotage gives it.
Enter LEAR and FOOL

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
What, fifty of my followers at a clap?
Within a fortnight?

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: What’s the matter, sir?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
I’ll tell thee.
[To GONERIL] Life and death! I am ashamed
That thou hast power to shake my manhood thus,
That these hot tears which break from me perforce
Should make thee worth them. Blasts and fogs upon thee!
Th' untented woundings of a father’s curse
Pierce every sense about thee! Old fond eyes,
Beweep this cause again, I’ll pluck ye out
And cast you, with the waters that you loose,
To temper clay. Yea, is ’t come to this?
Ha? Let it be so. I have another daughter,
Who I am sure is kind and comfortable.
When she shall hear this of thee, with her nails
She’ll flay thy wolvish visage. Thou shalt find
That I’ll resume the shape which thou dost think
I have cast off for ever. Thou shalt, I warrant thee.
Exit LEAR

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: Do you mark that, my lord?

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
I cannot be so partial, Goneril,
To the great love I bear you—

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Pray you, content.
Come, sire, no more.—What, Oswald, ho!
[to FOOL] You, sir, more knave than fool, after your
master.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Nuncle Lear, nuncle Lear, tarry and take the fool with
thee.
A fox when one has caught her
And such a daughter
Should sure to the slaughter,
If my cap would buy a halter.
So the fool follows after.
Exit FOOL

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
This man hath had good counsel—a hundred knights!
'Tis politic and safe to let him keep
At point a hundred knights, yes, that on every dream,
Each buzz, each fancy, each complaint, dislike,
He may enguard his dotage with their powers
And hold our lives in mercy?— Oswald, I say!

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: Well, you may fear too far.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Safer than trust too far.
Let me still take away the harms I fear,
Not fear still to be taken. I know his heart.
What he hath uttered I have writ my sister.
If she sustain him and his hundred knights
When I have showed th' unfitness—
Enter OSWALD the steward

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Here, madam.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
How now, Oswald?
What, have you writ that letter to my sister?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Ay, madam.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Take you some company, and away to horse.
Inform her full of my particular fear,
And thereto add such reasons of your own
As may compact it more. Get you gone
And hasten your return.
Exit OSWALD

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
No, no, my lord,
This milky gentleness and course of yours
Though I condemn not, yet, under pardon
You are much more attasked for want of wisdom
Than praised for harmful mildness.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
How far your eyes may pierce I cannot tell.
Striving to better, oft we mar what’s well.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: Nay, then—

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: Well, well, th' event.
Exeunt
Enter LEAR, KENT disguised, and FOOL

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
[to KENT, giving him letters] Go you before to
Gloucester with these letters. Acquaint my daughter no
further with anything you know than comes from her
demand out of the letter. If your diligence be not
speedy, I shall be there afore you.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
I will not sleep, my lord, till I have delivered your
letter.
Exit KENT

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
If a man’s brains were in ’s heels, were ’t not in
danger of kibes?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Ay, boy.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Then, I prithee, be merry. Thy wit shall ne'er go
slipshod.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Ha, ha, ha!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Shalt see thy other daughter will use thee kindly. For
though she’s as like this as a crab’s like an apple, yet
I can tell what I can tell.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Why, what canst thou tell, my boy?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
She will taste as like this as a crab does to a crab.
Thou canst tell why one’s nose stands i' th' middle on
’s face?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: No.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Why, to keep one’s eyes of either side ’s nose, that
what a man cannot smell out, he may spy into.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: I did her wrong—

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Canst tell how an oyster makes his shell?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: No.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Nor I neither. But I can tell why a snail has a house.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Why?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Why, to put ’s head in—not to give it away to his
daughters and leave his horns without a case.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
I will forget my nature. So kind a father!—Be my horses
ready?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Thy asses are gone about 'em. The reason why the seven
stars are no more than seven is a pretty reason.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Because they are not eight?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Yes indeed. Thou wouldst make a good fool.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: To take ’t again perforce—Monster ingratitude!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
If thou wert my fool, nuncle, I’d have thee beaten for
being old before thy time.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: How’s that?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Thou shouldst not have been old till thou hadst been
wise.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
O, let me not be mad, not mad, sweet heaven!
I would not be mad.
Keep me in temper. I would not be mad.
Enter a GENTLEMAN

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: How now? Are the horses ready?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: Ready, my lord.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Come, boy.
Exeunt LEAR and GENTLEMAN

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
She that’s a maid now and laughs at my departure,
Shall not be a maid long unless things be cut shorter.
Exit
Enter EDMUND the bastard and CURAN, severally

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Save thee, Curan.

units.speaker: CURAN
units.text:
And you, sir. I have been with your father and given
him notice that the Duke of Cornwall and Regan his
duchess will be here with him this night.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: How comes that?

units.speaker: CURAN
units.text:
Nay, I know not. You have heard of the news abroad?—I
mean the whispered ones, for they are yet but
ear-kissing arguments.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Not I. Pray you, what are they?

units.speaker: CURAN
units.text:
Have you heard of no likely wars toward ’twixt the two
Dukes of Cornwall and Albany?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Not a word.

units.speaker: CURAN
units.text: You may do then in time. Fare you well, sir.
Exit CURAN

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
The duke be here tonight? The better—best!
This weaves itself perforce into my business.
My father hath set guard to take my brother.
And I have one thing, of a queasy question,
Which I must act. Briefness and fortune, work!—
Brother, a word. Descend, brother, I say.
Enter EDGAR

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
My father watches. O sir, fly this place.
Intelligence is given where you are hid.
You have now the good advantage of the night.
Have you not spoken 'gainst the Duke of Cornwall aught?
He’s coming hither—now, i' th' night, i' th' haste,
And Regan with him. Have you nothing said
Upon his party 'gainst the Duke of Albany?
Advise yourself.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: I am sure on ’t, not a word.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
I hear my father coming. Pardon me.
In cunning I must draw my sword upon you.
Draw. Seem to defend yourself. Now quit you well.—
[loudly] Yield! Come before my father. Light, ho! Here!
[aside to EDGAR ] Fly, brother, fly.
[loudly]
Torches, torches!
[aside to EDGAR ] So, farewell.
Exit EDGAR

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
Some blood drawn on me would beget opinion
Of my more fierce endeavor.
[cuts his own arm]
I have seen drunkards
Do more than this in sport.— Father, father!—
Stop, stop!—No help?
Enter GLOUCESTER and servants with torches

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Now Edmund, where’s the villain?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
Here stood he in the dark, his sharp sword out,
Mumbling of wicked charms, conjuring the moon
To stand ’s auspicious mistress—

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: But where is he?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Look, sir, I bleed.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Where is the villain, Edmund?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Fled this way, sir, when by no means he could—

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Pursue him, ho! Go after.
Exeunt some servants

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: “By no means” what?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
Persuade me to the murder of your lordship,
But that I told him the revenging gods
'Gainst parricides did all the thunder bend,
Spoke with how manifold and strong a bond
The child was bound to th' father. Sir, in fine,
Seeing how loathly opposite I stood
To his unnatural purpose, in fell motion,
With his preparèd sword he charges home
My unprovided body, latched mine arm.
And when he saw my best alarumed spirits,
Bold in the quarrel’s right, roused to the encounter,
Or whether ghasted by the noise I made,
Full suddenly he fled.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Let him fly far.
Not in this land shall he remain uncaught.
And found—dispatch. The noble duke my master,
My worthy arch and patron, comes tonight.
By his authority I will proclaim it
That he which finds him shall deserve our thanks,
Bringing the murderous coward to the stake.
He that conceals him, death.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
When I dissuaded him from his intent,
And found him pight to do it, with cursed speech
I threatened to discover him. He replied,
“Thou unpossessing bastard! Dost thou think
If I would stand against thee, would the reposal
Of any trust, virtue, or worth in thee
Make thy words faithed? No. What I should deny—
As this I would, ay, though thou didst produce
My very character—I’d turn it all
To thy suggestion, plot, and damnèd practice.
And thou must make a dullard of the world,
If they not thought the profits of my death
Were very pregnant and potential spirits
To make thee seek it.”
Tucket within

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
O strange and fastened villain!
Would he deny his letter, said he? I never got him.—
Hark, the duke’s trumpets. I know not why he comes.
All ports I’ll bar. The villain shall not ’scape.
The duke must grant me that. Besides, his picture
I will send far and near, that all the kingdom
May have the due note of him.— And of my land,
Loyal and natural boy, I’ll work the means
To make thee capable.
Enter the Duke of CORNWALL, REGAN, and attendants

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
How now, my noble friend? Since I came hither,
Which I can call but now, I have heard strange news.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
If it be true, all vengeance comes too short
Which can pursue th' offender. How dost, my lord?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: O madam, my old heart is cracked, it’s cracked.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
What, did my father’s godson seek your life?—
He whom my father named, your Edgar?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: O, lady, lady, shame would have it hid.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Was he not companion with the riotous knights
That tend upon my father?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: I know not, madam. 'Tis too bad, too bad.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Yes, madam, he was of that consort.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
No marvel then, though he were ill affected.
'Tis they have put him on the old man’s death,
To have th' expense and spoil of his revenues.
I have this present evening from my sister
Been well informed of them—and with such cautions
That if they come to sojourn at my house
I’ll not be there.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
Nor I, assure thee, Regan.—
Edmund, I hear that you have shown your father
A childlike office.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: It was my duty, sir.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
He did bewray his practice, and received
This hurt you see striving to apprehend him.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Is he pursued?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Ay, my good lord.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
If he be taken, he shall never more
Be feared of doing harm. Make your own purpose
How in my strength you please.— For you, Edmund,
Whose virtue and obedience doth this instant
So much commend itself, you shall be ours.
Natures of such deep trust we shall much need.
You we first seize on.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
I shall serve you, sir,
Truly, however else.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: [to CORNWALL] For him I thank your grace.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: You know not why we came to visit you—

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Thus out of season, threading dark-eyed night.
Occasions, noble Gloucester, of some poise,
Wherein we must have use of your advice:
Our father he hath writ, so hath our sister,
Of differences, which I least thought it fit
To answer from our home. The several messengers
From hence attend dispatch. Our good old friend,
Lay comforts to your bosom, and bestow
Your needful counsel to our business,
Which craves the instant use.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
I serve you, madam.
Your graces are right welcome.
Flourish. Exeunt
Enter KENT disguised and OSWALD the steward, severally

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Good dawning to thee, friend. Art of this house?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Ay.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Where may we set our horses?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: I' th' mire.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Prithee, if thou lovest me, tell me.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: I love thee not.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Why, then, I care not for thee.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
If I had thee in Lipsbury pinfold, I would make thee
care for me.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Why dost thou use me thus? I know thee not.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Fellow, I know thee.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: What dost thou know me for?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
A knave, a rascal, an eater of broken meats; a base,
proud, shallow, beggarly, three-suited, hundred-pound,
filthy, worsted-stocking knave; a lily-livered,
action-taking knave; a whoreson, glass-gazing,
super-serviceable finical rogue; one-trunk-inheriting
slave; one that wouldst be a bawd in way of good
service; and art nothing but the composition of a knave,
beggar, coward, pander, and the son and heir of a
mongrel bitch; one whom I will beat into clamorous
whining if thou deniest the least syllable of thy
addition.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text:
Why, what a monstrous fellow art thou, thus to rail on
one that is neither known of thee nor knows thee!

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
What a brazen-faced varlet art thou to deny thou
knowest me! Is it two days ago since I tripped up thy
heels and beat thee before the king? Draw, you rogue,
for though it be night yet the moon shines. I’ll make a
sop o' th' moonshine of you. [draws his sword] Draw, you
whoreson cullionly barber-monger, draw!

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Away! I have nothing to do with thee.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Draw, you rascal. You come with letters against the
king and take Vanity the puppet’s part against the
royalty of her father. Draw, you rogue, or I’ll so
carbonado your shanks. Draw, you rascal! Come your ways.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Help, ho! Murder! Help!

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Strike, you slave. Stand, rogue. Stand, you neat
slave, strike! [strikes OSWALD]

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Help, ho! Murder, murder!
Enter EDMUND the bastard with his rapier drawn, the Duke of CORNWALL, the Duchess REGAN, GLOUCESTER, and servants

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: How now? What’s the matter? Part.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
[to EDMUND] With you, goodman boy, if you please. Come,
I’ll flesh ye. Come on, young master.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Weapons, arms? What’s the matter here?

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
Keep peace, upon your lives.
He dies that strikes again. What is the matter?

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: The messengers from our sister and the king.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: What is your difference? Speak.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: I am scarce in breath, my lord.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
No marvel, you have so bestirred your valor. You
cowardly rascal, nature disclaims in thee. A tailor made
thee.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Thou art a strange fellow. A tailor make a man?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Ay, a tailor, sir. A stone-cutter or painter could not
have made him so ill though they had been but two years
o' th' trade.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Speak yet. How grew your quarrel?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text:
This ancient ruffian, sir, whose life I have spared at
suit of his gray beard—

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Thou whoreson zed, thou unnecessary letter!—My lord, if
you will give me leave, I will tread this unbolted
villain into mortar and daub the wall of a jakes with
him.—Spare my gray beard, you wagtail?

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
Peace, sirrah!
You beastly knave, know you no reverence?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Yes, sir, but anger hath a privilege.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Why art thou angry?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
That such a slave as this should wear a sword,
Who wears no honesty. Such smiling rogues as these,
Like rats, oft bite the holy cords atwain
Which are too intrinse t' unloose, smooth every passion
That in the natures of their lords rebel,
Bring oil to fire, snow to the colder moods;
Renege, affirm, and turn their halcyon beaks
With every gale and vary of their masters,
Knowing naught, like dogs, but following.—
A plague upon your epileptic visage!
Smile you my speeches as I were a fool?
Goose, an I had you upon Sarum plain,
I’d drive ye cackling home to Camelot.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Why, art thou mad, old fellow?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
[to KENT] How fell you out?
Say that.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
No contraries hold more antipathy
Than I and such a knave.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Why dost thou call him “knave?” What’s his offense?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: His countenance likes me not.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: No more perchance does mine, nor his, nor hers.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Sir, ’tis my occupation to be plain.
I have seen better faces in my time
Than stands on any shoulder that I see
Before me at this instant.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
This is some fellow,
Who, having been praised for bluntness, doth affect
A saucy roughness and constrains the garb
Quite from his nature. He cannot flatter, he.
An honest mind and plain, he must speak truth.
An they will take it, so. If not, he’s plain.
These kind of knaves I know, which in this plainness
Harbor more craft and more corrupter ends
Than twenty silly-ducking observants
That stretch their duties nicely.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Sir, in good faith, or in sincere verity,
Under th' allowance of your great aspect,
Whose influence, like the wreath of radiant fire
On flickering Phoebus' front—

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: What mean’st by this?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
To go out of my dialect, which you discommend so much.
I know, sir, I am no flatterer. He that beguiled you in
a plain accent was a plain knave, which for my part I
will not be, though I should win your displeasure to
entreat me to ’t.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: [to OSWALD] What was th' offense you gave him?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text:
I never gave him any.
It pleased the king his master very late
To strike at me upon his misconstruction
When he, conjunct and flattering his displeasure,
Tripped me behind; being down, insulted, railed,
And put upon him such a deal of man
That worthied him, got praises of the king
For him attempting who was self-subdued.
And in the fleshment of this dread exploit
Drew on me here again.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
None of these rogues and cowards
But Ajax is their fool.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
Fetch forth the stocks, ho!—
You stubborn ancient knave, you reverend braggart,
We’ll teach you.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Sir, I am too old to learn.
Call not your stocks for me. I serve the king,
On whose employment I was sent to you.
You shall do small respect, show too bold malice
Against the grace and person of my master,
Stocking his messenger.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
Fetch forth the stocks!
As I have life and honor, there shall he sit till noon.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Till noon? Till night, my lord, and all night too.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Why, madam, if I were your father’s dog,
You should not use me so.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Sir, being his knave, I will.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
This is a fellow of the selfsame color
Our sister speaks of.—Come, bring away the stocks!

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Let me beseech your grace not to do so.
His fault is much, and the good king his master
Will check him for ’t. Your purposed low correction
Is such as basest and contemned’st wretches
For pilferings and most common trespasses
Are punished with.
The king his master needs must take it ill,
That he, so slightly valued in his messenger,
Should have him thus restrained.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: I’ll answer that.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
My sister may receive it much more worse
To have her gentleman abused, assaulted
For following her affairs.—Put in his legs.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: [to GLOUCESTER ] Come, my good lord, away.
Exeunt all but GLOUCESTER and KENT

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
I am sorry for thee, friend. 'Tis the duke’s pleasure,
Whose disposition, all the world well knows,
Will not be rubbed nor stopped. I’ll entreat for thee.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Pray you do not, sir. I have watched and traveled hard.
Some time I shall sleep out. The rest I’ll whistle.
A good man’s fortune may grow out at heels.
Give you good morrow.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: The duke’s to blame in this. 'Twill be ill taken.
Exit GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Good King, that must approve the common saw,
Thou out of heaven’s benediction comest
To the warm sun.
[takes out a letter]
Approach, thou beacon to this underglobe,
That by thy comfortable beams I may
Peruse this letter. Nothing almost sees miracles
But misery. I know ’tis from Cordelia,
Who hath most fortunately been informed
Of my obscurèd course and [reads the letter] “shall
find time
From this enormous state, seeking to give
Losses their remedies.” All weary and o'erwatched,
Take vantage, heavy eyes, not to behold
This shameful lodging.
Fortune, good night. Smile once more. Turn thy wheel.
[sleeps]
Enter EDGAR

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
I heard myself proclaimed,
And by the happy hollow of a tree
Escaped the hunt. No port is free, no place
That guard and most unusual vigilance
Does not attend my taking. Whiles I may ’scape,
I will preserve myself, and am bethought
To take the basest and most poorest shape
That ever penury in contempt of man
Brought near to beast. My face I’ll grime with filth,
Blanket my loins, elf all my hair in knots,
And with presented nakedness outface
The winds and persecutions of the sky.
The country gives me proof and precedent
Of Bedlam beggars, who with roaring voices
Strike in their numbed and mortified bare arms
Pins, wooden pricks, nails, sprigs of rosemary,
And with this horrible object from low farms,
Poor pelting villages, sheepcotes, and mills,
Sometime with lunatic bans, sometime with prayers,
Enforce their charity. “Poor Turlygod!” “Poor Tom!”—
That’s something yet. Edgar I nothing am.
Exit
KENT in the stocks. Enter LEAR, FOOL, and GENTLEMAN

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
'Tis strange that they should so depart from home,
And not send back my messenger.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
As I learned,
The night before there was no purpose in them
Of this remove.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: [to LEAR] Hail to thee, noble master!

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Ha! Makest thou this shame thy pastime?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: No, my lord.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Ha, ha! Look, he wears cruel garters. Horses are tied
by the heads, dogs and bears by the neck, monkeys by the
loins, and men by the legs. When a man’s overlusty at
legs, then he wears wooden nether-stocks.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
[to KENT] What’s he that hath so much thy place mistook
To set thee here?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
It is both he and she:
Your son and daughter.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: No.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Yes.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: No, I say.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: I say “Yea.”

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: No, no, they would not.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Yes, they have.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: By Jupiter, I swear “No.”

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: By Juno, I swear “Ay.”

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
They durst not do ’t.
They could not, would not do ’t. 'Tis worse than murder
To do upon respect such violent outrage.
Resolve me with all modest haste which way
Thou mightst deserve or they impose this usage,
Coming from us.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
My lord, when at their home
I did commend your highness' letters to them.
Ere I was risen from the place that showed
My duty kneeling, came there a reeking post,
Stewed in his haste, half breathless, panting forth
From Goneril his mistress salutations,
Delivered letters spite of intermission,
Which presently they read, on whose contents
They summoned up their meiny, straight took horse,
Commanded me to follow and attend
The leisure of their answer, gave me cold looks.
And meeting here the other messenger,
Whose welcome I perceived had poisoned mine—
Being the very fellow which of late
Displayed so saucily against your highness—
Having more man than wit about me, drew.
He raised the house with loud and coward cries.
Your son and daughter found this trespass worth
The shame which here it suffers.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Winter’s not gone yet, if the wild geese fly that way.
Fathers that wear rags
Do make their children blind.
But fathers that bear bags
Shall see their children kind.
Fortune, that arrant whore,
Ne'er turns the key to th' poor.
But for all this thou shalt have as many dolors for thy
daughters as thou canst tell in a year.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
O, how this mother swells up toward my heart!
Hysterica passio , down, thou climbing sorrow.
Thy element’s below.—Where is this daughter?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: With the earl, sir, here within.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Follow me not. Stay here.
Exit LEAR

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: Made you no more offense but what you speak of?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
None.
How chance the king comes with so small a train?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
An thou hadst been set i' th' stocks for that
question, thou’dst well deserved it.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Why, Fool?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
We’ll set thee to school to an ant to teach thee
there’s no laboring i' th' winter. All that follow their
noses are led by their eyes but blind men, and there’s
not a nose among twenty but can smell him that’s
stinking. Let go thy hold when a great wheel runs down a
hill, lest it break thy neck with following it. But the
great one that goes up the hill, let him draw thee
after. When a wise man gives thee better counsel, give
me mine again. I would have none but knaves follow it
since a fool gives it.
That sir which serves and seeks for gain,
And follows but for form,
Will pack when it begins to rain
And leave thee in the storm.
But I will tarry. The fool will stay.
And let the wise man fly.
The knave turns fool that runs away;
The fool, no knave, perdie.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Where learned you this, Fool?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Not i' th' stocks, fool.
Enter LEAR and GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Deny to speak with me? They are sick? They are weary?
They have traveled all the night?—mere fetches, ay!
The images of revolt and flying off.
Fetch me a better answer.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
My dear lord,
You know the fiery quality of the duke,
How unremoveable and fixed he is
In his own course.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Vengeance, plague, death, confusion!
“Fiery?” What “quality?” Why, Gloucester, Gloucester,
I’d speak with the Duke of Cornwall and his wife.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Well, my good lord, I have informed them so.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: “Informed them?” Dost thou understand me, man?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Ay, my good lord.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
The king would speak with Cornwall. The dear father
Would with his daughter speak, commands, tends service.
Are they “informed” of this? My breath and blood!
“Fiery?” The “fiery” duke? Tell the hot duke that Lear—
No, but not yet. Maybe he is not well.
Infirmity doth still neglect all office
Whereto our health is bound. We are not ourselves
When nature, being oppressed, commands the mind
To suffer with the body. I’ll forbear,
And am fallen out with my more headier will
To take the indisposed and sickly fit
For the sound man.
[notices KENT again]
Death on my state! Wherefore
Should he sit here? This act persuades me
That this remotion of the duke and her
Is practice only. Give me my servant forth.
Go tell the duke and ’s wife I’d speak with them—
Now, presently. Bid them come forth and hear me,
Or at their chamber door I’ll beat the drum
Till it cry sleep to death.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: I would have all well betwixt you.
Exit GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: O me, my heart, my rising heart! But down.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Cry to it, nuncle, as the cockney did to the eels when
she put 'em i' th' paste alive. She knapped 'em o' th'
coxcombs with a stick and cried, “Down, wantons, down!”
'Twas her brother that, in pure kindness to his horse,
buttered his hay.
Enter the Duke of CORNWALL, REGAN, GLOUCESTER, and servants

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Good morrow to you both.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Hail to your grace.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: I am glad to see your highness.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Regan, I think you are. I know what reason
I have to think so: if thou shouldst not be glad,
I would divorce me from thy mother’s tomb,
Sepulchring an adultress.
[to KENT] Oh, are you free?
Some other time for that.
Exit KENT

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Belovèd Regan,
Thy sister’s naught. O Regan, she hath tied
Sharp-toothed unkindness, like a vulture, here.
[indicates his heart]
I can scarce speak to thee. Thou'lt not believe
With how depraved a quality— O Regan!

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
I pray you, sir, take patience. I have hope
You less know how to value her desert
Than she to scant her duty.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Say, how is that?

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
I cannot think my sister in the least
Would fail her obligation. If, sir, perchance
She have restrained the riots of your followers,
'Tis on such ground and to such wholesome end
As clears her from all blame.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: My curses on her!

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
O sir, you are old.
Nature in you stands on the very verge
Of his confine. You should be ruled and led
By some discretion that discerns your state
Better than you yourself. Therefore I pray you
That to our sister you do make return.
Say you have wronged her, sir.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Ask her forgiveness?
Do you but mark how this becomes the house?—
[kneels] “Dear daughter, I confess that I am old.
Age is unnecessary. On my knees I beg
That you’ll vouchsafe me raiment, bed, and food.”

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Good sir, no more. These are unsightly tricks.
Return you to my sister.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
[rising] Never, Regan.
She hath abated me of half my train,
Looked black upon me, struck me with her tongue,
Most serpentlike, upon the very heart.
All the stored vengeances of heaven fall
On her ingrateful top! Strike her young bones,
You taking airs, with lameness!

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Fie, sir, fie!

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
You nimble lightnings, dart your blinding flames
Into her scornful eyes! Infect her beauty,
You fen-sucked fogs drawn by the powerful sun,
To fall and blister!

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
O the blessed gods!
So will you wish on me when the rash mood is on.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
No, Regan, thou shalt never have my curse.
Thy tender-hafted nature shall not give
Thee o'er to harshness. Her eyes are fierce, but thine
Do comfort and not burn. 'Tis not in thee
To grudge my pleasures, to cut off my train,
To bandy hasty words, to scant my sizes,
And in conclusion to oppose the bolt
Against my coming in. Thou better know’st
The offices of nature, bond of childhood,
Effects of courtesy, dues of gratitude.
Thy half o' th' kingdom hast thou not forgot,
Wherein I thee endowed.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Good sir, to the purpose.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Who put my man i' th' stocks?
Tucket within

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: What trumpet’s that?
Enter OSWALD the steward

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
I know ’t—my sister’s. This approves her letter
That she would soon be here. [to OSWALD]
Is your lady come?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
This is a slave whose easy borrowed pride
Dwells in the fickle grace of her he follows.—
Out, varlet, from my sight!

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: What means your grace?
Enter GONERIL

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Who stocked my servant? Regan, I have good hope
Thou didst not know on ’t.—Who comes here? O heavens,
If you do love old men, if your sweet sway
Allow obedience, if yourselves are old,
Make it your cause. Send down, and take my part!
[to GONERIL ] Art not ashamed to look upon this beard?—
O Regan, wilt thou take her by the hand?

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Why not by th' hand, sir? How have I offended?
All’s not offense that indiscretion finds
And dotage terms so.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
O sides, you are too tough.
Will you yet hold?—How came my man i' th' stocks?

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
I set him there, sir, but his own disorders
Deserved much less advancement.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: You! Did you?

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
I pray you, father, being weak, seem so.
If till the expiration of your month,
You will return and sojourn with my sister,
Dismissing half your train, come then to me.
I am now from home, and out of that provision
Which shall be needful for your entertainment.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Return to her, and fifty men dismissed?
No, rather I abjure all roofs, and choose
To be a comrade with the wolf and owl—
To wage against the enmity o' th' air—
Necessity’s sharp pinch! Return with her?
Why, the hot-blooded France that dowerless took
Our youngest born— I could as well be brought
To knee his throne, and, squirelike, pension beg
To keep base life afoot. Return with her?
Persuade me rather to be slave and sumpter
To this detested groom. [indicates OSWALD ]

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: At your choice, sir.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Now, I prithee, daughter, do not make me mad.
I will not trouble thee, my child. Farewell.
We’ll no more meet, no more see one another.
But yet thou art my flesh, my blood, my daughter—
Or rather a disease that’s in my flesh,
Which I must needs call mine. Thou art a boil,
A plague-sore or embossèd carbuncle
In my corrupted blood. But I’ll not chide thee.
Let shame come when it will. I do not call it.
I do not bid the thunder-bearer shoot,
Nor tell tales of thee to high-judging Jove.
Mend when thou canst. Be better at thy leisure.
I can be patient. I can stay with Regan,
I and my hundred knights.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Not altogether so, sir.
I looked not for you yet, nor am provided
