For your fit welcome. Give ear, sir, to my sister.
For those that mingle reason with your passion
Must be content to think you old, and so—
But she knows what she does.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Is this well spoken now?

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
I dare avouch it, sir. What, fifty followers?
Is it not well? What should you need of more—
Yea, or so many— sith that both charge and danger
Speak 'gainst so great a number? How, in one house,
Should many people under two commands
Hold amity? 'Tis hard; almost impossible.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Why might not you, my lord, receive attendance
From those that she calls servants, or from mine?

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Why not, my lord? If then they chanced to slack you,
We could control them. If you will come to me—
For now I spy a danger— I entreat you
To bring but five and twenty. To no more
Will I give place or notice.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: I gave you all—

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: And in good time you gave it.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Made you my guardians, my depositaries,
But kept a reservation to be followed
With such a number. What, must I come to you
With five and twenty, Regan? Said you so?

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: And speak ’t again, my lord. No more with me.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Those wicked creatures yet do look well favored
When others are more wicked. Not being the worst
Stands in some rank of praise.
[to GONERIL] I’ll go with thee.
Thy fifty yet doth double five and twenty,
And thou art twice her love.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Hear me, my lord.
What need you five and twenty, ten, or five
To follow in a house where twice so many
Have a command to tend you?

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: What need one?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
O, reason not the need! Our basest beggars
Are in the poorest thing superfluous.
Allow not nature more than nature needs,
Man’s life’s as cheap as beast’s. Thou art a lady.
If only to go warm were gorgeous,
Why, nature needs not what thou gorgeous wear’st,
Which scarcely keeps thee warm. But, for true need—
You heavens, give me that patience, patience I need.
You see me here, you gods, a poor old man,
As full of grief as age, wretched in both.
If it be you that stir these daughters' hearts
Against their father, fool me not so much
To bear it tamely. Touch me with noble anger.
And let not women’s weapons, water drops,
Stain my man’s cheeks! No, you unnatural hags,
I will have such revenges on you both
That all the world shall— I will do such things—
What they are yet I know not, but they shall be
The terrors of the earth. You think I’ll weep?
No, I’ll not weep.
Exeunt LEAR, GENTLEMAN, FOOL, and GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Let us withdraw. 'Twill be a storm.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
This house is little. The old man and his people
Cannot be well bestowed.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
'Tis his own blame. Hath put himself from rest,
And must needs taste his folly.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
For his particular I’ll receive him gladly,
But not one follower.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
So am I purposed.
Where is my lord of Gloucester?

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Followed the old man forth. He is returned.
Enter GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: The king is in high rage.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Whither is he going?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: He calls to horse, but will I know not whither.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: 'Tis best to give him way. He leads himself.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
[to GLOUCESTER] My lord, entreat him by no means to
stay.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Alack, the night comes on, and the high winds
Do sorely ruffle. For many miles about
There’s scarce a bush.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
O sir, to wilful men,
The injuries that they themselves procure
Must be their schoolmasters. Shut up your doors.
He is attended with a desperate train.
And what they may incense him to, being apt
To have his ear abused, wisdom bids fear.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
Shut up your doors, my lord. 'Tis a wild night.
My Regan counsels well. Come out o' th' storm.
Exeunt
Storm still. Enter KENT disguised and GENTLEMAN, severally

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Who’s there, besides foul weather?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: One minded like the weather, most unquietly.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: I know you. Where’s the king?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Contending with the fretful elements.
Bids the winds blow the earth into the sea
Or swell the curlèd water 'bove the main,
That things might change or cease. Tears his white
hair,
Which the impetuous blasts, with eyeless rage,
Catch in their fury and make nothing of.
Strives in his little world of man to outscorn
The to-and-fro–conflicting wind and rain.
This night—wherein the cub-drawn bear would couch,
The lion and the belly-pinchèd wolf
Keep their fur dry—unbonneted he runs,
And bids what will take all.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: But who is with him?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
None but the fool, who labors to outjest
His heart-struck injuries.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Sir, I do know you,
And dare upon the warrant of my note
Commend a dear thing to you. There is division,
Although as yet the face of it be covered
With mutual cunning, ’twixt Albany and Cornwall,
Who have—as who have not that their great stars
Throned and set high?—servants, who seem no less,
Which are to France the spies and speculations
Intelligent of our state. What hath been seen,
Either in snuffs and packings of the dukes,
Or the hard rein which both of them hath borne
Against the old kind king, or something deeper,
Whereof perchance these are but furnishings—
But true it is. From France there comes a power
Into this scattered kingdom, who already,
Wise in our negligence, have secret feet
In some of our best ports and are at point
To show their open banner. Now to you.
If on my credit you dare build so far
To make your speed to Dover, you shall find
Some that will thank you, making just report
Of how unnatural and bemadding sorrow
The king hath cause to plain.
I am a gentleman of blood and breeding,
And from some knowledge and assurance offer
This office to you.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: I will talk further with you.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
[giving GENTLEMAN a purse and a ring]
No, do not.
For confirmation that I am much more
Than my outwall, open this purse and take
What it contains. If you shall see Cordelia—
As fear not but you shall—show her this ring.
And she will tell you who that fellow is
That yet you do not know. Fie on this storm!
I will go seek the king.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: Give me your hand. Have you no more to say?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Few words, but to effect more than all yet:
That when we have found the king— in which your pain
That way; I’ll this—he that first lights on him
Holla the other.
Exeunt severally
Storm still. Enter LEAR and FOOL

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Blow, winds, and crack your cheeks! Rage, blow!
You cataracts and hurricanoes, spout
Till you have drenched our steeples, drowned the cocks!
You sulfurous and thought-executing fires,
Vaunt-couriers of oak-cleaving thunderbolts,
Singe my white head! And thou, all-shaking thunder,
Smite flat the thick rotundity o' th' world,
Crack nature’s molds, all germens spill at once
That make ingrateful man!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
O nuncle, court holy water in a dry house is better
than this rainwater out o' door. Good nuncle, in, and
ask thy daughters blessing. Here’s a night pities
neither wise man nor fool.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Rumble thy bellyful! Spit, fire! Spout, rain!
Nor rain, wind, thunder, fire are my daughters.
I tax not you, you elements, with unkindness.
I never gave you kingdom, called you children.
You owe me no subscription. Why then, let fall
Your horrible pleasure. Here I stand, your slave—
A poor, infirm, weak, and despised old man.
But yet I call you servile ministers,
That will with two pernicious daughters joined
Your high engendered battles 'gainst a head
So old and white as this. Oh, ho! 'Tis foul.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
He that has a house to put ’s head in has a good
headpiece.
The codpiece that will house
Before the head has any—
The head and he shall louse.
So beggars marry many.
The man that makes his toe
What he his heart should make
Shall of a corn cry woe,
And turn his sleep to wake.
For there was never yet fair woman but she made mouths
in a glass.
Enter KENT disguised

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
No, I will be the pattern of all patience.
I will say nothing.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Who’s there?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Marry, here’s grace and a codpiece—that’s a wise man
and a fool.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
[to LEAR] Alas, sir, are you here? Things that love
night
Love not such nights as these. The wrathful skies
Gallow the very wanderers of the dark
And make them keep their caves. Since I was man,
Such sheets of fire, such bursts of horrid thunder,
Such groans of roaring wind and rain I never
Remember to have heard. Man’s nature cannot carry
Th' affliction nor the fear.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Let the great gods
That keep this dreadful pudder o'er our heads
Find out their enemies now. Tremble, thou wretch
That hast within thee undivulgèd crimes
Unwhipped of justice. Hide thee, thou bloody hand,
Thou perjured, and thou simular man of virtue
That art incestuous. Caitiff, to pieces shake,
That under covert and convenient seeming
Hast practiced on man’s life. Close pent-up guilts,
Rive your concealing continents and cry
These dreadful summoners grace. I am a man
More sinned against than sinning.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Alack, bareheaded?
Gracious my lord, hard by here is a hovel.
Some friendship will it lend you 'gainst the tempest.
Repose you there, while I to this hard house—
More harder than the stones whereof ’tis raised,
Which even but now, demanding after you,
Denied me to come in—return, and force
Their scanted courtesy.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
My wits begin to turn.—
[to FOOL] Come on, my boy. How dost, my boy? Art cold?I
am cold myself.
[to KENT] Where is this straw, my fellow?
The art of our necessities is strange
That can make vile things precious. Come, your hovel.
Poor fool and knave, I have one part in my heart
That’s sorry yet for thee.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[sings]
He that has and a little tiny wit—
With heigh-ho, the wind and the rain—
Must make content with his fortunes fit,
For the rain it raineth every day.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: True, my good boy.—Come, bring us to this hovel.
Exeunt LEAR and KENT

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
This is a brave night to cool a courtesan.
I’ll speak a prophecy ere I go.
When priests are more in word than matter,
When brewers mar their malt with water,
When nobles are their tailors' tutors,
No heretics burned but wenches' suitors,
When every case in law is right,
No squire in debt nor no poor knight,
When slanders do not live in tongues,
Nor cutpurses come not to throngs,
When usurers tell their gold i' th' field,
And bawds and whores do churches build—
Then shall the realm of Albion
Come to great confusion.
Then comes the time, who lives to see ’t,
That going shall be used with feet.
This prophecy Merlin shall make, for I live before his
time.
Exit
Enter GLOUCESTER and EDMUND the bastard, with lights

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Alack, alack, Edmund, I like not this unnatural
dealing. When I desire their leave that I might pity
him, they took from me the use of mine own house,
charged me on pain of their perpetual displeasure
neither to speak of him, entreat for him, nor any way
sustain him.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Most savage and unnatural!

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Go to, say you nothing. There’s a division betwixt the
dukes. And a worse matter than that: I have received a
letter this night. 'Tis dangerous to be spoken. I have
locked the letter in my closet. These injuries the king
now bears will be revenged home. There’s part of a power
already footed. We must incline to the king. I will
look him and privily relieve him. Go you and maintain
talk with the duke, that my charity be not of him
perceived. If he ask for me, I am ill and gone to bed.
Though I die for it—as no less is threatened me—the king
my old master must be relieved. There is some strange
thing toward, Edmund. Pray you, be careful.
Exit GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
This courtesy, forbid thee, shall the duke
Instantly know, and of that letter too.
This seems a fair deserving, and must draw me
That which my father loses—no less than all.
The younger rises when the old doth fall.
Exit
Enter LEAR, KENT disguised, and FOOL

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Here is the place, my lord. Good my lord, enter.
The tyranny of the open night’s too rough
For nature to endure.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Let me alone.
Good my lord, enter here.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Wilt break my heart?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: I had rather break mine own. Good my lord, enter.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Thou think’st ’tis much that this contentious storm
Invades us to the skin. So ’tis to thee.
But where the greater malady is fixed
The lesser is scarce felt. Thou’dst shun a bear,
But if thy flight lay toward the raging sea
Thou’dst meet the bear i' th' mouth. When the mind’s
free,
The body’s delicate. The tempest in my mind
Doth from my senses take all feeling else
Save what beats there—filial ingratitude.
Is it not as this mouth should tear this hand
For lifting food to ’t? But I will punish home.
No, I will weep no more. In such a night
To shut me out! Pour on, I will endure.
In such a night as this! O Regan, Goneril,
Your old kind father, whose frank heart gave all—
Oh, that way madness lies. Let me shun that.
No more of that.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Good my lord, enter here.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Prithee, go in thyself. Seek thine own ease.
This tempest will not give me leave to ponder
On things would hurt me more. But I’ll go in.
[to FOOL] In, boy. Go first. You houseless poverty—
Nay, get thee in. I’ll pray, and then I’ll sleep.
Exit FOOL

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Poor naked wretches, whereso'er you are,
That bide the pelting of this pitiless storm,
How shall your houseless heads and unfed sides,
Your looped and windowed raggedness, defend you
From seasons such as these? Oh, I have ta'en
Too little care of this! Take physic, pomp.
Expose thyself to feel what wretches feel,
That thou mayst shake the superflux to them
And show the heavens more just.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: [within] Fathom and half, fathom and half! Poor Tom!
Enter FOOL

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Come not in here, nuncle. Here’s a spirit. Help me,
help me!

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Give me thy hand. Who’s there?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: A spirit, a spirit. He says his name’s Poor Tom.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
What art thou that dost grumble there i' th' straw?
Come forth.
Enter EDGAR disguised

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Away! The foul fiend follows me! Through the sharp
hawthorn blows the cold wind. Hum! Go to thy cold bed
and warm thee.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Didst thou give all to thy two daughters, and art thou
come to this?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Who gives any thing to Poor Tom, whom the foul fiend
hath led through fire and through flame, through ford
and whirlipool, o'er bog and quagmire; that hath laid
knives under his pillow and halters in his pew, set
ratsbane by his porridge, made him proud of heart to
ride on a bay trotting-horse over four-inched bridges to
course his own shadow for a traitor? Bless thy five
wits. Tom’s a-cold. Oh, do-de, do-de, do-de. Bless thee
from whirlwinds, star-blasting, and taking! Do Poor Tom
some charity, whom the foul fiend vexes. There could I
have him now—and there—and there again—and there.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
What, has his daughters brought him to this pass?—
Couldst thou save nothing? Wouldst thou give 'em all?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Nay, he reserved a blanket, else we had been all
shamed.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Now all the plagues that in the pendulous air
Hang fated o'er men’s faults light on thy daughters!

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: He hath no daughters, sir.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Death, traitor! Nothing could have subdued nature
To such a lowness but his unkind daughters.
Is it the fashion that discarded fathers
Should have thus little mercy on their flesh?
Judicious punishment! 'Twas this flesh begot
Those pelican daughters.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Pillicock sat on Pillicock hill. Alow, alow, loo, loo!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: This cold night will turn us all to fools and madmen.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Take heed o' th' foul fiend. Obey thy parents, keep thy
word’s justice, swear not, commit not with man’s sworn
spouse, set not thy sweet heart on proud array. Tom’s a-
cold.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: What hast thou been?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
A servingman, proud in heart and mind, that curled my
hair, wore gloves in my cap, served the lust of my
mistress' heart and did the act of darkness with her,
swore as many oaths as I spake words and broke them in
the sweet face of heaven—one that slept in the
contriving of lust and waked to do it. Wine loved I
deeply, dice dearly, and in woman outparamoured the
Turk. False of heart, light of ear, bloody of hand—hog
in sloth, fox in stealth, wolf in greediness, dog in
madness, lion in prey. Let not the creaking of shoes nor
the rustling of silks betray thy poor heart to woman.
Keep thy foot out of brothels, thy hand out of plackets,
thy pen from lenders' books, and defy the foul fiend.
Still through the hawthorn blows the cold wind, says,
“Suum, mun, nonny.” Dauphin my boy, my boy, cessez. Let
him trot by.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Why, thou wert better in thy grave than to answer with
thy uncovered body this extremity of the skies.—Is man
no more than this? Consider him well.— Thou owest the
worm no silk, the beast no hide, the sheep no wool, the
cat no perfume. Ha! Here’s three on ’s are
sophisticated. Thou art the thing itself. Unaccommodated
man is no more but such a poor, bare, forked animal as
thou art.—Off, off, you lendings! Come. Unbutton here.
[tears at his clothes]

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Prithee, nuncle, be contented. 'Tis a naughty night to
swim in. Now a little fire in a wild field were like an
old lecher’s heart—a small spark, all the rest on ’s
body cold. Look, here comes a walking fire.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
This is the foul fiend Flibbertigibbet. He begins at
curfew and walks till the first cock. He gives the web
and the pin, squints the eye and makes the harelip,
mildews the white wheat and hurts the poor creature of
earth.
St. Withold footed thrice the 'old.
He met the nightmare and her ninefold,
Bid her alight,
And her troth plight.
And aroint thee, witch, aroint thee!

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: How fares your grace?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: [indicating GLOUCESTER] What’s he?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Who’s there? What is ’t you seek?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: What are you there? Your names?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Poor Tom, that eats the swimming frog, the toad, the
tadpole, the wall newt, and the water; that in the fury
of his heart, when the foul fiend rages, eats cow dung
for salads, swallows the old rat and the ditch-dog,
drinks the green mantle of the standing pool; who is
whipped from tithing to tithing and stocked, punished
and imprisoned; who hath had three suits to his back,
six shirts to his body,
Horse to ride and weapon to wear.
But mice and rats and such small deer
Have been Tom’s food for seven long year.
Beware my follower. Peace, Smulkin. Peace, thou fiend!

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: [to LEAR] What, hath your grace no better company?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
The Prince of Darkness is a gentleman. Modo he’s
called, and Mahu.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
[To LEAR] Our flesh and blood, my lord, is grown so
vile
That it doth hate what gets it.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Poor Tom’s a-cold.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Go in with me. My duty cannot suffer
To obey in all your daughters' hard commands.
Though their injunction be to bar my doors
And let this tyrannous night take hold upon you,
Yet have I ventured to come seek you out
And bring you where both fire and food is ready.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
First let me talk with this philosopher.—
[to EDGAR] What is the cause of thunder?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
[to LEAR] Good my lord, take his offer. Go into the
house.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
I’ll talk a word with this same learnèd Theban.—
What is your study?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: How to prevent the fiend and to kill vermin.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Let me ask you one word in private.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
[aside to GLOUCESTER] Importune him once more to go, my
lord.
His wits begin t' unsettle.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Canst thou blame him?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
O, cry your mercy, sir.—
[to EDGAR] Noble philosopher, your company.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Tom’s a-cold.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: In, fellow. There, into th' hovel. Keep thee warm.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Come let’s in all.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: This way, my lord.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
[indicating EDGAR]
With him!
I will keep still with my philosopher.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
[to GLOUCESTER] Good my lord, soothe him. Let him take
the fellow.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Take him you on.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: [to EDGAR ] Sirrah, come on. Go along with us.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Come, good Athenian.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: No words, no words. Hush.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Child Roland to the dark tower came,
His word was still “Fie, foh, and fum,
I smell the blood of a British man.”
Exeunt
Enter CORNWALL and EDMUND

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: I will have my revenge ere I depart his house.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
How, my lord, I may be censured, that nature thus gives
way to loyalty, something fears me to think of.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
I now perceive it was not altogether your brother’s
evil disposition made him seek his death, but a
provoking merit set awork by a reprovable badness in
himself.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
How malicious is my fortune, that I must repent to be
just! [giving CORNWALL a letter] This is the letter
which he spoke of, which approves him an intelligent
party to the advantages of France. O heavens, that this
treason were not, or not I the detector!

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Go with me to the duchess.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
If the matter of this paper be certain, you have mighty
business in hand.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
True or false, it hath made thee Earl of Gloucester.
Seek out where thy father is, that he may be ready for
our apprehension.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
[aside] If I find him comforting the king, it will
stuff his suspicion more fully. [to CORNWALL] I will
persevere in my course of loyalty, though the conflict
be sore between that and my blood.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
I will lay trust upon thee, and thou shalt find a
dearer father in my love.
Exeunt
Enter GLOUCESTER, LEAR, KENT disguised, FOOL, and EDGAR disguised

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Here is better than the open air. Take it thankfully. I
will piece out the comfort with what addition I can. I
will not be long from you.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
All the power of his wits have given way to his
impatience.
The gods reward your kindness!
Exit GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Frateretto calls me and tells me Nero is an angler in
the lake of darkness. Pray, innocent, and beware the
foul fiend.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Prithee, nuncle, tell me whether a madman be a
gentleman or a yeoman?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: A king, a king!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
No, he’s a yeoman that has a gentleman to his son, for
he’s a mad yeoman that sees his son a gentleman before
him.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
To have a thousand with red burning spits
Come hissing in upon 'em!

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: The foul fiend bites my back.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
He’s mad that trusts in the tameness of a wolf, a
horse’s health, a boy’s love, or a whore’s oath.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
It shall be done. I will arraign them straight.
[to EDGAR] Come, sit thou here, most learnèd justicer.
[to FOOL] Thou, sapient sir, sit here.—Now, you
she-foxes—

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Look, where he stands and glares!—Want’st thou eyes at
trial, madam?
[sings]
Come o'er the bourn, Bessy, to me—

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[sings]
Her boat hath a leak,
And she must not speak
Why she dares not come over to thee.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
The foul fiend haunts Poor Tom in the voice of a
nightingale. Hoppedance cries in Tom’s belly for two
white herring. Croak not, black angel. I have no food
for thee.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
[to LEAR] How do you, sir? Stand you not so amazed.
Will you lie down and rest upon the cushions?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
I’ll see their trial first. Bring in the evidence.
[to EDGAR] Thou robèd man of justice, take thy place.
[to FOOL] And thou, his yoke-fellow of equity,
Bench by his side. [to KENT ] You are o' th'
commission.
Sit you too.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Let us deal justly.
[ sings]
Sleepest or wakest thou, jolly shepherd?
Thy sheep be in the corn.
And for one blast of thy minikin mouth,
Thy sheep shall take no harm.
Purr! The cat is gray.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Arraign her first. 'Tis Goneril. I here take my oath
before this honorable assembly, she kicked the poor king
her father.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Come hither, mistress. Is your name Goneril?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: She cannot deny it.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Cry you mercy, I took you for a joint-stool.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
And here’s another, whose warped looks proclaim
What store her heart is made on. Stop her there!
Arms, arms, sword, fire, corruption in the place!
False justicer, why hast thou let her ’scape?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Bless thy five wits.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
[to LEAR] O pity! Sir, where is the patience now,
That thou so oft have boasted to retain?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
[aside] My tears begin to take his part so much,
They’ll mar my counterfeiting.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
The little dogs and all,
Tray, Blanch, and Sweetheart—see, they bark at me.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Tom will throw his head at them.—Avaunt, you curs!
Be thy mouth or black or white,
Tooth that poisons if it bite,
Mastiff, greyhound, mongrel grim,
Hound or spaniel, brach or him,
Bobtail tyke or trundle-tail—
Tom will make them weep and wail.
For with throwing thus my head,
Dogs leap the hatch, and all are fled.
Do-de, de-de. Cessez! Come, march to wakes and fairs
and market towns. Poor Tom, thy horn is dry.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Then let them anatomize Regan. See what breeds about
her heart. Is there any cause in nature that makes these
hard hearts? [to EDGAR] You, sir, I entertain you for
one of my hundred. Only I do not like the fashion of
your garments. You will say they are Persian attire, but
let them be changed.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Now, good my lord, lie here and rest awhile.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Make no noise, make no noise. Draw the curtains—so, so,
so. We’ll go to supper i' th' morning. So, so, so.
[sleeps]

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: And I’ll go to bed at noon.
Enter GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
[to KENT] Come hither, friend. Where is the king my
master?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Here, sir, but trouble him not. His wits are gone.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Good friend, I prithee, take him in thy arms.
I have o'erheard a plot of death upon him.
There is a litter ready. Lay him in ’t
And drive towards Dover, friend, where thou shalt meet
Both welcome and protection. Take up thy master.
If thou shouldst dally half an hour, his life,
With thine and all that offer to defend him,
Stand in assurèd loss. Take up, take up,
And follow me, that will to some provision
Give thee quick conduct.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Oppressèd nature sleeps.—
This rest might yet have balmed thy broken sinews,
Which, if convenience will not allow,
Stand in hard cure.
[to FOOL]
Come, help to bear thy master.
Thou must not stay behind.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Come, come, away.
Exeunt all but EDGAR

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
When we our betters see bearing our woes,
We scarcely think our miseries our foes.
Who alone suffers, suffers most i' th' mind,
Leaving free things and happy shows behind.
But then the mind much sufferance doth o'erskip
When grief hath mates and bearing fellowship.
How light and portable my pain seems now
When that which makes me bend makes the king bow.
He childed as I fathered. Tom, away!
Mark the high noises and thyself bewray
When false opinion, whose wrong thought defiles thee,
In thy just proof repeals and reconciles thee.
What will hap more tonight, safe ’scape the king!
Lurk, lurk.
Exit
Enter CORNWALL, and REGAN, and GONERIL, and EDMUND the bastard, and servants

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
[to GONERIL] Post speedily to my lord your husband.
Show him this letter. The army of France is landed.
—Seek out the traitor Gloucester.
Exeunt some servants

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Hang him instantly.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: Pluck out his eyes.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
Leave him to my displeasure.— Edmund, keep you our
sister company. The revenges we are bound to take upon
your traitorous father are not fit for your beholding.
Advise the duke where you are going, to a most festinate
preparation. We are bound to the like. Our posts shall
be swift and intelligent betwixt us.— Farewell, dear
sister. [to EDMUND] Farewell, my lord of Gloucester.
Enter OSWALD the steward

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: How now? Where’s the king?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text:
My lord of Gloucester hath conveyed him hence.
Some five or six and thirty of his knights,
Hot questrists after him, met him at gate,
Who with some other of the lord’s dependants
Are gone with him towards Dover, where they boast
To have well-armèd friends.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Get horses for your mistress.
Exit OSWALD

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: Farewell, sweet lord, and sister.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Edmund, farewell.
Exeunt GONERIL and EDMUND the bastard

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
Go seek the traitor Gloucester.
Pinion him like a thief, bring him before us.
Exeunt some servants

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
Though well we may not pass upon his life
Without the form of justice, yet our power
Shall do a courtesy to our wrath, which men
May blame, but not control.—Who’s there? The traitor?
Enter GLOUCESTER, brought in by two or three servants

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Ingrateful fox, ’tis he.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Bind fast his corky arms.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
What mean your graces? Good my friends, consider
You are my guests. Do me no foul play, friends.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Bind him, I say.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Hard, hard. O filthy traitor!

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Unmerciful lady as you are, I’m none.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: To this chair bind him. Villain, thou shalt find—

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
By the kind gods, ’tis most ignobly done
To pluck me by the beard.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: So white, and such a traitor?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Naughty lady,
These hairs which thou dost ravish from my chin
Will quicken and accuse thee. I am your host.
With robbers' hands my hospitable favors
You should not ruffle thus. What will you do?

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Come, sir, what letters had you late from France?

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Be simple-answered, for we know the truth.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
And what confederacy have you with the traitors
Late footed in the kingdom?

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
To whose hands
You have sent the lunatic king. Speak.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
I have a letter guessingly set down,
Which came from one that’s of a neutral heart,
And not from one opposed.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Cunning.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: And false.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Where hast thou sent the king?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: To Dover.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Wherefore to Dover? Wast thou not charged at peril—

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: Wherefore to Dover?—Let him first answer that.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: I am tied to th' stake, and I must stand the course.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Wherefore to Dover, sir?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Because I would not see thy cruèl nails
Pluck out his poor old eyes, nor thy fierce sister
In his anointed flesh stick boarish fangs.
The sea, with such a storm as his bare head
In hell-black night endured, would have buoyed up,
And quenched the stellèd fires.
Yet poor old heart, he holp the heavens to rain.
If wolves had at thy gate howled that stern time,
Thou shouldst have said, “Good porter, turn the key,”
All cruèls else subscribed. But I shall see
The wingèd vengeance overtake such children.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
“See” ’t shalt thou never.—Fellows, hold the chair.—
Upon these eyes of thine I’ll set my foot.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
He that will think to live till he be old,
Give me some help!

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: One side will mock another—th' other too.

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: If you see vengeance—

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text:
Hold your hand, my lord!
I have served you ever since I was a child.
But better service have I never done you
Than now to bid you hold.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: How now, you dog?

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text:
If you did wear a beard upon your chin,
I’d shake it on this quarrel.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: What do you mean?

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text: My villain!

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text: Nay then, come on, and take the chance of anger.
FIRST SERVANT and CORNWALL draw and fight. CORNWALL is wounded

[to another servant]
Give me thy sword.—A peasant stand up thus?
[takes a sword, runs at FIRST SERVANT behind, and kills
him]

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text:
Oh, I am slain!—My lord, you have one eye left
To see some mischief on him. Oh!
[dies]

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
Lest it see more, prevent it. Out, vile jelly!
[plucks out GLOUCESTER’s other eye]
Where is thy luster now?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
All dark and comfortless. Where’s my son Edmund?
Edmund, enkindle all the sparks of nature
To quit this horrid act.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Out, treacherous villain!
Thou call’st on him that hates thee. It was he
That made the overture of thy treasons to us,
Who is too good to pity thee.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
O my follies! Then Edgar was abused.
Kind gods, forgive me that, and prosper him!

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Go thrust him out at gates, and let him smell
His way to Dover.
Exeunt some servants with GLOUCESTER

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: [to CORNWALL] How is ’t, my lord? How look you?

units.speaker: CORNWALL
units.text:
I have received a hurt. Follow me, lady.—
Turn out that eyeless villain. Throw this slave
Upon the dunghill.— Regan, I bleed apace.
Untimely comes this hurt. Give me your arm.
Exit CORNWALL with REGAN

units.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
units.text:
I’ll never care what wickedness I do,
If this man come to good.

units.speaker: THIRD SERVANT
units.text:
If she live long,
And in the end meet the old course of death,
Women will all turn monsters.

units.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
units.text:
Let’s follow the old earl, and get the Bedlam
To lead him where he would. His roguish madness
Allows itself to any thing.

units.speaker: THIRD SERVANT
units.text:
Go thou. I’ll fetch some flax and whites of eggs
To apply to his bleeding face. Now heaven help him!
Exeunt severally
Enter EDGAR diguised

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Yet better thus, and known to be contemned,
Than still contemned and flattered. To be worst,
The lowest and most dejected thing of fortune
Stands still in esperance, lives not in fear.
The lamentable change is from the best;
The worst returns to laughter. Welcome, then,
Thou unsubstantial air that I embrace!
The wretch that thou hast blown unto the worst
Owes nothing to thy blasts.
Enter GLOUCESTER led by an OLD MAN

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
But who comes here?
My father, poorly led? World, world, O world!
But that thy strange mutations make us hate thee,
Life would not yield to age.

units.speaker: OLD MAN
units.text:
[to GLOUCESTER] O my good lord,
I have been your tenant and your father’s tenant these
fourscore years.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Away, get thee away. Good friend, be gone.
Thy comforts can do me no good at all.
Thee they may hurt.

units.speaker: OLD MAN
units.text: Alack, sir, you cannot see your way.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
I have no way, and therefore want no eyes.
I stumbled when I saw. Full oft ’tis seen,
Our means secure us and our mere defects
Prove our commodities. O dear son Edgar,
The food of thy abusèd father’s wrath,
Might I but live to see thee in my touch,
I’d say I had eyes again!

units.speaker: OLD MAN
units.text: How now? Who’s there?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
[aside] O gods! Who is ’t can say “I am at the worst?”
I am worse than e'er I was.

units.speaker: OLD MAN
units.text:
[to GLOUCESTER]
'Tis poor mad Tom.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
[aside] And worse I may be yet. The worst is not
So long as we can say “This is the worst.”

units.speaker: OLD MAN
units.text: [to EDGAR] Fellow, where goest?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Is it a beggarman?

units.speaker: OLD MAN
units.text: Madman and beggar too.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
He has some reason, else he could not beg.
I' th' last night’s storm I such a fellow saw,
Which made me think a man a worm. My son
Came then into my mind, and yet my mind
Was then scarce friends with him. I have heard more
since.
As flies to wanton boys are we to th' gods.
They kill us for their sport.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
[aside]
How should this be?
Bad is the trade that must play fool to sorrow,
Angering itself and others.—Bless thee, master!

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Is that the naked fellow?

units.speaker: OLD MAN
units.text: Ay, my lord.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Then prithee, get thee gone. If for my sake
Thou wilt o'ertake us hence a mile or twain
I' th' way toward Dover, do it for ancient love.
And bring some covering for this naked soul,
Which I’ll entreat to lead me.

units.speaker: OLD MAN
units.text: Alack, sir, he is mad.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
'Tis the time’s plague when madmen lead the blind.
Do as I bid thee. Or rather, do thy pleasure.
Above the rest, be gone.

units.speaker: OLD MAN
units.text:
I’ll bring him the best 'parel that I have,
Come on ’t what will.
Exit OLD MAN

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Sirrah, naked fellow—

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Poor Tom’s a-cold.
[aside] I cannot daub it further.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Come hither, fellow.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
[aside] And yet I must.—Bless thy sweet eyes, they
bleed.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Know’st thou the way to Dover?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Both stile and gate, horseway and footpath. Poor Tom
hath been scared out of his good wits. Bless thee,
goodman’s son, from the foul fiend! Five fiends have
been in poor Tom at once: of lust, as Obidicut;
Hobbididence, prince of dumbness; Mahu, of stealing;
Modo, of murder; Flibbertigibbet, of mopping and mowing,
who since possesses chambermaids and waiting-women. So
bless thee, master.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
[Giving EDGAR a purse]
Here, take this purse, thou whom the heavens' plagues
Have humbled to all strokes. That I am wretched
Makes thee the happier. Heavens, deal so still.
Let the superfluous and lust-dieted man,
That slaves your ordinance, that will not see
Because he doth not feel, feel your power quickly.
So distribution should undo excess,
And each man have enough. Dost thou know Dover?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Ay, master.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
There is a cliff, whose high and bending head
Looks fearfully in the confinèd deep.
Bring me but to the very brim of it,
And I’ll repair the misery thou dost bear
With something rich about me. From that place
I shall no leading need.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Give me thy arm.
Poor Tom shall lead thee.
Exeunt
Enter GONERIL and EDMUND the bastard

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Welcome, my lord. I marvel our mild husband
Not met us on the way.
Enter OSWALD

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: Now, where’s your master?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text:
Madam, within—but never man so changed.
I told him of the army that was landed.
He smiled at it. I told him you were coming.
His answer was “The worse.” Of Gloucester’s treachery
And of the loyal service of his son,
When I informed him, then he called me “sot,”
And told me I had turned the wrong side out.
What most he should dislike seems pleasant to him;
What like, offensive.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
[to EDMUND ]
Then shall you go no further.
It is the cowish terror of his spirit
That dares not undertake. He’ll not feel wrongs
Which tie him to an answer. Our wishes on the way
May prove effects. Back, Edmund, to my brother.
Hasten his musters and conduct his powers.
I must change names at home, and give the distaff
Into my husband’s hands. This trusty servant
Shall pass between us. Ere long you are like to hear—
If you dare venture in your own behalf—
A mistress’s command. Wear this. Spare speech.
Decline your head. This kiss, if it durst speak,
Would stretch thy spirits up into the air.
[kisses EDMUND] Conceive, and fare thee well.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Yours in the ranks of death.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: My most dear Gloucester!
Exit EDMUND

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Oh, the difference of man and man!
To thee a woman’s services are due.
My fool usurps my body.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Madam, here comes my lord.
Exit OSWALD
Enter ALBANY

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: I have been worth the whistle.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
O Goneril,
You are not worth the dust which the rude wind
Blows in your face. I fear your disposition.
That nature, which contemns its origin
Cannot be bordered certain in itself.
She that herself will sliver and disbranch
From her material sap perforce must wither
And come to deadly use.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: No more. The text is foolish.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
Wisdom and goodness to the vile seem vile.
Filths savor but themselves. What have you done?
Tigers, not daughters, what have you performed?
A father, and a gracious agèd man,
Whose reverence even the head-lugged bear would lick,
Most barbarous, most degenerate, have you madded.
Could my good brother suffer you to do it—
A man, a prince by him so benefited?
If that the heavens do not their visible spirits
Send quickly down to tame these vile offenses,
It will come:
Humanity must perforce prey on itself
Like monsters of the deep.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Milk-livered man
That bear’st a cheek for blows, a head for wrongs—
Who hast not in thy brows an eye discerning
Thine honor from thy suffering; that not know’st
Fools do those villains pity who are punished
Ere they have done their mischief. Where’s thy drum?
France spreads his banners in our noiseless land,
With plumèd helm thy state begins to threat,
Whiles thou, a moral fool, sits still and cries,
“Alack, why does he so?”

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
See thyself, devil!
Proper deformity shows not in the fiend
So horrid as in woman.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: O vain fool!

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
Thou changèd and self-covered thing, for shame!
Bemonster not thy feature. Were ’t my fitness
To let these hands obey my blood,
They are apt enough to dislocate and tear
Thy flesh and bones. Howe'er thou art a fiend,
A woman’s shape doth shield thee.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: Marry, your manhood, mew!
Enter FIRST MESSENGER

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: What news?

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text:
O my good lord, the Duke of Cornwall’s dead,
Slain by his servant, going to put out
The other eye of Gloucester.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: Gloucester’s eyes?

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text:
A servant that he bred, thrilled with remorse,
Opposed against the act, bending his sword
To his great master; who thereat enraged
Flew on him and amongst them felled him dead—
But not without that harmful stroke, which since
Hath plucked him after.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
This shows you are above,
You justicers, that these our nether crimes
So speedily can venge! But oh, poor Gloucester—
Lost he his other eye?

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text:
Both, both, my lord.—
This letter, madam, craves a speedy answer.
'Tis from your sister.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
[aside]
One way I like this well.
But being widow, and my Gloucester with her,
May all the building in my fancy pluck
Upon my hateful life. Another way
The news is not so tart.— I’ll read and answer.
Exit GONERIL

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: Where was his son when they did take his eyes?

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text: Come with my lady hither.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: He is not here.

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text: No, my good lord. I met him back again.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: Knows he the wickedness?

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text:
Ay, my good lord. 'Twas he informed against him,
And quit the house on purpose that their punishment
Might have the freer course.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
Gloucester, I live
To thank thee for the love thou showed’st the king,
And to revenge thine eyes.— Come hither, friend.
Tell me what more thou know’st.
Exeunt
Enter KENT disguised and GENTLEMAN

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Why the King of France is so suddenly gone back know
you the reason?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Something he left imperfect in the state which, since
his coming forth, is thought of; which imports to the
kingdom so much fear and danger that his personal return
was most required and necessary.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Who hath he left behind him general?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: The Marshal of France, Monsieur la Far.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Did your letters pierce the queen to any demonstration
of grief?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Ay, sir. She took them, read them in my presence,
And now and then an ample tear trilled down
Her delicate cheek. It seemed she was a queen
Over her passion, who, most rebel-like,
Sought to be king o'er her.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: O, then it moved her?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Not to a rage. Patience and sorrow strove
Who should express her goodliest. You have seen
Sunshine and rain at once—her smiles and tears
Were like a better way. Those happy smilets
That played on her ripe lip seemed not to know
What guests were in her eyes, which parted thence
As pearls from diamonds dropped. In brief,
Sorrow would be a rarity most beloved
If all could so become it.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Made she no verbal question?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Faith, once or twice she heaved the name of “father”
Pantingly forth as if it pressed her heart,
Cried, “Sisters, sisters! Shame of ladies, sisters!
Kent, father, sisters! What, i' th' storm, i' th'
night?
Let pity not be believed.” There she shook
The holy water from her heavenly eyes,
And clamor moistened. Then away she started
To deal with grief alone.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
It is the stars,
The stars above us, govern our conditions.
Else one self mate and mate could not beget
Such different issues. You spoke not with her since?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: No.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Was this before the king returned?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: No, since.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Well, sir, the poor distressèd Lear’s i' th' town,
Who sometime in his better tune remembers
What we are come about, and by no means
Will yield to see his daughter.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: Why, good sir?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
A sovereign shame so elbows him. His own unkindness
That stripped her from his benediction turned her
To foreign casualties, gave her dear rights
To his dog-hearted daughters. These things sting
His mind so venomously that burning shame
Detains him from Cordelia.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: Alack, poor gentleman!

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Of Albany’s and Cornwall’s powers you heard not?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: 'Tis so. They are afoot.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Well, sir, I’ll bring you to our master Lear
And leave you to attend him. Some dear cause
Will in concealment wrap me up awhile.
When I am known aright you shall not grieve
Lending me this acquaintance. I pray you, go
Along with me.
Exeunt
Enter, with drum and colors, CORDELIA, DOCTOR, and soldiers

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
Alack, ’tis he. Why, he was met even now
As mad as the vexed sea, singing aloud,
Crowned with rank fumiter and furrow-weeds,
With burdocks, hemlock, nettles, cuckoo-flowers,
Darnel, and all the idle weeds that grow
In our sustaining corn.— A century send forth.
Search every acre in the high-grown field,
And bring him to our eye.
Exit some soldiers

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
What can man’s wisdom
In the restoring his bereavèd sense?
He that helps him take all my outward worth.

units.speaker: DOCTOR
units.text:
There is means, madam.
Our foster nurse of nature is repose,
The which he lacks— that to provoke in him
Are many simples operative, whose power
Will close the eye of anguish.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
All blessed secrets,
All you unpublished virtues of the earth,
Spring with my tears. Be aidant and remediate
In the good man’s distress. Seek, seek for him,
Lest his ungoverned rage dissolve the life
That wants the means to lead it.
Enter SECOND MESSENGER

units.speaker: SECOND MESSENGER
units.text:
News, madam.
The British powers are marching hitherward.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
'Tis known before. Our preparation stands
In expectation of them. O dear father,
It is thy business that I go about.
Therefore great France
My mourning and importuned tears hath pitied.
No blown ambition doth our arms incite,
But love—dear love!—and our aged father’s right.
Soon may I hear and see him.
Exeunt
Enter REGAN and the steward OSWALD

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: But are my brother’s powers set forth?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Ay, madam.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Himself in person there?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text:
Madam, with much ado.
Your sister is the better soldier.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Lord Edmund spake not with your lord at home?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: No, madam.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: What might import my sister’s letter to him?

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: I know not, lady.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Faith, he is posted hence on serious matter.
It was great ignorance, Gloucester’s eyes being out,
To let him live. Where he arrives he moves
All hearts against us. Edmund I think is gone
In pity of his misery to dispatch
His nighted life; moreover to descry
The strength o' th' enemy.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: I must needs after him, madam, with my letter.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Our troops set forth tomorrow. Stay with us.
The ways are dangerous.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text:
I may not, madam.
My lady charged my duty in this business.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Why should she write to Edmund? Might not you
Transport her purposes by word? Belike
Some things—I know not what. I’ll love thee much.
Let me unseal the letter.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Madam, I had rather—

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
I know your lady does not love her husband.
I am sure of that. And at her late being here
She gave strange oeillades and most speaking looks
To noble Edmund. I know you are of her bosom.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: I, madam?

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
I speak in understanding. Y' are. I know ’t.
Therefore I do advise you, take this note.
My lord is dead. Edmund and I have talked,
And more convenient is he for my hand
Than for your lady’s. You may gather more.
If you do find him, pray you give him this.
And when your mistress hears thus much from you,
I pray desire her call her wisdom to her.
So fare you well.
If you do chance to hear of that blind traitor,
Preferment falls on him that cuts him off.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text:
Would I could meet him, madam, I should show
What party I do follow.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Fare thee well.
Exeunt severally
Enter GLOUCESTER, and EDGAR disguised in peasant clothing

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: When shall we come to th' top of that same hill?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: You do climb up it now. Look how we labor.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Methinks the ground is even.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Horrible steep.
Hark, do you hear the sea?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: No, truly.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Why then, your other senses grow imperfect
By your eyes' anguish.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
So may it be indeed.
Methinks thy voice is altered, and thou speak’st
In better phrase and matter than thou didst.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
You’re much deceived. In nothing am I changed
But in my garments.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Methinks you’re better spoken.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Come on, sir. Here’s the place. Stand still. How
fearful
And dizzy ’tis to cast one’s eyes so low!
The crows and choughs that wing the midway air
Show scarce so gross as beetles. Halfway down
Hangs one that gathers samphire—dreadful trade!
Methinks he seems no bigger than his head.
The fishermen that walk upon the beach
Appear like mice. And yon tall anchoring bark,
Diminished to her cock, her cock a buoy
Almost too small for sight. The murmuring surge
That on th' unnumbered idle pebbles chafes
Cannot be heard so high. I’ll look no more
Lest my brain turn and the deficient sight
Topple down headlong.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Set me where you stand.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Give me your hand. You are now within a foot
Of th' extreme verge. For all beneath the moon
Would I not leap upright.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Let go my hand.
[gives EDGAR another purse]
Here, friend, ’s another purse, in it a jewel
Well worth a poor man’s taking. Fairies and gods
Prosper it with thee! Go thou farther off.
Bid me farewell, and let me hear thee going.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Now fare you well, good sir.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: With all my heart.
EDGAR moves aside

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
[aside] Why I do trifle thus with his despair
Is done to cure it.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
O you mighty gods, [kneels]
This world I do renounce, and in your sights
Shake patiently my great affliction off.
If I could bear it longer and not fall
To quarrel with your great opposeless wills,
My snuff and loathèd part of nature should
Burn itself out. If Edgar live, O, bless him!—
Now, fellow, fare thee well. (falls)

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Gone, sir. Farewell.
[aside] And yet I know not how conceit may rob
The treasury of life when life itself
Yields to the theft. Had he been where he thought,
By this had thought been past. Alive or dead?—
Ho you, sir, friend! Hear you, sir? Speak.—
Thus might he pass indeed. Yet he revives.—
What are you, sir?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Away, and let me die.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Hadst thou been aught but gossamer, feathers, air,
So many fathom down precipitating,
Thou’dst shivered like an egg. But thou dost breathe,
Hast heavy substance, bleed’st not, speak’st, art
sound.
Ten masts at each make not the altitude
Which thou hast perpendicularly fell.
Thy life’s a miracle. Speak yet again.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: But have I fall'n, or no?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
From the dread summit of this chalky bourn.
Look up a-height. The shrill-gorged lark so far
Cannot be seen or heard. Do but look up.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Alack, I have no eyes.
Is wretchedness deprived that benefit,
To end itself by death? 'Twas yet some comfort
When misery could beguile the tyrant’s rage
And frustrate his proud will.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Give me your arm.
Up—so. How is ’t? Feel you your legs? You stand.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Too well, too well.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
This is above all strangeness.
Upon the crown o' th' cliff, what thing was that
Which parted from you?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: A poor unfortunate beggar.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
As I stood here below, methought his eyes
Were two full moons. He had a thousand noses,
Horns whelked and waved like the enragèd sea.
It was some fiend. Therefore, thou happy father,
Think that the clearest gods, who make them honors
Of men’s impossibilities, have preserved thee.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
I do remember now. Henceforth I’ll bear
Affliction till it do cry out itself,
“Enough, enough,” and die. That thing you speak of,
I took it for a man. Often ’twould say,
“The fiend, the fiend!” He led me to that place.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Bear free and patient thoughts.
Enter LEAR, mad

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
But who comes here?
The safer sense will ne'er accommodate
His master thus.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
No, they cannot touch me for coining. I am the king
himself.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: [aside] O thou side-piercing sight!

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Nature’s above art in that respect. There’s your press-
money. That fellow handles his bow like a crowkeeper.
Draw me a clothier’s yard. Look, look, a mouse!
Peace, peace, this piece of toasted cheese will do ’t.
There’s my gauntlet. I’ll prove it on a giant. Bring up
the brown bills. O, well flown, bird. I' th' clout, i'
th' clout. Hewgh! Give the word.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Sweet marjoram.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Pass.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: I know that voice.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Ha! Goneril with a white beard? Ha, Regan? They
flattered me like a dog and told me I had white hairs in
my beard ere the black ones were there. To say “Ay” and
“No” to everything that I said “Ay” and “No” to was no
good divinity. When the rain came to wet me once, and
the wind to make me chatter, when the thunder would not
peace at my bidding—there I found 'em, there I smelt 'em
out. Go to, they are not men o' their words. They told
me I was everything. 'Tis a lie, I am not ague-proof.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
The trick of that voice I do well remember.
Is ’t not the king?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Ay, every inch a king!
When I do stare, see how the subject quakes.
I pardon that man’s life. What was thy cause?
Adultery?
Thou shalt not die. Die for adultery? No.
The wren goes to ’t, and the small gilded fly
Does lecher in my sight.
Let copulation thrive, for Gloucester’s bastard son
Was kinder to his father than my daughters
Got ’tween the lawful sheets. To ’t, luxury, pell-mell!
For I lack soldiers. Behold yond simpering dame,
Whose face between her forks presages snow;
That minces virtue and does shake the head
To hear of pleasure’s name.
The fitchew, nor the soiled horse, goes to ’t
With a more riotous appetite.
Down from the waist they are centaurs,
Though women all above.
But to the girdle do the gods inherit.
Beneath is all the fiends'; there’s hell, there’s
darkness,
There’s the sulphurous pit— burning, scalding,
Stench, consumption! Fie, fie, fie, pah, pah!
Give me an ounce of civet, good apothecary,
To sweeten my imagination.
There’s money for thee.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: O, let me kiss that hand!

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Let me wipe it first. It smells of mortality.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
O ruined piece of nature! This great world
Shall so wear out to naught. Dost thou know me?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
I remember thine eyes well enough. Dost thou squiny at
me? No, do thy worst, blind Cupid. I’ll not love. Read
thou this challenge. Mark but the penning of it.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Were all thy letters suns, I could not see one.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
[aside] I would not take this from report. It is,
And my heart breaks at it.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Read.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: What, with the case of eyes?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Oh ho, are you there with me? No eyes in your head, nor
no money in your purse? Your eyes are in a heavy case,
your purse in a light. Yet you see how this world goes.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: I see it feelingly.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
What, art mad? A man may see how this world goes with
no eyes. Look with thine ears. See how yon justice rails
upon yon simple thief. Hark in thine ear: change places
and, handy-dandy, which is the justice, which is the
thief? Thou hast seen a farmer’s dog bark at a beggar?

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Ay, sir.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
And the creature run from the cur? There thou mightst
behold the great image of authority: a dog’s obeyed in
office.
Thou rascal beadle, hold thy bloody hand.
Why dost thou lash that whore? Strip thine own back.
Thou hotly lust’st to use her in that kind
For which thou whipp’st her. The usurer hangs the
cozener.
Through tattered clothes great vices do appear;
Robes and furred gowns hide all. Plate sin with gold,
And the strong lance of justice hurtless breaks.
Arm it in rags, a pigmy’s straw does pierce it.
None does offend—none, I say, none. I’ll able 'em.
Take that of me, my friend, who have the power
To seal th' accuser’s lips. Get thee glass eyes,
And like a scurvy politician seem
To see the things thou dost not. Now, now, now, now,
Pull off my boots. Harder, harder. So.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
[aside] O matter and impertinency mixed! Reason in
madness!

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
If thou wilt weep my fortunes, take my eyes.
I know thee well enough. Thy name is Gloucester.
Thou must be patient. We came crying hither.
Thou know’st the first time that we smell the air
We wawl and cry. I will preach to thee. Mark me.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Alack, alack the day!

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
When we are born, we cry that we are come
To this great stage of fools. This a good block.
It were a delicate stratagem to shoe
A troop of horse with felt. I’ll put ’t in proof.
And when I have stol'n upon these sons-in-law,
Then, kill, kill, kill, kill, kill, kill!
Enter GENTLEMAN with two others

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Oh, here he is. Lay hand upon him.—Sir,
Your most dear daughter—

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
No rescue? What, a prisoner? I am even
The natural fool of fortune. Use me well.
You shall have ransom. Let me have surgeons.
I am cut to th' brains.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: You shall have anything.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
No seconds? All myself?
Why, this would make a man a man of salt,
To use his eyes for garden water-pots,
Ay, and laying autumn’s dust.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: Good sir—

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
I will die bravely, like a smug bridegroom.
What, I will be jovial. Come, come.
I am a king, my masters, know you that?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: You are a royal one, and we obey you.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Then there’s life in ’t. Come, an if you get it, you
shall get it by running. Sa, sa, sa, sa.
Exit LEAR running, followed by two gentlemen

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
A sight most pitiful in the meanest wretch,
Past speaking of in a king. Thou hast a daughter
Who redeems nature from the general curse
Which twain have brought her to.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Hail, gentle sir.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: Sir, speed you. What’s your will?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Do you hear aught, sir, of a battle toward?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Most sure and vulgar. Everyone hears that
That can distinguish sound.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
But, by your favor,
How near’s the other army?

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Near and on speedy foot. The main descry
Stands in the hourly thought.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: I thank you, sir. That’s all.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Though that the queen on special cause is here,
Her army is moved on.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: I thank you, sir.
Exit GENTLEMAN

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
You ever gentle gods, take my breath from me.
Let not my worser spirit tempt me again
To die before you please.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: Well pray you, father.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: Now, good sir, what are you?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
A most poor man made tame to fortune’s blows,
Who by the art of known and feeling sorrows
Am pregnant to good pity. Give me your hand,
I’ll lead you to some biding.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Hearty thanks.
The bounty and the benison of heaven
To boot and boot.
Enter OSWALD the steward

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text:
A proclaimed prize! Most happy!
That eyeless head of thine was first framed flesh
To raise my fortunes. Thou old unhappy traitor,
Briefly thyself remember. The sword is out
That must destroy thee.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
Now let thy friendly hand
Put strength enough to ’t.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text:
Wherefore, bold peasant,
Darest thou support a published traitor? Hence,
Lest that th' infection of his fortune take
Like hold on thee. Let go his arm.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text: 'Chill not let go, zir, without vurther 'casion.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Let go, slave, or thou diest!

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Good gentleman, go your gait, and let poor volk pass.
An 'chud ha' bin zwaggered out of my life, ’twould not
ha' bin zo long as ’tis by a vortnight. Nay, come not
near th' old man. Keep out, che vor' ye, or I’se try
whether your costard or my ballow be the harder. 'Chill
be plain with you.

units.speaker: OSWALD
units.text: Out, dunghill!

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
'Chill pick your teeth, zir. Come, no matter vor your
foins.
EDGAR and OSWALD fight

[falling] Slave, thou hast slain me. Villain, take my
purse.
If ever thou wilt thrive, bury my body.
And give the letters which thou find’st about me
To Edmund, Earl of Gloucester. Seek him out
Upon the British party. O untimely death! [ dies]

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
I know thee well—a serviceable villain,
As duteous to the vices of thy mistress
As badness would desire.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text: What, is he dead?

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Sit you down, father. Rest you.
Let’s see these pockets. The letters that he speaks of
May be my friends. He’s dead. I am only sorry
He had no other death’s-man. Let us see.
[takes letters out of OSWALD’s pocket and opens them]
Leave, gentle wax, and, manners, blame us not.
To know our enemies' minds, we rip their hearts.
Their papers is more lawful.
[reads]
“Let our reciprocal vows be remembered. You have many
opportunities to cut him off. If your will want not,
time and place will be fruitfully offered. There is
nothing done if he return the conqueror. Then am I the
prisoner and his bed my gaol, from the loathed warmth
whereof deliver me, and supply the place for your labor.
Your—wife, so I would say—affectionate servant,
Goneril.”
O indistinguished space of woman’s will!
A plot upon her virtuous husband’s life,
And the exchange my brother!— Here in the sands
Thee I’ll rake up, the post unsanctified
Of murderous lechers. And in the mature time
With this ungracious paper strike the sight
Of the death-practiced duke. For him ’tis well
That of thy death and business I can tell.

units.speaker: GLOUCESTER
units.text:
The king is mad. How stiff is my vile sense,
That I stand up and have ingenious feeling
Of my huge sorrows. Better I were distract—
So should my thoughts be severed from my griefs,
And woes by wrong imaginations lose
The knowledge of themselves.
Drum afar off

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
Give me your hand.
Far off methinks I hear the beaten drum.
Come, father, I’ll bestow you with a friend.
Exeunt
Enter CORDELIA, KENT disguised, GENTLEMAN, and DOCTOR

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
O thou good Kent, how shall I live and work
To match thy goodness? My life will be too short,
And every measure fail me.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
To be acknowledged, madam, is o'erpaid.
All my reports go with the modest truth,
Nor more, nor clipped, but so.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
Be better suited.
These weeds are memories of those worser hours.
I prithee, put them off.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Pardon, dear madam.
Yet to be known shortens my made intent.
My boon I make it that you know me not
Till time and I think meet.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
Then be ’t so, my good lord.—
How does the king?

units.speaker: DOCTOR
units.text: Madam, sleeps still.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
O you kind gods,
Cure this great breach in his abusèd nature,
Th' untuned and jarring senses, O, wind up,
Of this child-changèd father!

units.speaker: DOCTOR
units.text:
So please your majesty
That we may wake the king? He hath slept long.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
Be governed by your knowledge, and proceed
I' th' sway of your own will. Is he arrayed?
Enter LEAR asleep in a chair carried by servants

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Ay, madam. In the heaviness of his sleep
We put fresh garments on him.

units.speaker: DOCTOR
units.text:
Be by, good madam, when we do awake him.
I doubt not of his temperance.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: Very well.

units.speaker: DOCTOR
units.text: Please you, draw near.—Louder the music there!

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
[kisses LEAR] O my dear father, restoration hang
Thy medicine on my lips, and let this kiss
Repair those violent harms that my two sisters
Have in thy reverence made!

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Kind and dear princess!

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
Had you not been their father, these white flakes
Did challenge pity of them. Was this a face
To be opposed against the warring winds?
To stand against the deep dread-bolted thunder
In the most terrible and nimble stroke
Of quick cross lightning? To watch—poor perdu!—
With this thin helm? Mine enemy’s meanest dog,
Though he had bit me, should have stood that night
Against my fire. And wast thou fain, poor father,
To hovel thee with swine and rogues forlorn
In short and musty straw? Alack, alack!
'Tis wonder that thy life and wits at once
Had not concluded all.—He wakes. Speak to him.

units.speaker: DOCTOR
units.text: Madam, do you. 'Tis fittest.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: How does my royal lord? How fares your majesty?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
You do me wrong to take me out o' th' grave.
Thou art a soul in bliss, but I am bound
Upon a wheel of fire, that mine own tears
Do scald like molten lead.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: Sir, do you know me?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: You are a spirit, I know. Where did you die?

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: [aside to DOCTOR] Still, still far wide!

units.speaker: DOCTOR
units.text: He’s scarce awake. Let him alone awhile.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Where have I been? Where am I? Fair daylight?
I am mightily abused. I should ev'n die with pity
To see another thus. I know not what to say.
I will not swear these are my hands. Let’s see.
I feel this pinprick. Would I were assured
Of my condition.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text:
[kneels]
O, look upon me, sir,
And hold your hands in benediction o'er me.
No, sir, you must not kneel.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Pray, do not mock me.
I am a very foolish fond old man,
Fourscore and upward, not an hour more nor less.
And to deal plainly
I fear I am not in my perfect mind.
Methinks I should know you, and know this man.
Yet I am doubtful, for I am mainly ignorant
What place this is, and all the skill I have
Remembers not these garments. Nor I know not
Where I did lodge last night. Do not laugh at me,
For as I am a man, I think this lady
To be my child Cordelia.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: And so I am, I am.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
Be your tears wet? Yes, faith. I pray, weep not.
If you have poison for me, I will drink it.
I know you do not love me, for your sisters
Have, as I do remember, done me wrong.
You have some cause; they have not.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: No cause, no cause.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Am I in France?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: In your own kingdom, sir.

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text: Do not abuse me.

units.speaker: DOCTOR
units.text:
Be comforted, good madam. The great rage,
You see, is killed in him. And yet it is danger
To make him even o'er the time he has lost.
Desire him to go in. Trouble him no more
Till further settling.

units.speaker: CORDELIA
units.text: Will ’t please your highness walk?

units.speaker: LEAR
units.text:
You must bear with me.
Pray you now, forget and forgive.
I am old and foolish.
Exeunt. Manent KENT and GENTLEMAN.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Holds it true, sir, that the Duke of Cornwall was so
slain?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: Most certain, sir.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: Who is conductor of his people?

units.speaker: KENT
units.text: As ’tis said, the bastard son of Gloucester.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
They say Edgar, his banished son, is with the Earl of
Kent in Germany.

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
Report is changeable. Tis time to look about. The
powers of the kingdom approach apace.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text:
The arbitrement is like to be bloody. Fare you well,
sir.
Exit GENTLEMAN

units.speaker: KENT
units.text:
My point and period will be throughly wrought,
Or well or ill, as this day’s battle’s fought.
Exit
Enter with drum and colors EDMUND, REGAN, gentlemen, and soldiers

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
[To a gentleman] Know of the duke if his last purpose
hold,
Or whether since he is advised by aught
To change the course. He’s full of alteration
And self-reproving. Bring his constant pleasure.
Exit gentleman

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Our sister’s man is certainly miscarried.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: 'Tis to be doubted, madam.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
Now, sweet lord,
You know the goodness I intend upon you.
Tell me but truly— but then speak the truth—
Do you not love my sister?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: In honored love.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
But have you never found my brother’s way
To the forfended place?

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: That thought abuses you.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
I am doubtful that you have been conjunct
And bosomed with her as far as we call hers.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: No, by mine honor, madam.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text:
I never shall endure her. Dear my lord,
Be not familiar with her.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
Fear me not.
She and the duke her husband!
Enter with drum and colors ALBANY and GONERIL, with troops

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
[aside] I had rather lose the battle than that sister
Should loosen him and me.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
Our very loving sister, well be-met.
Sir, this I hear: the king is come to his daughter,
With others whom the rigor of our state
Forced to cry out. Where I could not be honest
I never yet was valiant. For this business,
It touches us as France invades our land,
Not bolds the king, with others whom I fear
Most just and heavy causes make oppose.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: Sir, you speak nobly.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Why is this reasoned?

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text:
Combine together 'gainst the enemy,
For these domestic and particular broils
Are not the question here.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
Let’s then determine with the ancient of war
On our proceedings.

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text: I shall attend you presently at your tent.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: Sister, you’ll go with us?

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: No.

units.speaker: REGAN
units.text: 'Tis most convenient. Pray you, go with us.

units.speaker: GONERIL
units.text: [aside] Oh ho, I know the riddle.—I will go.
Enter EDGAR disguised

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
[to ALBANY] If e'er your grace had speech with man so
poor,
Hear me one word.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text:
[To EDMUND, REGAN, and GONERIL]
I’ll overtake you.—
Exeunt all but ALBANY and EDGAR

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: Speak.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
[Giving ALBANY a letter]
Before you fight the battle, ope this letter.
If you have victory, let the trumpet sound
For him that brought it. Wretched though I seem,
I can produce a champion that will prove
What is avouchèd there. If you miscarry,
Your business of the world hath so an end,
And machination ceases. Fortune love you.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: Stay till I have read the letter.

units.speaker: EDGAR
units.text:
I was forbid it.
When time shall serve, let but the herald cry,
And I’ll appear again.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: Why, fare thee well. I will o'erlook thy paper.
Exit EDGAR
Enter EDMUND

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
The enemy’s in view. Draw up your powers.
[gives ALBANY a document]
Here is the guess of their true strength and forces
By diligent discovery, but your haste
Is now urged on you.

units.speaker: ALBANY
units.text: We will greet the time.
Exit ALBANY

units.speaker: EDMUND
units.text:
To both these sisters have I sworn my love,
Each jealous of the other as the stung
Are of the adder. Which of them shall I take?
