sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: Do you mark that, my lord?

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
I cannot be so partial, Goneril,
To the great love I bear you—

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Pray you, content.
Come, sire, no more.—What, Oswald, ho!
[to FOOL] You, sir, more knave than fool, after your
master.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Pray you, content.
Come, sire, no more.—What, Oswald, ho!
[to FOOL] You, sir, more knave than fool, after your
master.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Nuncle Lear, nuncle Lear, tarry and take the fool with
thee.
A fox when one has caught her
And such a daughter
Should sure to the slaughter,
If my cap would buy a halter.
So the fool follows after.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:

thee.

Should sure to the slaughter,

So the fool follows after.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
This man hath had good counsel—a hundred knights!
'Tis politic and safe to let him keep
At point a hundred knights, yes, that on every dream,
Each buzz, each fancy, each complaint, dislike,
He may enguard his dotage with their powers
And hold our lives in mercy?— Oswald, I say!

sentences.speaker: GONERIL

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: Well, you may fear too far.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Safer than trust too far.
Let me still take away the harms I fear,
Not fear still to be taken. I know his heart.
What he hath uttered I have writ my sister.
If she sustain him and his hundred knights
When I have showed th' unfitness—

sentences.speaker: GONERIL

Enter OSWALD the steward

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Here, madam.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
How now, Oswald?
What, have you writ that letter to my sister?

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
How now, Oswald?
What, have you writ that letter to my sister?

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Ay, madam.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Take you some company, and away to horse.
Inform her full of my particular fear,
And thereto add such reasons of your own
As may compact it more. Get you gone
And hasten your return.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:

Inform her full of my particular fear,
And thereto add such reasons of your own
As may compact it more. Get you gone
And hasten your return.

Exit OSWALD

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
No, no, my lord,
This milky gentleness and course of yours
Though I condemn not, yet, under pardon
You are much more attasked for want of wisdom
Than praised for harmful mildness.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
No, no, my lord,
This milky gentleness and course of yours
Though I condemn not, yet, under pardon
You are much more attasked for want of wisdom
Than praised for harmful mildness.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
How far your eyes may pierce I cannot tell.
Striving to better, oft we mar what’s well.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
How far your eyes may pierce I cannot tell.
Striving to better, oft we mar what’s well.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: Nay, then—

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: Well, well, th' event.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: Well, well, th' event.
Exeunt
Enter LEAR, KENT disguised, and FOOL

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
[to KENT, giving him letters] Go you before to
Gloucester with these letters. Acquaint my daughter no
further with anything you know than comes from her
demand out of the letter. If your diligence be not
speedy, I shall be there afore you.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
[to KENT, giving him letters] Go you before to
Gloucester with these letters. Acquaint my daughter no
further with anything you know than comes from her
demand out of the letter. If your diligence be not
speedy, I shall be there afore you.

sentences.speaker: LEAR

[to KENT, giving him letters] Go you before to

further with anything you know than comes from her
demand out of the letter. If your diligence be not
speedy, I shall be there afore you.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
I will not sleep, my lord, till I have delivered your
letter.
Exit KENT

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
If a man’s brains were in ’s heels, were ’t not in
danger of kibes?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Ay, boy.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Then, I prithee, be merry. Thy wit shall ne'er go
slipshod.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Then, I prithee, be merry. Thy wit shall ne'er go
slipshod.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Then, I prithee, be merry. Thy wit shall ne'er go
slipshod.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Ha, ha, ha!

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Shalt see thy other daughter will use thee kindly. For
though she’s as like this as a crab’s like an apple, yet
I can tell what I can tell.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Shalt see thy other daughter will use thee kindly. For
though she’s as like this as a crab’s like an apple, yet
I can tell what I can tell.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Why, what canst thou tell, my boy?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
She will taste as like this as a crab does to a crab.
Thou canst tell why one’s nose stands i' th' middle on
’s face?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
She will taste as like this as a crab does to a crab.
Thou canst tell why one’s nose stands i' th' middle on
’s face?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: No.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Why, to keep one’s eyes of either side ’s nose, that
what a man cannot smell out, he may spy into.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: I did her wrong—

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: Canst tell how an oyster makes his shell?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: No.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: Nor I neither. But I can tell why a snail has a house.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: Nor I neither. But I can tell why a snail has a house.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Why?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Why, to put ’s head in—not to give it away to his
daughters and leave his horns without a case.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Why, to put ’s head in—not to give it away to his
daughters and leave his horns without a case.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
I will forget my nature. So kind a father!—Be my horses
ready?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
I will forget my nature. So kind a father!—Be my horses
ready?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Thy asses are gone about 'em. The reason why the seven
stars are no more than seven is a pretty reason.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Thy asses are gone about 'em. The reason why the seven
stars are no more than seven is a pretty reason.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Because they are not eight?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: Yes indeed. Thou wouldst make a good fool.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: Yes indeed. Thou wouldst make a good fool.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: To take ’t again perforce—Monster ingratitude!

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: To take ’t again perforce—Monster ingratitude!

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
If thou wert my fool, nuncle, I’d have thee beaten for
being old before thy time.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: How’s that?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Thou shouldst not have been old till thou hadst been
wise.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
O, let me not be mad, not mad, sweet heaven!
I would not be mad.
Keep me in temper. I would not be mad.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
O, let me not be mad, not mad, sweet heaven!
I would not be mad.
Keep me in temper. I would not be mad.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
O, let me not be mad, not mad, sweet heaven!
I would not be mad.
Keep me in temper. I would not be mad.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
O, let me not be mad, not mad, sweet heaven!
I would not be mad.
Keep me in temper. I would not be mad.
Enter a GENTLEMAN

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: How now? Are the horses ready?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Ready, my lord.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Come, boy.
Exeunt LEAR and GENTLEMAN

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
She that’s a maid now and laughs at my departure,
Shall not be a maid long unless things be cut shorter.
Exit
Enter EDMUND the bastard and CURAN, severally

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Save thee, Curan.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Save thee, Curan.

sentences.speaker: CURAN
sentences.text:
And you, sir. I have been with your father and given
him notice that the Duke of Cornwall and Regan his
duchess will be here with him this night.

sentences.speaker: CURAN
sentences.text:
And you, sir. I have been with your father and given
him notice that the Duke of Cornwall and Regan his
duchess will be here with him this night.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: How comes that?

sentences.speaker: CURAN
sentences.text:
Nay, I know not. You have heard of the news abroad?—I
mean the whispered ones, for they are yet but
ear-kissing arguments.

sentences.speaker: CURAN
sentences.text:
Nay, I know not. You have heard of the news abroad?—I
mean the whispered ones, for they are yet but
ear-kissing arguments.

sentences.speaker: CURAN
sentences.text:
Nay, I know not. You have heard of the news abroad?—I
mean the whispered ones, for they are yet but
ear-kissing arguments.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Not I. Pray you, what are they?

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Not I. Pray you, what are they?

sentences.speaker: CURAN
sentences.text:
Have you heard of no likely wars toward ’twixt the two
Dukes of Cornwall and Albany?

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Not a word.

sentences.speaker: CURAN
sentences.text: You may do then in time. Fare you well, sir.

sentences.speaker: CURAN
sentences.text: You may do then in time. Fare you well, sir.
Exit CURAN

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
The duke be here tonight? The better—best!
This weaves itself perforce into my business.
My father hath set guard to take my brother.
And I have one thing, of a queasy question,
Which I must act. Briefness and fortune, work!—
Brother, a word. Descend, brother, I say.

Enter EDGAR

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
My father watches. O sir, fly this place.
Intelligence is given where you are hid.
You have now the good advantage of the night.
Have you not spoken 'gainst the Duke of Cornwall aught?
He’s coming hither—now, i' th' night, i' th' haste,
And Regan with him. Have you nothing said
Upon his party 'gainst the Duke of Albany?
Advise yourself.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: I am sure on ’t, not a word.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
I hear my father coming. Pardon me.
In cunning I must draw my sword upon you.
Draw. Seem to defend yourself. Now quit you well.—
[loudly] Yield! Come before my father. Light, ho! Here!
[aside to EDGAR ] Fly, brother, fly.
[loudly]
Torches, torches!
[aside to EDGAR ] So, farewell.

sentences.text:
Some blood drawn on me would beget opinion
Of my more fierce endeavor.
[cuts his own arm]
I have seen drunkards
Do more than this in sport.— Father, father!—
Stop, stop!—No help?

Enter GLOUCESTER and servants with torches

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Now Edmund, where’s the villain?

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
Here stood he in the dark, his sharp sword out,
Mumbling of wicked charms, conjuring the moon
To stand ’s auspicious mistress—

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: But where is he?

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Look, sir, I bleed.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Where is the villain, Edmund?

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Fled this way, sir, when by no means he could—

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Pursue him, ho! Go after.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Pursue him, ho! Go after.
Exeunt some servants

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: “By no means” what?

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
Persuade me to the murder of your lordship,
But that I told him the revenging gods
'Gainst parricides did all the thunder bend,
Spoke with how manifold and strong a bond
The child was bound to th' father. Sir, in fine,
Seeing how loathly opposite I stood
To his unnatural purpose, in fell motion,
With his preparèd sword he charges home
My unprovided body, latched mine arm.
And when he saw my best alarumed spirits,
Bold in the quarrel’s right, roused to the encounter,
Or whether ghasted by the noise I made,
Full suddenly he fled.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Let him fly far.
Not in this land shall he remain uncaught.
And found—dispatch. The noble duke my master,
My worthy arch and patron, comes tonight.
By his authority I will proclaim it
That he which finds him shall deserve our thanks,
Bringing the murderous coward to the stake.
He that conceals him, death.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
When I dissuaded him from his intent,
And found him pight to do it, with cursed speech
I threatened to discover him. He replied,
“Thou unpossessing bastard! Dost thou think
If I would stand against thee, would the reposal
Of any trust, virtue, or worth in thee
Make thy words faithed? No. What I should deny—
As this I would, ay, though thou didst produce
My very character—I’d turn it all
To thy suggestion, plot, and damnèd practice.
And thou must make a dullard of the world,
If they not thought the profits of my death
Were very pregnant and potential spirits
To make thee seek it.”

Tucket within

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
O strange and fastened villain!
Would he deny his letter, said he? I never got him.—
Hark, the duke’s trumpets. I know not why he comes.
All ports I’ll bar. The villain shall not ’scape.
The duke must grant me that. Besides, his picture
I will send far and near, that all the kingdom
May have the due note of him.— And of my land,
Loyal and natural boy, I’ll work the means
To make thee capable.

Enter the Duke of CORNWALL, REGAN, and attendants

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
How now, my noble friend? Since I came hither,
Which I can call but now, I have heard strange news.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
How now, my noble friend? Since I came hither,
Which I can call but now, I have heard strange news.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
If it be true, all vengeance comes too short
Which can pursue th' offender. How dost, my lord?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
If it be true, all vengeance comes too short
Which can pursue th' offender. How dost, my lord?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: O madam, my old heart is cracked, it’s cracked.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
What, did my father’s godson seek your life?—
He whom my father named, your Edgar?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
What, did my father’s godson seek your life?—
He whom my father named, your Edgar?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: O, lady, lady, shame would have it hid.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Was he not companion with the riotous knights
That tend upon my father?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: I know not, madam. 'Tis too bad, too bad.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: I know not, madam. 'Tis too bad, too bad.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Yes, madam, he was of that consort.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
No marvel then, though he were ill affected.
'Tis they have put him on the old man’s death,
To have th' expense and spoil of his revenues.
I have this present evening from my sister
Been well informed of them—and with such cautions
That if they come to sojourn at my house
I’ll not be there.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Nor I, assure thee, Regan.—
Edmund, I hear that you have shown your father
A childlike office.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Nor I, assure thee, Regan.—
Edmund, I hear that you have shown your father
A childlike office.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: It was my duty, sir.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
He did bewray his practice, and received
This hurt you see striving to apprehend him.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Is he pursued?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Ay, my good lord.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
If he be taken, he shall never more
Be feared of doing harm. Make your own purpose
How in my strength you please.— For you, Edmund,
Whose virtue and obedience doth this instant
So much commend itself, you shall be ours.
Natures of such deep trust we shall much need.
You we first seize on.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
I shall serve you, sir,
Truly, however else.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: [to CORNWALL] For him I thank your grace.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: You know not why we came to visit you—

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Thus out of season, threading dark-eyed night.
Occasions, noble Gloucester, of some poise,
Wherein we must have use of your advice:
Our father he hath writ, so hath our sister,
Of differences, which I least thought it fit
To answer from our home. The several messengers
From hence attend dispatch. Our good old friend,
Lay comforts to your bosom, and bestow
Your needful counsel to our business,
Which craves the instant use.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
I serve you, madam.
Your graces are right welcome.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER

Your graces are right welcome.
Flourish. Exeunt
Flourish. Exeunt
Enter KENT disguised and OSWALD the steward, severally

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Good dawning to thee, friend. Art of this house?

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Good dawning to thee, friend. Art of this house?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Ay.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Where may we set our horses?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: I' th' mire.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Prithee, if thou lovest me, tell me.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: I love thee not.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Why, then, I care not for thee.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Why, then, I care not for thee.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
If I had thee in Lipsbury pinfold, I would make thee
care for me.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Why dost thou use me thus? I know thee not.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Why dost thou use me thus? I know thee not.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Fellow, I know thee.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: What dost thou know me for?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
A knave, a rascal, an eater of broken meats; a base,
proud, shallow, beggarly, three-suited, hundred-pound,
filthy, worsted-stocking knave; a lily-livered,
action-taking knave; a whoreson, glass-gazing,
super-serviceable finical rogue; one-trunk-inheriting
slave; one that wouldst be a bawd in way of good
service; and art nothing but the composition of a knave,
beggar, coward, pander, and the son and heir of a
mongrel bitch; one whom I will beat into clamorous
whining if thou deniest the least syllable of thy
addition.

service; and art nothing but the composition of a knave,
beggar, coward, pander, and the son and heir of a
mongrel bitch; one whom I will beat into clamorous
whining if thou deniest the least syllable of thy
addition.

action-taking knave; a whoreson, glass-gazing,

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
Why, what a monstrous fellow art thou, thus to rail on
one that is neither known of thee nor knows thee!

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
What a brazen-faced varlet art thou to deny thou
knowest me! Is it two days ago since I tripped up thy
heels and beat thee before the king? Draw, you rogue,
for though it be night yet the moon shines. I’ll make a
sop o' th' moonshine of you. [draws his sword] Draw, you
whoreson cullionly barber-monger, draw!

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Away! I have nothing to do with thee.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Away! I have nothing to do with thee.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Draw, you rascal. You come with letters against the
king and take Vanity the puppet’s part against the
royalty of her father. Draw, you rogue, or I’ll so
carbonado your shanks. Draw, you rascal! Come your ways.

Enter EDMUND the bastard with his rapier drawn, the Duke of CORNWALL, the Duchess REGAN, GLOUCESTER, and servants

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: How now? What’s the matter? Part.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: How now? What’s the matter? Part.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: How now? What’s the matter? Part.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
[to EDMUND] With you, goodman boy, if you please. Come,
I’ll flesh ye. Come on, young master.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
[to EDMUND] With you, goodman boy, if you please. Come,
I’ll flesh ye. Come on, young master.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
[to EDMUND] With you, goodman boy, if you please. Come,

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Weapons, arms? What’s the matter here?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Weapons, arms? What’s the matter here?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Keep peace, upon your lives.
He dies that strikes again. What is the matter?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Keep peace, upon your lives.
He dies that strikes again. What is the matter?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Keep peace, upon your lives.
He dies that strikes again. What is the matter?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: The messengers from our sister and the king.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: What is your difference? Speak.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: What is your difference? Speak.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: I am scarce in breath, my lord.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
No marvel, you have so bestirred your valor. You
cowardly rascal, nature disclaims in thee. A tailor made
thee.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
No marvel, you have so bestirred your valor. You
cowardly rascal, nature disclaims in thee. A tailor made
thee.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
No marvel, you have so bestirred your valor. You
cowardly rascal, nature disclaims in thee. A tailor made
thee.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Thou art a strange fellow. A tailor make a man?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Thou art a strange fellow. A tailor make a man?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Ay, a tailor, sir. A stone-cutter or painter could not
have made him so ill though they had been but two years
o' th' trade.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Ay, a tailor, sir. A stone-cutter or painter could not
have made him so ill though they had been but two years
o' th' trade.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Speak yet. How grew your quarrel?

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
This ancient ruffian, sir, whose life I have spared at
suit of his gray beard—

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Thou whoreson zed, thou unnecessary letter!—My lord, if
you will give me leave, I will tread this unbolted
villain into mortar and daub the wall of a jakes with
him.—Spare my gray beard, you wagtail?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Peace, sirrah!
You beastly knave, know you no reverence?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Peace, sirrah!
You beastly knave, know you no reverence?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Yes, sir, but anger hath a privilege.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Why art thou angry?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
That such a slave as this should wear a sword,
Who wears no honesty. Such smiling rogues as these,
Like rats, oft bite the holy cords atwain
Which are too intrinse t' unloose, smooth every passion
That in the natures of their lords rebel,
Bring oil to fire, snow to the colder moods;
Renege, affirm, and turn their halcyon beaks
With every gale and vary of their masters,
Knowing naught, like dogs, but following.—
A plague upon your epileptic visage!
Smile you my speeches as I were a fool?
Goose, an I had you upon Sarum plain,
I’d drive ye cackling home to Camelot.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Why, art thou mad, old fellow?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
[to KENT] How fell you out?
Say that.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
[to KENT] How fell you out?
Say that.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
No contraries hold more antipathy
Than I and such a knave.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Why dost thou call him “knave?” What’s his offense?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: His countenance likes me not.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: No more perchance does mine, nor his, nor hers.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Sir, ’tis my occupation to be plain.
I have seen better faces in my time
Than stands on any shoulder that I see
Before me at this instant.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
This is some fellow,
Who, having been praised for bluntness, doth affect
A saucy roughness and constrains the garb
Quite from his nature. He cannot flatter, he.
An honest mind and plain, he must speak truth.
An they will take it, so. If not, he’s plain.
These kind of knaves I know, which in this plainness
Harbor more craft and more corrupter ends
Than twenty silly-ducking observants
That stretch their duties nicely.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Sir, in good faith, or in sincere verity,
Under th' allowance of your great aspect,
Whose influence, like the wreath of radiant fire
On flickering Phoebus' front—

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: What mean’st by this?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
To go out of my dialect, which you discommend so much.
I know, sir, I am no flatterer. He that beguiled you in
a plain accent was a plain knave, which for my part I
will not be, though I should win your displeasure to
entreat me to ’t.

entreat me to ’t.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: [to OSWALD] What was th' offense you gave him?

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
I never gave him any.
It pleased the king his master very late
To strike at me upon his misconstruction
When he, conjunct and flattering his displeasure,
Tripped me behind; being down, insulted, railed,
And put upon him such a deal of man
That worthied him, got praises of the king
For him attempting who was self-subdued.
And in the fleshment of this dread exploit
Drew on me here again.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
None of these rogues and cowards
But Ajax is their fool.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Fetch forth the stocks, ho!—
You stubborn ancient knave, you reverend braggart,
We’ll teach you.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Fetch forth the stocks, ho!—
You stubborn ancient knave, you reverend braggart,
We’ll teach you.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Sir, I am too old to learn.
Call not your stocks for me. I serve the king,
On whose employment I was sent to you.
You shall do small respect, show too bold malice
Against the grace and person of my master,
Stocking his messenger.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Fetch forth the stocks!
As I have life and honor, there shall he sit till noon.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Fetch forth the stocks!
As I have life and honor, there shall he sit till noon.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Till noon? Till night, my lord, and all night too.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Till noon? Till night, my lord, and all night too.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Why, madam, if I were your father’s dog,
You should not use me so.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Sir, being his knave, I will.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Sir, being his knave, I will.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
This is a fellow of the selfsame color
Our sister speaks of.—Come, bring away the stocks!

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
This is a fellow of the selfsame color
Our sister speaks of.—Come, bring away the stocks!

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Let me beseech your grace not to do so.
His fault is much, and the good king his master
Will check him for ’t. Your purposed low correction
Is such as basest and contemned’st wretches
For pilferings and most common trespasses
Are punished with.
The king his master needs must take it ill,
That he, so slightly valued in his messenger,
Should have him thus restrained.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: I’ll answer that.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
My sister may receive it much more worse
To have her gentleman abused, assaulted
For following her affairs.—Put in his legs.

sentences.speaker: REGAN

For following her affairs.—Put in his legs.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: [to GLOUCESTER ] Come, my good lord, away.
Exeunt all but GLOUCESTER and KENT

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
I am sorry for thee, friend. 'Tis the duke’s pleasure,
Whose disposition, all the world well knows,
Will not be rubbed nor stopped. I’ll entreat for thee.

sentences.text:
I am sorry for thee, friend. 'Tis the duke’s pleasure,
Whose disposition, all the world well knows,
Will not be rubbed nor stopped. I’ll entreat for thee.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Pray you do not, sir. I have watched and traveled hard.
Some time I shall sleep out. The rest I’ll whistle.
A good man’s fortune may grow out at heels.
Give you good morrow.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: The duke’s to blame in this. 'Twill be ill taken.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: The duke’s to blame in this. 'Twill be ill taken.
Exit GLOUCESTER

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Good King, that must approve the common saw,
Thou out of heaven’s benediction comest
To the warm sun.
[takes out a letter]
Approach, thou beacon to this underglobe,
That by thy comfortable beams I may
Peruse this letter. Nothing almost sees miracles
But misery. I know ’tis from Cordelia,
Who hath most fortunately been informed
Of my obscurèd course and [reads the letter] “shall
find time
From this enormous state, seeking to give
Losses their remedies.” All weary and o'erwatched,
Take vantage, heavy eyes, not to behold
This shameful lodging.
Fortune, good night. Smile once more. Turn thy wheel.
[sleeps]

Enter EDGAR

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
I heard myself proclaimed,
And by the happy hollow of a tree
Escaped the hunt. No port is free, no place
That guard and most unusual vigilance
Does not attend my taking. Whiles I may ’scape,
I will preserve myself, and am bethought
To take the basest and most poorest shape
That ever penury in contempt of man
Brought near to beast. My face I’ll grime with filth,
Blanket my loins, elf all my hair in knots,
And with presented nakedness outface
The winds and persecutions of the sky.
The country gives me proof and precedent
Of Bedlam beggars, who with roaring voices
Strike in their numbed and mortified bare arms
Pins, wooden pricks, nails, sprigs of rosemary,
And with this horrible object from low farms,
Poor pelting villages, sheepcotes, and mills,
Sometime with lunatic bans, sometime with prayers,
Enforce their charity. “Poor Turlygod!” “Poor Tom!”—
That’s something yet. Edgar I nothing am.

KENT in the stocks. Enter LEAR, FOOL, and GENTLEMAN
KENT in the stocks. Enter LEAR, FOOL, and GENTLEMAN

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
'Tis strange that they should so depart from home,
And not send back my messenger.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
As I learned,
The night before there was no purpose in them
Of this remove.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: [to LEAR] Hail to thee, noble master!

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Ha! Makest thou this shame thy pastime?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Ha! Makest thou this shame thy pastime?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: No, my lord.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Ha, ha! Look, he wears cruel garters. Horses are tied
by the heads, dogs and bears by the neck, monkeys by the
loins, and men by the legs. When a man’s overlusty at
legs, then he wears wooden nether-stocks.

by the heads, dogs and bears by the neck, monkeys by the

legs, then he wears wooden nether-stocks.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
[to KENT] What’s he that hath so much thy place mistook
To set thee here?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
It is both he and she:
Your son and daughter.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: No.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Yes.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: No, I say.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: I say “Yea.”

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: No, no, they would not.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Yes, they have.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: By Jupiter, I swear “No.”

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: By Juno, I swear “Ay.”

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
They durst not do ’t.
They could not, would not do ’t. 'Tis worse than murder
To do upon respect such violent outrage.
Resolve me with all modest haste which way
Thou mightst deserve or they impose this usage,
Coming from us.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
My lord, when at their home
I did commend your highness' letters to them.
Ere I was risen from the place that showed
My duty kneeling, came there a reeking post,
Stewed in his haste, half breathless, panting forth
From Goneril his mistress salutations,
Delivered letters spite of intermission,
Which presently they read, on whose contents
They summoned up their meiny, straight took horse,
Commanded me to follow and attend
The leisure of their answer, gave me cold looks.
And meeting here the other messenger,
Whose welcome I perceived had poisoned mine—
Being the very fellow which of late
Displayed so saucily against your highness—
Having more man than wit about me, drew.
He raised the house with loud and coward cries.
Your son and daughter found this trespass worth
The shame which here it suffers.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Winter’s not gone yet, if the wild geese fly that way.
Fathers that wear rags
Do make their children blind.
But fathers that bear bags
Shall see their children kind.
Fortune, that arrant whore,
Ne'er turns the key to th' poor.
But for all this thou shalt have as many dolors for thy
daughters as thou canst tell in a year.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
O, how this mother swells up toward my heart!
Hysterica passio , down, thou climbing sorrow.
Thy element’s below.—Where is this daughter?

sentences.speaker: LEAR

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: With the earl, sir, here within.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Follow me not. Stay here.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Follow me not. Stay here.
Exit LEAR

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Made you no more offense but what you speak of?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
None.
How chance the king comes with so small a train?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
None.
How chance the king comes with so small a train?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
An thou hadst been set i' th' stocks for that
question, thou’dst well deserved it.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Why, Fool?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
We’ll set thee to school to an ant to teach thee
there’s no laboring i' th' winter. All that follow their
noses are led by their eyes but blind men, and there’s
not a nose among twenty but can smell him that’s
stinking. Let go thy hold when a great wheel runs down a
hill, lest it break thy neck with following it. But the
great one that goes up the hill, let him draw thee
after. When a wise man gives thee better counsel, give
me mine again. I would have none but knaves follow it
since a fool gives it.
That sir which serves and seeks for gain,
And follows but for form,
Will pack when it begins to rain
And leave thee in the storm.
But I will tarry. The fool will stay.
And let the wise man fly.
The knave turns fool that runs away;
The fool, no knave, perdie.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Where learned you this, Fool?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: Not i' th' stocks, fool.
Enter LEAR and GLOUCESTER

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Deny to speak with me? They are sick? They are weary?
They have traveled all the night?—mere fetches, ay!
The images of revolt and flying off.
Fetch me a better answer.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:

They have traveled all the night?—mere fetches, ay!
The images of revolt and flying off.
Fetch me a better answer.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
My dear lord,
You know the fiery quality of the duke,
How unremoveable and fixed he is
In his own course.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Vengeance, plague, death, confusion!
“Fiery?” What “quality?” Why, Gloucester, Gloucester,
I’d speak with the Duke of Cornwall and his wife.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Vengeance, plague, death, confusion!
“Fiery?” What “quality?” Why, Gloucester, Gloucester,
I’d speak with the Duke of Cornwall and his wife.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Well, my good lord, I have informed them so.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: “Informed them?” Dost thou understand me, man?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Ay, my good lord.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
The king would speak with Cornwall. The dear father
Would with his daughter speak, commands, tends service.
Are they “informed” of this? My breath and blood!
“Fiery?” The “fiery” duke? Tell the hot duke that Lear—
No, but not yet. Maybe he is not well.
Infirmity doth still neglect all office
Whereto our health is bound. We are not ourselves
When nature, being oppressed, commands the mind
To suffer with the body. I’ll forbear,
And am fallen out with my more headier will
To take the indisposed and sickly fit
For the sound man.
[notices KENT again]
Death on my state! Wherefore
Should he sit here? This act persuades me
That this remotion of the duke and her
Is practice only. Give me my servant forth.
Go tell the duke and ’s wife I’d speak with them—
Now, presently. Bid them come forth and hear me,
Or at their chamber door I’ll beat the drum
Till it cry sleep to death.

Infirmity doth still neglect all office
Whereto our health is bound. We are not ourselves
When nature, being oppressed, commands the mind
To suffer with the body. I’ll forbear,
And am fallen out with my more headier will
To take the indisposed and sickly fit
For the sound man.
[notices KENT again]
Death on my state! Wherefore
Should he sit here? This act persuades me
That this remotion of the duke and her
Is practice only. Give me my servant forth.
Go tell the duke and ’s wife I’d speak with them—
Now, presently. Bid them come forth and hear me,
Or at their chamber door I’ll beat the drum
Till it cry sleep to death.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: I would have all well betwixt you.
Exit GLOUCESTER

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: O me, my heart, my rising heart! But down.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: O me, my heart, my rising heart! But down.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Cry to it, nuncle, as the cockney did to the eels when
she put 'em i' th' paste alive. She knapped 'em o' th'
coxcombs with a stick and cried, “Down, wantons, down!”
'Twas her brother that, in pure kindness to his horse,
buttered his hay.

Enter the Duke of CORNWALL, REGAN, GLOUCESTER, and servants

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Good morrow to you both.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Hail to your grace.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Hail to your grace.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: I am glad to see your highness.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Regan, I think you are. I know what reason
I have to think so: if thou shouldst not be glad,
I would divorce me from thy mother’s tomb,
Sepulchring an adultress.
[to KENT] Oh, are you free?
Some other time for that.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Belovèd Regan,
Thy sister’s naught. O Regan, she hath tied
Sharp-toothed unkindness, like a vulture, here.
[indicates his heart]
I can scarce speak to thee. Thou'lt not believe
With how depraved a quality— O Regan!

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I pray you, sir, take patience. I have hope
You less know how to value her desert
Than she to scant her duty.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I pray you, sir, take patience. I have hope
You less know how to value her desert
Than she to scant her duty.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Say, how is that?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I cannot think my sister in the least
Would fail her obligation. If, sir, perchance
She have restrained the riots of your followers,
'Tis on such ground and to such wholesome end
As clears her from all blame.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I cannot think my sister in the least
Would fail her obligation. If, sir, perchance
She have restrained the riots of your followers,
'Tis on such ground and to such wholesome end
As clears her from all blame.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: My curses on her!

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
O sir, you are old.
Nature in you stands on the very verge
Of his confine. You should be ruled and led
By some discretion that discerns your state
Better than you yourself. Therefore I pray you
That to our sister you do make return.
Say you have wronged her, sir.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Ask her forgiveness?
Do you but mark how this becomes the house?—
[kneels] “Dear daughter, I confess that I am old.
Age is unnecessary. On my knees I beg
That you’ll vouchsafe me raiment, bed, and food.”

Return you to my sister.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
[rising] Never, Regan.
She hath abated me of half my train,
Looked black upon me, struck me with her tongue,
Most serpentlike, upon the very heart.
All the stored vengeances of heaven fall
On her ingrateful top! Strike her young bones,
You taking airs, with lameness!

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Fie, sir, fie!

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Fie, sir, fie!

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
You nimble lightnings, dart your blinding flames
Into her scornful eyes! Infect her beauty,
You fen-sucked fogs drawn by the powerful sun,
To fall and blister!

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
O the blessed gods!
So will you wish on me when the rash mood is on.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
O the blessed gods!
So will you wish on me when the rash mood is on.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
No, Regan, thou shalt never have my curse.
Thy tender-hafted nature shall not give
Thee o'er to harshness. Her eyes are fierce, but thine
Do comfort and not burn. 'Tis not in thee
To grudge my pleasures, to cut off my train,
To bandy hasty words, to scant my sizes,
And in conclusion to oppose the bolt
Against my coming in. Thou better know’st
The offices of nature, bond of childhood,
Effects of courtesy, dues of gratitude.
Thy half o' th' kingdom hast thou not forgot,
Wherein I thee endowed.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Good sir, to the purpose.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Who put my man i' th' stocks?
Tucket within

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: What trumpet’s that?
Enter OSWALD the steward

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I know ’t—my sister’s. This approves her letter
That she would soon be here. [to OSWALD]
Is your lady come?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I know ’t—my sister’s. This approves her letter
That she would soon be here. [to OSWALD]
Is your lady come?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
This is a slave whose easy borrowed pride
Dwells in the fickle grace of her he follows.—
Out, varlet, from my sight!

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: What means your grace?
Enter GONERIL

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Who stocked my servant? Regan, I have good hope
Thou didst not know on ’t.—Who comes here? O heavens,
If you do love old men, if your sweet sway
Allow obedience, if yourselves are old,
Make it your cause. Send down, and take my part!
[to GONERIL ] Art not ashamed to look upon this beard?—
O Regan, wilt thou take her by the hand?

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Why not by th' hand, sir? How have I offended?
All’s not offense that indiscretion finds
And dotage terms so.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
O sides, you are too tough.
Will you yet hold?—How came my man i' th' stocks?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
I set him there, sir, but his own disorders
Deserved much less advancement.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: You! Did you?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: You! Did you?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I pray you, father, being weak, seem so.
If till the expiration of your month,
You will return and sojourn with my sister,
Dismissing half your train, come then to me.
I am now from home, and out of that provision
Which shall be needful for your entertainment.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Return to her, and fifty men dismissed?
No, rather I abjure all roofs, and choose
To be a comrade with the wolf and owl—
To wage against the enmity o' th' air—
Necessity’s sharp pinch! Return with her?
Why, the hot-blooded France that dowerless took
Our youngest born— I could as well be brought
To knee his throne, and, squirelike, pension beg
To keep base life afoot. Return with her?
Persuade me rather to be slave and sumpter
To this detested groom. [indicates OSWALD ]

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: At your choice, sir.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Now, I prithee, daughter, do not make me mad.
I will not trouble thee, my child. Farewell.
We’ll no more meet, no more see one another.
But yet thou art my flesh, my blood, my daughter—
Or rather a disease that’s in my flesh,
Which I must needs call mine. Thou art a boil,
A plague-sore or embossèd carbuncle
In my corrupted blood. But I’ll not chide thee.
Let shame come when it will. I do not call it.
I do not bid the thunder-bearer shoot,
Nor tell tales of thee to high-judging Jove.
Mend when thou canst. Be better at thy leisure.
I can be patient. I can stay with Regan,
I and my hundred knights.

Nor tell tales of thee to high-judging Jove.
Mend when thou canst. Be better at thy leisure.
I can be patient. I can stay with Regan,
I and my hundred knights.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Now, I prithee, daughter, do not make me mad.
I will not trouble thee, my child. Farewell.
We’ll no more meet, no more see one another.
But yet thou art my flesh, my blood, my daughter—
Or rather a disease that’s in my flesh,
Which I must needs call mine. Thou art a boil,
A plague-sore or embossèd carbuncle
In my corrupted blood. But I’ll not chide thee.
Let shame come when it will. I do not call it.
I do not bid the thunder-bearer shoot,
Nor tell tales of thee to high-judging Jove.
Mend when thou canst. Be better at thy leisure.
I can be patient. I can stay with Regan,
I and my hundred knights.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Not altogether so, sir.
I looked not for you yet, nor am provided
For your fit welcome. Give ear, sir, to my sister.
For those that mingle reason with your passion
Must be content to think you old, and so—
But she knows what she does.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Is this well spoken now?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I dare avouch it, sir. What, fifty followers?
Is it not well? What should you need of more—
Yea, or so many— sith that both charge and danger
Speak 'gainst so great a number? How, in one house,
Should many people under two commands
Hold amity? 'Tis hard; almost impossible.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Why might not you, my lord, receive attendance
From those that she calls servants, or from mine?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Why not, my lord? If then they chanced to slack you,
We could control them. If you will come to me—
For now I spy a danger— I entreat you
To bring but five and twenty. To no more
Will I give place or notice.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: I gave you all—

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: And in good time you gave it.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Made you my guardians, my depositaries,
But kept a reservation to be followed
With such a number. What, must I come to you
With five and twenty, Regan? Said you so?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: And speak ’t again, my lord. No more with me.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: And speak ’t again, my lord. No more with me.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Those wicked creatures yet do look well favored
When others are more wicked. Not being the worst
Stands in some rank of praise.
[to GONERIL] I’ll go with thee.
Thy fifty yet doth double five and twenty,
And thou art twice her love.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Hear me, my lord.
What need you five and twenty, ten, or five
To follow in a house where twice so many
Have a command to tend you?

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Hear me, my lord.
What need you five and twenty, ten, or five
To follow in a house where twice so many
Have a command to tend you?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: What need one?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
O, reason not the need! Our basest beggars
Are in the poorest thing superfluous.
Allow not nature more than nature needs,
Man’s life’s as cheap as beast’s. Thou art a lady.
If only to go warm were gorgeous,
Why, nature needs not what thou gorgeous wear’st,
Which scarcely keeps thee warm. But, for true need—
You heavens, give me that patience, patience I need.
You see me here, you gods, a poor old man,
As full of grief as age, wretched in both.
If it be you that stir these daughters' hearts
Against their father, fool me not so much
To bear it tamely. Touch me with noble anger.
And let not women’s weapons, water drops,
Stain my man’s cheeks! No, you unnatural hags,
I will have such revenges on you both
That all the world shall— I will do such things—
What they are yet I know not, but they shall be
The terrors of the earth. You think I’ll weep?
No, I’ll not weep.

Which scarcely keeps thee warm. But, for true need—
You heavens, give me that patience, patience I need.
You see me here, you gods, a poor old man,
As full of grief as age, wretched in both.
If it be you that stir these daughters' hearts
Against their father, fool me not so much
To bear it tamely. Touch me with noble anger.
And let not women’s weapons, water drops,
Stain my man’s cheeks! No, you unnatural hags,
I will have such revenges on you both
That all the world shall— I will do such things—
What they are yet I know not, but they shall be
The terrors of the earth. You think I’ll weep?
No, I’ll not weep.

Are in the poorest thing superfluous.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Let us withdraw. 'Twill be a storm.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Let us withdraw. 'Twill be a storm.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
This house is little. The old man and his people
Cannot be well bestowed.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
This house is little. The old man and his people
Cannot be well bestowed.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
'Tis his own blame. Hath put himself from rest,
And must needs taste his folly.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
'Tis his own blame. Hath put himself from rest,
And must needs taste his folly.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
For his particular I’ll receive him gladly,
But not one follower.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
So am I purposed.
Where is my lord of Gloucester?

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
So am I purposed.
Where is my lord of Gloucester?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Followed the old man forth. He is returned.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Followed the old man forth. He is returned.
Enter GLOUCESTER

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: The king is in high rage.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Whither is he going?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: He calls to horse, but will I know not whither.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: 'Tis best to give him way. He leads himself.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: 'Tis best to give him way. He leads himself.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
[to GLOUCESTER] My lord, entreat him by no means to
stay.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Alack, the night comes on, and the high winds
Do sorely ruffle. For many miles about
There’s scarce a bush.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:

Do sorely ruffle. For many miles about
There’s scarce a bush.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:

Do sorely ruffle. For many miles about

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
O sir, to wilful men,
The injuries that they themselves procure
Must be their schoolmasters. Shut up your doors.
He is attended with a desperate train.
And what they may incense him to, being apt
To have his ear abused, wisdom bids fear.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Shut up your doors, my lord. 'Tis a wild night.
My Regan counsels well. Come out o' th' storm.

Storm still. Enter KENT disguised and GENTLEMAN, severally
Storm still. Enter KENT disguised and GENTLEMAN, severally

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Who’s there, besides foul weather?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: One minded like the weather, most unquietly.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: I know you. Where’s the king?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: I know you. Where’s the king?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Contending with the fretful elements.
Bids the winds blow the earth into the sea
Or swell the curlèd water 'bove the main,
That things might change or cease. Tears his white
hair,
Which the impetuous blasts, with eyeless rage,
Catch in their fury and make nothing of.
Strives in his little world of man to outscorn
The to-and-fro–conflicting wind and rain.
This night—wherein the cub-drawn bear would couch,
The lion and the belly-pinchèd wolf
Keep their fur dry—unbonneted he runs,
And bids what will take all.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: But who is with him?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
None but the fool, who labors to outjest
His heart-struck injuries.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Sir, I do know you,
And dare upon the warrant of my note
Commend a dear thing to you. There is division,
Although as yet the face of it be covered
With mutual cunning, ’twixt Albany and Cornwall,
Who have—as who have not that their great stars
Throned and set high?—servants, who seem no less,
Which are to France the spies and speculations
Intelligent of our state. What hath been seen,
Either in snuffs and packings of the dukes,
Or the hard rein which both of them hath borne
Against the old kind king, or something deeper,
Whereof perchance these are but furnishings—
But true it is. From France there comes a power
Into this scattered kingdom, who already,
Wise in our negligence, have secret feet
In some of our best ports and are at point
To show their open banner. Now to you.
If on my credit you dare build so far
To make your speed to Dover, you shall find
Some that will thank you, making just report
Of how unnatural and bemadding sorrow
The king hath cause to plain.
I am a gentleman of blood and breeding,
And from some knowledge and assurance offer
This office to you.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: I will talk further with you.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
[giving GENTLEMAN a purse and a ring]
No, do not.
For confirmation that I am much more
Than my outwall, open this purse and take
What it contains. If you shall see Cordelia—
As fear not but you shall—show her this ring.
And she will tell you who that fellow is
That yet you do not know. Fie on this storm!
I will go seek the king.

[giving GENTLEMAN a purse and a ring]
No, do not.
For confirmation that I am much more
Than my outwall, open this purse and take
What it contains. If you shall see Cordelia—
As fear not but you shall—show her this ring.
And she will tell you who that fellow is
That yet you do not know. Fie on this storm!
I will go seek the king.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Give me your hand. Have you no more to say?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Give me your hand. Have you no more to say?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Few words, but to effect more than all yet:
That when we have found the king— in which your pain
That way; I’ll this—he that first lights on him
Holla the other.

sentences.text:

Storm still. Enter LEAR and FOOL
Storm still. Enter LEAR and FOOL

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Blow, winds, and crack your cheeks! Rage, blow!
You cataracts and hurricanoes, spout
Till you have drenched our steeples, drowned the cocks!
You sulfurous and thought-executing fires,
Vaunt-couriers of oak-cleaving thunderbolts,
Singe my white head! And thou, all-shaking thunder,
Smite flat the thick rotundity o' th' world,
Crack nature’s molds, all germens spill at once
That make ingrateful man!

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
O nuncle, court holy water in a dry house is better
than this rainwater out o' door. Good nuncle, in, and
ask thy daughters blessing. Here’s a night pities
neither wise man nor fool.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Rumble thy bellyful! Spit, fire! Spout, rain!
Nor rain, wind, thunder, fire are my daughters.
I tax not you, you elements, with unkindness.
I never gave you kingdom, called you children.
You owe me no subscription. Why then, let fall
Your horrible pleasure. Here I stand, your slave—
A poor, infirm, weak, and despised old man.
But yet I call you servile ministers,
That will with two pernicious daughters joined
Your high engendered battles 'gainst a head
So old and white as this. Oh, ho! 'Tis foul.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
He that has a house to put ’s head in has a good
headpiece.
The codpiece that will house
Before the head has any—
The head and he shall louse.
So beggars marry many.
The man that makes his toe
What he his heart should make
Shall of a corn cry woe,
And turn his sleep to wake.
For there was never yet fair woman but she made mouths
in a glass.

sentences.text:
No, I will be the pattern of all patience.
I will say nothing.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
No, I will be the pattern of all patience.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Who’s there?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Marry, here’s grace and a codpiece—that’s a wise man
and a fool.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
[to LEAR] Alas, sir, are you here? Things that love
night
Love not such nights as these. The wrathful skies
Gallow the very wanderers of the dark
And make them keep their caves. Since I was man,
Such sheets of fire, such bursts of horrid thunder,
Such groans of roaring wind and rain I never
Remember to have heard. Man’s nature cannot carry
Th' affliction nor the fear.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Let the great gods
That keep this dreadful pudder o'er our heads
Find out their enemies now. Tremble, thou wretch
That hast within thee undivulgèd crimes
Unwhipped of justice. Hide thee, thou bloody hand,
Thou perjured, and thou simular man of virtue
That art incestuous. Caitiff, to pieces shake,
That under covert and convenient seeming
Hast practiced on man’s life. Close pent-up guilts,
Rive your concealing continents and cry
These dreadful summoners grace. I am a man
More sinned against than sinning.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Alack, bareheaded?
Gracious my lord, hard by here is a hovel.
Some friendship will it lend you 'gainst the tempest.
Repose you there, while I to this hard house—
More harder than the stones whereof ’tis raised,
Which even but now, demanding after you,
Denied me to come in—return, and force
Their scanted courtesy.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
My wits begin to turn.—
[to FOOL] Come on, my boy. How dost, my boy? Art cold?I
am cold myself.
[to KENT] Where is this straw, my fellow?
The art of our necessities is strange
That can make vile things precious. Come, your hovel.
Poor fool and knave, I have one part in my heart
That’s sorry yet for thee.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
[sings]
He that has and a little tiny wit—
With heigh-ho, the wind and the rain—
Must make content with his fortunes fit,
For the rain it raineth every day.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: True, my good boy.—Come, bring us to this hovel.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: True, my good boy.—Come, bring us to this hovel.
Exeunt LEAR and KENT

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
This is a brave night to cool a courtesan.
I’ll speak a prophecy ere I go.
When priests are more in word than matter,
When brewers mar their malt with water,
When nobles are their tailors' tutors,
No heretics burned but wenches' suitors,
When every case in law is right,
No squire in debt nor no poor knight,
When slanders do not live in tongues,
Nor cutpurses come not to throngs,
When usurers tell their gold i' th' field,
And bawds and whores do churches build—
Then shall the realm of Albion
Come to great confusion.
Then comes the time, who lives to see ’t,
That going shall be used with feet.
This prophecy Merlin shall make, for I live before his
time.

When nobles are their tailors' tutors,
No heretics burned but wenches' suitors,
When every case in law is right,
No squire in debt nor no poor knight,
When slanders do not live in tongues,
Nor cutpurses come not to throngs,
When usurers tell their gold i' th' field,
And bawds and whores do churches build—
Then shall the realm of Albion
Come to great confusion.
Then comes the time, who lives to see ’t,
That going shall be used with feet.
This prophecy Merlin shall make, for I live before his
time.
Exit
Enter GLOUCESTER and EDMUND the bastard, with lights

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Alack, alack, Edmund, I like not this unnatural
dealing. When I desire their leave that I might pity
him, they took from me the use of mine own house,
charged me on pain of their perpetual displeasure
neither to speak of him, entreat for him, nor any way
sustain him.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Most savage and unnatural!

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Go to, say you nothing. There’s a division betwixt the
dukes. And a worse matter than that: I have received a
letter this night. 'Tis dangerous to be spoken. I have
locked the letter in my closet. These injuries the king
now bears will be revenged home. There’s part of a power
already footed. We must incline to the king. I will
look him and privily relieve him. Go you and maintain
talk with the duke, that my charity be not of him
perceived. If he ask for me, I am ill and gone to bed.
Though I die for it—as no less is threatened me—the king
my old master must be relieved. There is some strange
thing toward, Edmund. Pray you, be careful.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
This courtesy, forbid thee, shall the duke
Instantly know, and of that letter too.
This seems a fair deserving, and must draw me
That which my father loses—no less than all.
The younger rises when the old doth fall.

Enter LEAR, KENT disguised, and FOOL

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Here is the place, my lord. Good my lord, enter.
The tyranny of the open night’s too rough
For nature to endure.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Let me alone.
Good my lord, enter here.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Wilt break my heart?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: I had rather break mine own. Good my lord, enter.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: I had rather break mine own. Good my lord, enter.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Thou think’st ’tis much that this contentious storm
Invades us to the skin. So ’tis to thee.
But where the greater malady is fixed
The lesser is scarce felt. Thou’dst shun a bear,
But if thy flight lay toward the raging sea
Thou’dst meet the bear i' th' mouth. When the mind’s
free,
The body’s delicate. The tempest in my mind
Doth from my senses take all feeling else
Save what beats there—filial ingratitude.
Is it not as this mouth should tear this hand
For lifting food to ’t? But I will punish home.
No, I will weep no more. In such a night
To shut me out! Pour on, I will endure.
In such a night as this! O Regan, Goneril,
Your old kind father, whose frank heart gave all—
Oh, that way madness lies. Let me shun that.
No more of that.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Good my lord, enter here.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Prithee, go in thyself. Seek thine own ease.
This tempest will not give me leave to ponder
On things would hurt me more. But I’ll go in.
[to FOOL] In, boy. Go first. You houseless poverty—
Nay, get thee in. I’ll pray, and then I’ll sleep.

Nay, get thee in. I’ll pray, and then I’ll sleep.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Poor naked wretches, whereso'er you are,
That bide the pelting of this pitiless storm,
How shall your houseless heads and unfed sides,
Your looped and windowed raggedness, defend you
From seasons such as these? Oh, I have ta'en
Too little care of this! Take physic, pomp.
Expose thyself to feel what wretches feel,
That thou mayst shake the superflux to them
And show the heavens more just.

Come not in here, nuncle. Here’s a spirit. Help me,
help me!

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Give me thy hand. Who’s there?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Give me thy hand. Who’s there?

sentences.speaker: FOOL

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: A spirit, a spirit. He says his name’s Poor Tom.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
What art thou that dost grumble there i' th' straw?
Come forth.

sentences.speaker: KENT

Come forth.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Away! The foul fiend follows me! Through the sharp
hawthorn blows the cold wind. Hum! Go to thy cold bed
and warm thee.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Didst thou give all to thy two daughters, and art thou
come to this?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Who gives any thing to Poor Tom, whom the foul fiend
hath led through fire and through flame, through ford
and whirlipool, o'er bog and quagmire; that hath laid
knives under his pillow and halters in his pew, set
ratsbane by his porridge, made him proud of heart to
ride on a bay trotting-horse over four-inched bridges to
course his own shadow for a traitor? Bless thy five
wits. Tom’s a-cold. Oh, do-de, do-de, do-de. Bless thee
from whirlwinds, star-blasting, and taking! Do Poor Tom
some charity, whom the foul fiend vexes. There could I
have him now—and there—and there again—and there.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
What, has his daughters brought him to this pass?—
Couldst thou save nothing? Wouldst thou give 'em all?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
What, has his daughters brought him to this pass?—
Couldst thou save nothing? Wouldst thou give 'em all?

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Nay, he reserved a blanket, else we had been all
shamed.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Now all the plagues that in the pendulous air
Hang fated o'er men’s faults light on thy daughters!

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: He hath no daughters, sir.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Death, traitor! Nothing could have subdued nature
To such a lowness but his unkind daughters.
Is it the fashion that discarded fathers
Should have thus little mercy on their flesh?
Judicious punishment! 'Twas this flesh begot
Those pelican daughters.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: Pillicock sat on Pillicock hill. Alow, alow, loo, loo!

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: Pillicock sat on Pillicock hill. Alow, alow, loo, loo!

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: This cold night will turn us all to fools and madmen.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Take heed o' th' foul fiend. Obey thy parents, keep thy
word’s justice, swear not, commit not with man’s sworn
spouse, set not thy sweet heart on proud array. Tom’s a-
cold.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: What hast thou been?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
A servingman, proud in heart and mind, that curled my
hair, wore gloves in my cap, served the lust of my
mistress' heart and did the act of darkness with her,
swore as many oaths as I spake words and broke them in
the sweet face of heaven—one that slept in the
contriving of lust and waked to do it. Wine loved I
deeply, dice dearly, and in woman outparamoured the
Turk. False of heart, light of ear, bloody of hand—hog
in sloth, fox in stealth, wolf in greediness, dog in
madness, lion in prey. Let not the creaking of shoes nor
the rustling of silks betray thy poor heart to woman.
Keep thy foot out of brothels, thy hand out of plackets,
thy pen from lenders' books, and defy the foul fiend.
Still through the hawthorn blows the cold wind, says,
“Suum, mun, nonny.” Dauphin my boy, my boy, cessez. Let
him trot by.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Why, thou wert better in thy grave than to answer with
thy uncovered body this extremity of the skies.—Is man
no more than this? Consider him well.— Thou owest the
worm no silk, the beast no hide, the sheep no wool, the
cat no perfume. Ha! Here’s three on ’s are
sophisticated. Thou art the thing itself. Unaccommodated
man is no more but such a poor, bare, forked animal as
thou art.—Off, off, you lendings! Come. Unbutton here.
[tears at his clothes]

thy uncovered body this extremity of the skies.—Is man

worm no silk, the beast no hide, the sheep no wool, the
cat no perfume. Ha! Here’s three on ’s are
sophisticated. Thou art the thing itself. Unaccommodated
man is no more but such a poor, bare, forked animal as
thou art.—Off, off, you lendings! Come. Unbutton here.
[tears at his clothes]

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Prithee, nuncle, be contented. 'Tis a naughty night to
swim in. Now a little fire in a wild field were like an
old lecher’s heart—a small spark, all the rest on ’s
body cold. Look, here comes a walking fire.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
This is the foul fiend Flibbertigibbet. He begins at
curfew and walks till the first cock. He gives the web
and the pin, squints the eye and makes the harelip,
mildews the white wheat and hurts the poor creature of
earth.
St. Withold footed thrice the 'old.
He met the nightmare and her ninefold,
Bid her alight,
And her troth plight.
And aroint thee, witch, aroint thee!

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: How fares your grace?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: [indicating GLOUCESTER] What’s he?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Who’s there? What is ’t you seek?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Who’s there? What is ’t you seek?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: What are you there? Your names?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: What are you there? Your names?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Poor Tom, that eats the swimming frog, the toad, the
tadpole, the wall newt, and the water; that in the fury
of his heart, when the foul fiend rages, eats cow dung
for salads, swallows the old rat and the ditch-dog,
drinks the green mantle of the standing pool; who is
whipped from tithing to tithing and stocked, punished
and imprisoned; who hath had three suits to his back,
six shirts to his body,
Horse to ride and weapon to wear.
But mice and rats and such small deer
Have been Tom’s food for seven long year.
Beware my follower. Peace, Smulkin. Peace, thou fiend!

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: [to LEAR] What, hath your grace no better company?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
The Prince of Darkness is a gentleman. Modo he’s
called, and Mahu.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
The Prince of Darkness is a gentleman. Modo he’s
called, and Mahu.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
[To LEAR] Our flesh and blood, my lord, is grown so
vile
That it doth hate what gets it.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: Poor Tom’s a-cold.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Go in with me. My duty cannot suffer
To obey in all your daughters' hard commands.
Though their injunction be to bar my doors
And let this tyrannous night take hold upon you,
Yet have I ventured to come seek you out
And bring you where both fire and food is ready.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
First let me talk with this philosopher.—
[to EDGAR] What is the cause of thunder?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
[to LEAR] Good my lord, take his offer. Go into the
house.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
I’ll talk a word with this same learnèd Theban.—
What is your study?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: How to prevent the fiend and to kill vermin.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Let me ask you one word in private.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Let me ask you one word in private.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
[aside to GLOUCESTER] Importune him once more to go, my
lord.
His wits begin t' unsettle.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
[aside to GLOUCESTER] Importune him once more to go, my

His wits begin t' unsettle.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Canst thou blame him?

sentences.speaker: LEAR

O, cry your mercy, sir.—
[to EDGAR] Noble philosopher, your company.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: Tom’s a-cold.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: In, fellow. There, into th' hovel. Keep thee warm.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: In, fellow. There, into th' hovel. Keep thee warm.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: In, fellow. There, into th' hovel. Keep thee warm.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Come let’s in all.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: This way, my lord.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
[indicating EDGAR]
With him!
I will keep still with my philosopher.

[indicating EDGAR]
With him!
I will keep still with my philosopher.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
[to GLOUCESTER] Good my lord, soothe him. Let him take
the fellow.

[to GLOUCESTER] Good my lord, soothe him. Let him take

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Take him you on.

sentences.speaker: KENT

sentences.speaker: KENT

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Come, good Athenian.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: No words, no words. Hush.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: No words, no words. Hush.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Child Roland to the dark tower came,
His word was still “Fie, foh, and fum,
I smell the blood of a British man.”

sentences.speaker: EDGAR

Child Roland to the dark tower came,
His word was still “Fie, foh, and fum,
I smell the blood of a British man.”
Exeunt
Enter CORNWALL and EDMUND

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: I will have my revenge ere I depart his house.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
How, my lord, I may be censured, that nature thus gives
way to loyalty, something fears me to think of.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
I now perceive it was not altogether your brother’s
evil disposition made him seek his death, but a
provoking merit set awork by a reprovable badness in
himself.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
I now perceive it was not altogether your brother’s
evil disposition made him seek his death, but a
provoking merit set awork by a reprovable badness in
himself.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
How malicious is my fortune, that I must repent to be
just! [giving CORNWALL a letter] This is the letter
which he spoke of, which approves him an intelligent
party to the advantages of France. O heavens, that this
treason were not, or not I the detector!

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
How malicious is my fortune, that I must repent to be
just! [giving CORNWALL a letter] This is the letter
which he spoke of, which approves him an intelligent
party to the advantages of France. O heavens, that this
treason were not, or not I the detector!

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Go with me to the duchess.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
If the matter of this paper be certain, you have mighty
business in hand.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
True or false, it hath made thee Earl of Gloucester.
Seek out where thy father is, that he may be ready for
our apprehension.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
True or false, it hath made thee Earl of Gloucester.
Seek out where thy father is, that he may be ready for
our apprehension.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
[aside] If I find him comforting the king, it will
stuff his suspicion more fully. [to CORNWALL] I will
persevere in my course of loyalty, though the conflict
be sore between that and my blood.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
I will lay trust upon thee, and thou shalt find a
dearer father in my love.
Exeunt
Enter GLOUCESTER, LEAR, KENT disguised, FOOL, and EDGAR disguised

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Here is better than the open air. Take it thankfully. I
will piece out the comfort with what addition I can. I
will not be long from you.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:

will piece out the comfort with what addition I can. I
will not be long from you.
