sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Here is better than the open air. Take it thankfully. I
will piece out the comfort with what addition I can. I
will not be long from you.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
All the power of his wits have given way to his
impatience.
The gods reward your kindness!

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
All the power of his wits have given way to his
impatience.
The gods reward your kindness!
Exit GLOUCESTER

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Frateretto calls me and tells me Nero is an angler in
the lake of darkness. Pray, innocent, and beware the
foul fiend.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Frateretto calls me and tells me Nero is an angler in
the lake of darkness. Pray, innocent, and beware the
foul fiend.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Frateretto calls me and tells me Nero is an angler in
the lake of darkness. Pray, innocent, and beware the
foul fiend.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
Prithee, nuncle, tell me whether a madman be a
gentleman or a yeoman?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: A king, a king!

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
No, he’s a yeoman that has a gentleman to his son, for
he’s a mad yeoman that sees his son a gentleman before
him.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
To have a thousand with red burning spits
Come hissing in upon 'em!

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: The foul fiend bites my back.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
He’s mad that trusts in the tameness of a wolf, a
horse’s health, a boy’s love, or a whore’s oath.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
It shall be done. I will arraign them straight.
[to EDGAR] Come, sit thou here, most learnèd justicer.
[to FOOL] Thou, sapient sir, sit here.—Now, you
she-foxes—

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
It shall be done. I will arraign them straight.
[to EDGAR] Come, sit thou here, most learnèd justicer.
[to FOOL] Thou, sapient sir, sit here.—Now, you
she-foxes—

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
It shall be done. I will arraign them straight.
[to EDGAR] Come, sit thou here, most learnèd justicer.
[to FOOL] Thou, sapient sir, sit here.—Now, you
she-foxes—

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Look, where he stands and glares!—Want’st thou eyes at
trial, madam?
[sings]
Come o'er the bourn, Bessy, to me—

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Look, where he stands and glares!—Want’st thou eyes at
trial, madam?
[sings]
Come o'er the bourn, Bessy, to me—

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text:
[sings]
Her boat hath a leak,
And she must not speak
Why she dares not come over to thee.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
The foul fiend haunts Poor Tom in the voice of a
nightingale. Hoppedance cries in Tom’s belly for two
white herring. Croak not, black angel. I have no food
for thee.

sentences.text:

Will you lie down and rest upon the cushions?

[to FOOL] And thou, his yoke-fellow of equity,
Bench by his side. [to KENT ] You are o' th'
commission.
Sit you too.

sentences.text:
Let us deal justly.
[ sings]
Sleepest or wakest thou, jolly shepherd?
Thy sheep be in the corn.
And for one blast of thy minikin mouth,
Thy sheep shall take no harm.
Purr! The cat is gray.

Arraign her first. 'Tis Goneril. I here take my oath
before this honorable assembly, she kicked the poor king
her father.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: Come hither, mistress. Is your name Goneril?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: She cannot deny it.

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: Cry you mercy, I took you for a joint-stool.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
And here’s another, whose warped looks proclaim
What store her heart is made on. Stop her there!
Arms, arms, sword, fire, corruption in the place!
False justicer, why hast thou let her ’scape?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
[to LEAR] O pity! Sir, where is the patience now,
That thou so oft have boasted to retain?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside] My tears begin to take his part so much,
They’ll mar my counterfeiting.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
The little dogs and all,
Tray, Blanch, and Sweetheart—see, they bark at me.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Tom will throw his head at them.—Avaunt, you curs!
Be thy mouth or black or white,
Tooth that poisons if it bite,
Mastiff, greyhound, mongrel grim,
Hound or spaniel, brach or him,
Bobtail tyke or trundle-tail—
Tom will make them weep and wail.
For with throwing thus my head,
Dogs leap the hatch, and all are fled.
Do-de, de-de. Cessez! Come, march to wakes and fairs
and market towns. Poor Tom, thy horn is dry.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Then let them anatomize Regan. See what breeds about
her heart. Is there any cause in nature that makes these
hard hearts? [to EDGAR] You, sir, I entertain you for
one of my hundred. Only I do not like the fashion of
your garments. You will say they are Persian attire, but
let them be changed.

Make no noise, make no noise. Draw the curtains—so, so,
so. We’ll go to supper i' th' morning. So, so, so.
[sleeps]

sentences.speaker: FOOL
sentences.text: And I’ll go to bed at noon.
Enter GLOUCESTER

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
[to KENT] Come hither, friend. Where is the king my
master?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
[to KENT] Come hither, friend. Where is the king my
master?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Here, sir, but trouble him not. His wits are gone.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Here, sir, but trouble him not. His wits are gone.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Good friend, I prithee, take him in thy arms.
I have o'erheard a plot of death upon him.
There is a litter ready. Lay him in ’t
And drive towards Dover, friend, where thou shalt meet
Both welcome and protection. Take up thy master.
If thou shouldst dally half an hour, his life,
With thine and all that offer to defend him,
Stand in assurèd loss. Take up, take up,
And follow me, that will to some provision
Give thee quick conduct.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Oppressèd nature sleeps.—
This rest might yet have balmed thy broken sinews,
Which, if convenience will not allow,
Stand in hard cure.
[to FOOL]
Come, help to bear thy master.
Thou must not stay behind.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Come, come, away.
Exeunt all but EDGAR

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
When we our betters see bearing our woes,
We scarcely think our miseries our foes.
Who alone suffers, suffers most i' th' mind,
Leaving free things and happy shows behind.
But then the mind much sufferance doth o'erskip
When grief hath mates and bearing fellowship.
How light and portable my pain seems now
When that which makes me bend makes the king bow.
He childed as I fathered. Tom, away!
Mark the high noises and thyself bewray
When false opinion, whose wrong thought defiles thee,
In thy just proof repeals and reconciles thee.
What will hap more tonight, safe ’scape the king!
Lurk, lurk.

Enter CORNWALL, and REGAN, and GONERIL, and EDMUND the bastard, and servants

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
[to GONERIL] Post speedily to my lord your husband.
Show him this letter. The army of France is landed.
—Seek out the traitor Gloucester.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
[to GONERIL] Post speedily to my lord your husband.
Show him this letter. The army of France is landed.
—Seek out the traitor Gloucester.

sentences.text:
[to GONERIL] Post speedily to my lord your husband.
Show him this letter. The army of France is landed.
—Seek out the traitor Gloucester.
Exeunt some servants

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Hang him instantly.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: Pluck out his eyes.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Leave him to my displeasure.— Edmund, keep you our
sister company. The revenges we are bound to take upon
your traitorous father are not fit for your beholding.
Advise the duke where you are going, to a most festinate
preparation. We are bound to the like. Our posts shall
be swift and intelligent betwixt us.— Farewell, dear
sister. [to EDMUND] Farewell, my lord of Gloucester.

sister company. The revenges we are bound to take upon
your traitorous father are not fit for your beholding.
Advise the duke where you are going, to a most festinate
preparation. We are bound to the like. Our posts shall
be swift and intelligent betwixt us.— Farewell, dear
sister. [to EDMUND] Farewell, my lord of Gloucester.

Enter OSWALD the steward

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: How now? Where’s the king?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: How now? Where’s the king?

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
My lord of Gloucester hath conveyed him hence.
Some five or six and thirty of his knights,
Hot questrists after him, met him at gate,
Who with some other of the lord’s dependants
Are gone with him towards Dover, where they boast
To have well-armèd friends.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Get horses for your mistress.
Exit OSWALD

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: Farewell, sweet lord, and sister.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Edmund, farewell.
Exeunt GONERIL and EDMUND the bastard

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Go seek the traitor Gloucester.
Pinion him like a thief, bring him before us.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Go seek the traitor Gloucester.
Pinion him like a thief, bring him before us.
Exeunt some servants

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Though well we may not pass upon his life
Without the form of justice, yet our power
Shall do a courtesy to our wrath, which men
May blame, but not control.—Who’s there? The traitor?

Enter GLOUCESTER, brought in by two or three servants

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Ingrateful fox, ’tis he.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Ingrateful fox, ’tis he.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Bind fast his corky arms.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
What mean your graces? Good my friends, consider
You are my guests. Do me no foul play, friends.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
What mean your graces? Good my friends, consider
You are my guests. Do me no foul play, friends.

You are my guests. Do me no foul play, friends.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Bind him, I say.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Bind him, I say.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Hard, hard. O filthy traitor!

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Hard, hard. O filthy traitor!

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Unmerciful lady as you are, I’m none.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: To this chair bind him. Villain, thou shalt find—

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: To this chair bind him. Villain, thou shalt find—

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
By the kind gods, ’tis most ignobly done
To pluck me by the beard.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: So white, and such a traitor?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Naughty lady,
These hairs which thou dost ravish from my chin
Will quicken and accuse thee. I am your host.
With robbers' hands my hospitable favors
You should not ruffle thus. What will you do?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Come, sir, what letters had you late from France?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Be simple-answered, for we know the truth.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
And what confederacy have you with the traitors
Late footed in the kingdom?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
To whose hands
You have sent the lunatic king. Speak.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
To whose hands
You have sent the lunatic king. Speak.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
I have a letter guessingly set down,
Which came from one that’s of a neutral heart,
And not from one opposed.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
I have a letter guessingly set down,
Which came from one that’s of a neutral heart,
And not from one opposed.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Cunning.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: And false.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Where hast thou sent the king?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: To Dover.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Wherefore to Dover? Wast thou not charged at peril—

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Wherefore to Dover? Wast thou not charged at peril—

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: Wherefore to Dover?—Let him first answer that.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: I am tied to th' stake, and I must stand the course.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Wherefore to Dover, sir?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Because I would not see thy cruèl nails
Pluck out his poor old eyes, nor thy fierce sister
In his anointed flesh stick boarish fangs.
The sea, with such a storm as his bare head
In hell-black night endured, would have buoyed up,
And quenched the stellèd fires.
Yet poor old heart, he holp the heavens to rain.
If wolves had at thy gate howled that stern time,
Thou shouldst have said, “Good porter, turn the key,”
All cruèls else subscribed. But I shall see
The wingèd vengeance overtake such children.

“See” ’t shalt thou never.—Fellows, hold the chair.—
Upon these eyes of thine I’ll set my foot.

He that will think to live till he be old,
Give me some help!

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: One side will mock another—th' other too.

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: If you see vengeance—

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
Hold your hand, my lord!
I have served you ever since I was a child.
But better service have I never done you
Than now to bid you hold.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: How now, you dog?

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
If you did wear a beard upon your chin,
I’d shake it on this quarrel.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: What do you mean?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text: My villain!

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text: Nay then, come on, and take the chance of anger.
FIRST SERVANT and CORNWALL draw and fight. CORNWALL is wounded
FIRST SERVANT and CORNWALL draw and fight. CORNWALL is wounded

[to another servant]
Give me thy sword.—A peasant stand up thus?
[takes a sword, runs at FIRST SERVANT behind, and kills
him]

[takes a sword, runs at FIRST SERVANT behind, and kills

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
Oh, I am slain!—My lord, you have one eye left
To see some mischief on him. Oh!
[dies]

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Lest it see more, prevent it. Out, vile jelly!
[plucks out GLOUCESTER’s other eye]
Where is thy luster now?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
Lest it see more, prevent it. Out, vile jelly!
[plucks out GLOUCESTER’s other eye]
Where is thy luster now?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
All dark and comfortless. Where’s my son Edmund?
Edmund, enkindle all the sparks of nature
To quit this horrid act.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Out, treacherous villain!
Thou call’st on him that hates thee. It was he
That made the overture of thy treasons to us,
Who is too good to pity thee.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
O my follies! Then Edgar was abused.
Kind gods, forgive me that, and prosper him!

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Go thrust him out at gates, and let him smell
His way to Dover.
Exeunt some servants with GLOUCESTER

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: [to CORNWALL] How is ’t, my lord? How look you?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: [to CORNWALL] How is ’t, my lord? How look you?

sentences.speaker: CORNWALL
sentences.text:
I have received a hurt. Follow me, lady.—
Turn out that eyeless villain. Throw this slave
Upon the dunghill.— Regan, I bleed apace.
Untimely comes this hurt. Give me your arm.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
sentences.text:
I’ll never care what wickedness I do,
If this man come to good.

sentences.speaker: THIRD SERVANT
sentences.text:
If she live long,
And in the end meet the old course of death,
Women will all turn monsters.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
sentences.text:
Let’s follow the old earl, and get the Bedlam
To lead him where he would. His roguish madness
Allows itself to any thing.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
sentences.text:

To lead him where he would. His roguish madness
Allows itself to any thing.

sentences.speaker: THIRD SERVANT
sentences.text:
Go thou. I’ll fetch some flax and whites of eggs
To apply to his bleeding face. Now heaven help him!

sentences.speaker: THIRD SERVANT
sentences.text:

To apply to his bleeding face. Now heaven help him!

sentences.speaker: THIRD SERVANT

Exeunt severally
Enter EDGAR diguised

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Yet better thus, and known to be contemned,
Than still contemned and flattered. To be worst,
The lowest and most dejected thing of fortune
Stands still in esperance, lives not in fear.
The lamentable change is from the best;
The worst returns to laughter. Welcome, then,
Thou unsubstantial air that I embrace!
The wretch that thou hast blown unto the worst
Owes nothing to thy blasts.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
But who comes here?
My father, poorly led? World, world, O world!
But that thy strange mutations make us hate thee,
Life would not yield to age.

sentences.speaker: OLD MAN
sentences.text:
[to GLOUCESTER] O my good lord,
I have been your tenant and your father’s tenant these
fourscore years.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Away, get thee away. Good friend, be gone.
Thy comforts can do me no good at all.
Thee they may hurt.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Away, get thee away. Good friend, be gone.
Thy comforts can do me no good at all.
Thee they may hurt.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Away, get thee away. Good friend, be gone.
Thy comforts can do me no good at all.
Thee they may hurt.

sentences.speaker: OLD MAN
sentences.text: Alack, sir, you cannot see your way.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
I have no way, and therefore want no eyes.
I stumbled when I saw. Full oft ’tis seen,
Our means secure us and our mere defects
Prove our commodities. O dear son Edgar,
The food of thy abusèd father’s wrath,
Might I but live to see thee in my touch,
I’d say I had eyes again!

sentences.speaker: OLD MAN
sentences.text: How now? Who’s there?

sentences.speaker: OLD MAN
sentences.text: How now? Who’s there?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside] O gods! Who is ’t can say “I am at the worst?”
I am worse than e'er I was.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside] O gods! Who is ’t can say “I am at the worst?”
I am worse than e'er I was.

sentences.speaker: OLD MAN
sentences.text:
[to GLOUCESTER]
'Tis poor mad Tom.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside] And worse I may be yet. The worst is not
So long as we can say “This is the worst.”

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside] And worse I may be yet. The worst is not
So long as we can say “This is the worst.”

sentences.speaker: OLD MAN
sentences.text: [to EDGAR] Fellow, where goest?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Is it a beggarman?

sentences.speaker: OLD MAN
sentences.text: Madman and beggar too.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
He has some reason, else he could not beg.
I' th' last night’s storm I such a fellow saw,
Which made me think a man a worm. My son
Came then into my mind, and yet my mind
Was then scarce friends with him. I have heard more
since.
As flies to wanton boys are we to th' gods.
They kill us for their sport.

He has some reason, else he could not beg.
I' th' last night’s storm I such a fellow saw,
Which made me think a man a worm. My son
Came then into my mind, and yet my mind
Was then scarce friends with him. I have heard more
since.
As flies to wanton boys are we to th' gods.
They kill us for their sport.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside]
How should this be?
Bad is the trade that must play fool to sorrow,
Angering itself and others.—Bless thee, master!

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Is that the naked fellow?

sentences.speaker: OLD MAN
sentences.text: Ay, my lord.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Then prithee, get thee gone. If for my sake
Thou wilt o'ertake us hence a mile or twain
I' th' way toward Dover, do it for ancient love.
And bring some covering for this naked soul,
Which I’ll entreat to lead me.

sentences.speaker: OLD MAN
sentences.text: Alack, sir, he is mad.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
'Tis the time’s plague when madmen lead the blind.
Do as I bid thee. Or rather, do thy pleasure.
Above the rest, be gone.

sentences.speaker: OLD MAN
sentences.text:
I’ll bring him the best 'parel that I have,
Come on ’t what will.
Exit OLD MAN

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Sirrah, naked fellow—

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Poor Tom’s a-cold.
[aside] I cannot daub it further.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Come hither, fellow.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside] And yet I must.—Bless thy sweet eyes, they
bleed.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside] And yet I must.—Bless thy sweet eyes, they
bleed.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Know’st thou the way to Dover?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Both stile and gate, horseway and footpath. Poor Tom
hath been scared out of his good wits. Bless thee,
goodman’s son, from the foul fiend! Five fiends have
been in poor Tom at once: of lust, as Obidicut;
Hobbididence, prince of dumbness; Mahu, of stealing;
Modo, of murder; Flibbertigibbet, of mopping and mowing,
who since possesses chambermaids and waiting-women. So
bless thee, master.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
[Giving EDGAR a purse]
Here, take this purse, thou whom the heavens' plagues
Have humbled to all strokes. That I am wretched
Makes thee the happier. Heavens, deal so still.
Let the superfluous and lust-dieted man,
That slaves your ordinance, that will not see
Because he doth not feel, feel your power quickly.
So distribution should undo excess,
And each man have enough. Dost thou know Dover?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: Ay, master.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
There is a cliff, whose high and bending head
Looks fearfully in the confinèd deep.
Bring me but to the very brim of it,
And I’ll repair the misery thou dost bear
With something rich about me. From that place
I shall no leading need.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Give me thy arm.
Poor Tom shall lead thee.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Give me thy arm.
Poor Tom shall lead thee.
Exeunt
Enter GONERIL and EDMUND the bastard

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Welcome, my lord. I marvel our mild husband
Not met us on the way.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Welcome, my lord. I marvel our mild husband
Not met us on the way.
Enter OSWALD

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: Now, where’s your master?

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
Madam, within—but never man so changed.
I told him of the army that was landed.
He smiled at it. I told him you were coming.
His answer was “The worse.” Of Gloucester’s treachery
And of the loyal service of his son,
When I informed him, then he called me “sot,”
And told me I had turned the wrong side out.
What most he should dislike seems pleasant to him;
What like, offensive.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
[to EDMUND ]
Then shall you go no further.
It is the cowish terror of his spirit
That dares not undertake. He’ll not feel wrongs
Which tie him to an answer. Our wishes on the way
May prove effects. Back, Edmund, to my brother.
Hasten his musters and conduct his powers.
I must change names at home, and give the distaff
Into my husband’s hands. This trusty servant
Shall pass between us. Ere long you are like to hear—
If you dare venture in your own behalf—
A mistress’s command. Wear this. Spare speech.
Decline your head. This kiss, if it durst speak,
Would stretch thy spirits up into the air.
[kisses EDMUND] Conceive, and fare thee well.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Yours in the ranks of death.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: My most dear Gloucester!
Exit EDMUND

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Oh, the difference of man and man!
To thee a woman’s services are due.
My fool usurps my body.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Madam, here comes my lord.
Exit OSWALD
Enter ALBANY

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: I have been worth the whistle.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
O Goneril,
You are not worth the dust which the rude wind
Blows in your face. I fear your disposition.
That nature, which contemns its origin
Cannot be bordered certain in itself.
She that herself will sliver and disbranch
From her material sap perforce must wither
And come to deadly use.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: No more. The text is foolish.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: No more. The text is foolish.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
Wisdom and goodness to the vile seem vile.
Filths savor but themselves. What have you done?
Tigers, not daughters, what have you performed?
A father, and a gracious agèd man,
Whose reverence even the head-lugged bear would lick,
Most barbarous, most degenerate, have you madded.
Could my good brother suffer you to do it—
A man, a prince by him so benefited?
If that the heavens do not their visible spirits
Send quickly down to tame these vile offenses,
It will come:
Humanity must perforce prey on itself
Like monsters of the deep.

Tigers, not daughters, what have you performed?
A father, and a gracious agèd man,
Whose reverence even the head-lugged bear would lick,
Most barbarous, most degenerate, have you madded.
Could my good brother suffer you to do it—
A man, a prince by him so benefited?
If that the heavens do not their visible spirits
Send quickly down to tame these vile offenses,
It will come:
Humanity must perforce prey on itself
Like monsters of the deep.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Milk-livered man
That bear’st a cheek for blows, a head for wrongs—
Who hast not in thy brows an eye discerning
Thine honor from thy suffering; that not know’st
Fools do those villains pity who are punished
Ere they have done their mischief. Where’s thy drum?
France spreads his banners in our noiseless land,
With plumèd helm thy state begins to threat,
Whiles thou, a moral fool, sits still and cries,
“Alack, why does he so?”

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
See thyself, devil!
Proper deformity shows not in the fiend
So horrid as in woman.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
See thyself, devil!
Proper deformity shows not in the fiend
So horrid as in woman.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: O vain fool!

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
Thou changèd and self-covered thing, for shame!
Bemonster not thy feature. Were ’t my fitness
To let these hands obey my blood,
They are apt enough to dislocate and tear
Thy flesh and bones. Howe'er thou art a fiend,
A woman’s shape doth shield thee.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: Marry, your manhood, mew!
Enter FIRST MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: What news?

sentences.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
sentences.text:
O my good lord, the Duke of Cornwall’s dead,
Slain by his servant, going to put out
The other eye of Gloucester.

sentences.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
sentences.text:
O my good lord, the Duke of Cornwall’s dead,
Slain by his servant, going to put out
The other eye of Gloucester.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: Gloucester’s eyes?

sentences.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
sentences.text:
A servant that he bred, thrilled with remorse,
Opposed against the act, bending his sword
To his great master; who thereat enraged
Flew on him and amongst them felled him dead—
But not without that harmful stroke, which since
Hath plucked him after.

sentences.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
sentences.text:

To his great master; who thereat enraged
Flew on him and amongst them felled him dead—
But not without that harmful stroke, which since
Hath plucked him after.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
This shows you are above,
You justicers, that these our nether crimes
So speedily can venge! But oh, poor Gloucester—
Lost he his other eye?

sentences.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Both, both, my lord.—
This letter, madam, craves a speedy answer.
'Tis from your sister.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
[aside]
One way I like this well.
But being widow, and my Gloucester with her,
May all the building in my fancy pluck
Upon my hateful life. Another way
The news is not so tart.— I’ll read and answer.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: Where was his son when they did take his eyes?

sentences.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
sentences.text: Come with my lady hither.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: He is not here.

sentences.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
sentences.text: No, my good lord. I met him back again.

sentences.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
sentences.text: No, my good lord. I met him back again.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: Knows he the wickedness?

sentences.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Ay, my good lord. 'Twas he informed against him,
And quit the house on purpose that their punishment
Might have the freer course.

sentences.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Ay, my good lord. 'Twas he informed against him,
And quit the house on purpose that their punishment
Might have the freer course.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
Gloucester, I live
To thank thee for the love thou showed’st the king,
And to revenge thine eyes.— Come hither, friend.
Tell me what more thou know’st.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:

Tell me what more thou know’st.
Exeunt
Enter KENT disguised and GENTLEMAN

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Why the King of France is so suddenly gone back know
you the reason?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Something he left imperfect in the state which, since
his coming forth, is thought of; which imports to the
kingdom so much fear and danger that his personal return
was most required and necessary.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Something he left imperfect in the state which, since
his coming forth, is thought of; which imports to the
kingdom so much fear and danger that his personal return
was most required and necessary.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Who hath he left behind him general?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: The Marshal of France, Monsieur la Far.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Did your letters pierce the queen to any demonstration
of grief?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Ay, sir. She took them, read them in my presence,
And now and then an ample tear trilled down
Her delicate cheek. It seemed she was a queen
Over her passion, who, most rebel-like,
Sought to be king o'er her.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Ay, sir. She took them, read them in my presence,
And now and then an ample tear trilled down
Her delicate cheek. It seemed she was a queen
Over her passion, who, most rebel-like,
Sought to be king o'er her.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Ay, sir. She took them, read them in my presence,
And now and then an ample tear trilled down
Her delicate cheek. It seemed she was a queen
Over her passion, who, most rebel-like,
Sought to be king o'er her.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: O, then it moved her?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Not to a rage. Patience and sorrow strove
Who should express her goodliest. You have seen
Sunshine and rain at once—her smiles and tears
Were like a better way. Those happy smilets
That played on her ripe lip seemed not to know
What guests were in her eyes, which parted thence
As pearls from diamonds dropped. In brief,
Sorrow would be a rarity most beloved
If all could so become it.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Made she no verbal question?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Faith, once or twice she heaved the name of “father”
Pantingly forth as if it pressed her heart,
Cried, “Sisters, sisters! Shame of ladies, sisters!
Kent, father, sisters! What, i' th' storm, i' th'
night?
Let pity not be believed.” There she shook
The holy water from her heavenly eyes,
And clamor moistened. Then away she started
To deal with grief alone.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
It is the stars,
The stars above us, govern our conditions.
Else one self mate and mate could not beget
Such different issues. You spoke not with her since?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
It is the stars,
The stars above us, govern our conditions.
Else one self mate and mate could not beget
Such different issues. You spoke not with her since?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
It is the stars,
The stars above us, govern our conditions.
Else one self mate and mate could not beget
Such different issues. You spoke not with her since?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: No.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Was this before the king returned?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: No, since.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Well, sir, the poor distressèd Lear’s i' th' town,
Who sometime in his better tune remembers
What we are come about, and by no means
Will yield to see his daughter.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Well, sir, the poor distressèd Lear’s i' th' town,
Who sometime in his better tune remembers
What we are come about, and by no means
Will yield to see his daughter.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Why, good sir?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
A sovereign shame so elbows him. His own unkindness
That stripped her from his benediction turned her
To foreign casualties, gave her dear rights
To his dog-hearted daughters. These things sting
His mind so venomously that burning shame
Detains him from Cordelia.

His mind so venomously that burning shame
Detains him from Cordelia.

Detains him from Cordelia.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Alack, poor gentleman!

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Of Albany’s and Cornwall’s powers you heard not?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: 'Tis so. They are afoot.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: 'Tis so. They are afoot.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Well, sir, I’ll bring you to our master Lear
And leave you to attend him. Some dear cause
Will in concealment wrap me up awhile.
When I am known aright you shall not grieve
Lending me this acquaintance. I pray you, go
Along with me.

Enter, with drum and colors, CORDELIA, DOCTOR, and soldiers

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
Alack, ’tis he. Why, he was met even now
As mad as the vexed sea, singing aloud,
Crowned with rank fumiter and furrow-weeds,
With burdocks, hemlock, nettles, cuckoo-flowers,
Darnel, and all the idle weeds that grow
In our sustaining corn.— A century send forth.
Search every acre in the high-grown field,
And bring him to our eye.

sentences.text:

Exit some soldiers

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
What can man’s wisdom
In the restoring his bereavèd sense?
He that helps him take all my outward worth.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
What can man’s wisdom
In the restoring his bereavèd sense?
He that helps him take all my outward worth.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text:
There is means, madam.
Our foster nurse of nature is repose,
The which he lacks— that to provoke in him
Are many simples operative, whose power
Will close the eye of anguish.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text:

Will close the eye of anguish.

Are many simples operative, whose power
Will close the eye of anguish.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
All blessed secrets,
All you unpublished virtues of the earth,
Spring with my tears. Be aidant and remediate
In the good man’s distress. Seek, seek for him,
Lest his ungoverned rage dissolve the life
That wants the means to lead it.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA

Enter SECOND MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: SECOND MESSENGER
sentences.text:
News, madam.
The British powers are marching hitherward.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MESSENGER
sentences.text:
News, madam.
The British powers are marching hitherward.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
'Tis known before. Our preparation stands
In expectation of them. O dear father,
It is thy business that I go about.
Therefore great France
My mourning and importuned tears hath pitied.
No blown ambition doth our arms incite,
But love—dear love!—and our aged father’s right.
Soon may I hear and see him.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
'Tis known before. Our preparation stands
In expectation of them. O dear father,
It is thy business that I go about.
Therefore great France
My mourning and importuned tears hath pitied.
No blown ambition doth our arms incite,
But love—dear love!—and our aged father’s right.
Soon may I hear and see him.

Enter REGAN and the steward OSWALD

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: But are my brother’s powers set forth?

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Ay, madam.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Himself in person there?

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
Madam, with much ado.
Your sister is the better soldier.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
Madam, with much ado.
Your sister is the better soldier.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Lord Edmund spake not with your lord at home?

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: No, madam.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: What might import my sister’s letter to him?

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: I know not, lady.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Faith, he is posted hence on serious matter.
It was great ignorance, Gloucester’s eyes being out,
To let him live. Where he arrives he moves
All hearts against us. Edmund I think is gone
In pity of his misery to dispatch
His nighted life; moreover to descry
The strength o' th' enemy.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: I must needs after him, madam, with my letter.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Our troops set forth tomorrow. Stay with us.
The ways are dangerous.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Our troops set forth tomorrow. Stay with us.
The ways are dangerous.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Our troops set forth tomorrow. Stay with us.
The ways are dangerous.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
I may not, madam.
My lady charged my duty in this business.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
I may not, madam.
My lady charged my duty in this business.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Why should she write to Edmund? Might not you
Transport her purposes by word? Belike
Some things—I know not what. I’ll love thee much.
Let me unseal the letter.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:

Some things—I know not what. I’ll love thee much.
Let me unseal the letter.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Madam, I had rather—

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I know your lady does not love her husband.
I am sure of that. And at her late being here
She gave strange oeillades and most speaking looks
To noble Edmund. I know you are of her bosom.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I know your lady does not love her husband.
I am sure of that. And at her late being here
She gave strange oeillades and most speaking looks
To noble Edmund. I know you are of her bosom.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I know your lady does not love her husband.
I am sure of that. And at her late being here
She gave strange oeillades and most speaking looks
To noble Edmund. I know you are of her bosom.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: I, madam?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I speak in understanding. Y' are. I know ’t.
Therefore I do advise you, take this note.
My lord is dead. Edmund and I have talked,
And more convenient is he for my hand
Than for your lady’s. You may gather more.
If you do find him, pray you give him this.
And when your mistress hears thus much from you,
I pray desire her call her wisdom to her.
So fare you well.
If you do chance to hear of that blind traitor,
Preferment falls on him that cuts him off.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
Would I could meet him, madam, I should show
What party I do follow.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
Would I could meet him, madam, I should show
What party I do follow.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Fare thee well.
Exeunt severally
Enter GLOUCESTER, and EDGAR disguised in peasant clothing

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: When shall we come to th' top of that same hill?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: You do climb up it now. Look how we labor.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: You do climb up it now. Look how we labor.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Methinks the ground is even.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Horrible steep.
Hark, do you hear the sea?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Horrible steep.
Hark, do you hear the sea?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: No, truly.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Why then, your other senses grow imperfect
By your eyes' anguish.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
So may it be indeed.
Methinks thy voice is altered, and thou speak’st
In better phrase and matter than thou didst.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
So may it be indeed.
Methinks thy voice is altered, and thou speak’st
In better phrase and matter than thou didst.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
You’re much deceived. In nothing am I changed
But in my garments.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
You’re much deceived. In nothing am I changed
But in my garments.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Methinks you’re better spoken.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Come on, sir. Here’s the place. Stand still. How
fearful
And dizzy ’tis to cast one’s eyes so low!
The crows and choughs that wing the midway air
Show scarce so gross as beetles. Halfway down
Hangs one that gathers samphire—dreadful trade!
Methinks he seems no bigger than his head.
The fishermen that walk upon the beach
Appear like mice. And yon tall anchoring bark,
Diminished to her cock, her cock a buoy
Almost too small for sight. The murmuring surge
That on th' unnumbered idle pebbles chafes
Cannot be heard so high. I’ll look no more
Lest my brain turn and the deficient sight
Topple down headlong.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Set me where you stand.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Give me your hand. You are now within a foot
Of th' extreme verge. For all beneath the moon
Would I not leap upright.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Give me your hand. You are now within a foot
Of th' extreme verge. For all beneath the moon
Would I not leap upright.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Give me your hand. You are now within a foot
Of th' extreme verge. For all beneath the moon
Would I not leap upright.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Let go my hand.
[gives EDGAR another purse]
Here, friend, ’s another purse, in it a jewel
Well worth a poor man’s taking. Fairies and gods
Prosper it with thee! Go thou farther off.
Bid me farewell, and let me hear thee going.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: Now fare you well, good sir.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: With all my heart.
EDGAR moves aside

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside] Why I do trifle thus with his despair
Is done to cure it.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
O you mighty gods, [kneels]
This world I do renounce, and in your sights
Shake patiently my great affliction off.
If I could bear it longer and not fall
To quarrel with your great opposeless wills,
My snuff and loathèd part of nature should
Burn itself out. If Edgar live, O, bless him!—
Now, fellow, fare thee well. (falls)

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Gone, sir. Farewell.
[aside] And yet I know not how conceit may rob
The treasury of life when life itself
Yields to the theft. Had he been where he thought,
By this had thought been past. Alive or dead?—
Ho you, sir, friend! Hear you, sir? Speak.—
Thus might he pass indeed. Yet he revives.—
What are you, sir?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Away, and let me die.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Hadst thou been aught but gossamer, feathers, air,
So many fathom down precipitating,
Thou’dst shivered like an egg. But thou dost breathe,
Hast heavy substance, bleed’st not, speak’st, art
sound.
Ten masts at each make not the altitude
Which thou hast perpendicularly fell.
Thy life’s a miracle. Speak yet again.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Hadst thou been aught but gossamer, feathers, air,
So many fathom down precipitating,
Thou’dst shivered like an egg. But thou dost breathe,
Hast heavy substance, bleed’st not, speak’st, art
sound.
Ten masts at each make not the altitude
Which thou hast perpendicularly fell.
Thy life’s a miracle. Speak yet again.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
From the dread summit of this chalky bourn.
Look up a-height. The shrill-gorged lark so far
Cannot be seen or heard. Do but look up.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Alack, I have no eyes.
Is wretchedness deprived that benefit,
To end itself by death? 'Twas yet some comfort
When misery could beguile the tyrant’s rage
And frustrate his proud will.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
This is above all strangeness.
Upon the crown o' th' cliff, what thing was that
Which parted from you?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
This is above all strangeness.
Upon the crown o' th' cliff, what thing was that
Which parted from you?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: A poor unfortunate beggar.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
As I stood here below, methought his eyes
Were two full moons. He had a thousand noses,
Horns whelked and waved like the enragèd sea.
It was some fiend. Therefore, thou happy father,
Think that the clearest gods, who make them honors
Of men’s impossibilities, have preserved thee.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
I do remember now. Henceforth I’ll bear
Affliction till it do cry out itself,
“Enough, enough,” and die. That thing you speak of,
I took it for a man. Often ’twould say,
“The fiend, the fiend!” He led me to that place.

sentences.text: Bear free and patient thoughts.
Enter LEAR, mad

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
But who comes here?
The safer sense will ne'er accommodate
His master thus.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
No, they cannot touch me for coining. I am the king
himself.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
No, they cannot touch me for coining. I am the king

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: [aside] O thou side-piercing sight!

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Nature’s above art in that respect. There’s your press-
money. That fellow handles his bow like a crowkeeper.
Draw me a clothier’s yard. Look, look, a mouse!
Peace, peace, this piece of toasted cheese will do ’t.
There’s my gauntlet. I’ll prove it on a giant. Bring up
the brown bills. O, well flown, bird. I' th' clout, i'
th' clout. Hewgh! Give the word.

sentences.text: Sweet marjoram.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Pass.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Pass.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: I know that voice.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Ha! Goneril with a white beard? Ha, Regan? They
flattered me like a dog and told me I had white hairs in
my beard ere the black ones were there. To say “Ay” and
“No” to everything that I said “Ay” and “No” to was no
good divinity. When the rain came to wet me once, and
the wind to make me chatter, when the thunder would not
peace at my bidding—there I found 'em, there I smelt 'em
out. Go to, they are not men o' their words. They told
me I was everything. 'Tis a lie, I am not ague-proof.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
The trick of that voice I do well remember.
Is ’t not the king?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
The trick of that voice I do well remember.
Is ’t not the king?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Ay, every inch a king!
When I do stare, see how the subject quakes.
I pardon that man’s life. What was thy cause?
Adultery?
Thou shalt not die. Die for adultery? No.
The wren goes to ’t, and the small gilded fly
Does lecher in my sight.
Let copulation thrive, for Gloucester’s bastard son
Was kinder to his father than my daughters
Got ’tween the lawful sheets. To ’t, luxury, pell-mell!
For I lack soldiers. Behold yond simpering dame,
Whose face between her forks presages snow;
That minces virtue and does shake the head
To hear of pleasure’s name.
The fitchew, nor the soiled horse, goes to ’t
With a more riotous appetite.
Down from the waist they are centaurs,
Though women all above.
But to the girdle do the gods inherit.
Beneath is all the fiends'; there’s hell, there’s
darkness,
There’s the sulphurous pit— burning, scalding,
Stench, consumption! Fie, fie, fie, pah, pah!
Give me an ounce of civet, good apothecary,
To sweeten my imagination.
There’s money for thee.

But to the girdle do the gods inherit.
Beneath is all the fiends'; there’s hell, there’s
darkness,
There’s the sulphurous pit— burning, scalding,
Stench, consumption! Fie, fie, fie, pah, pah!
Give me an ounce of civet, good apothecary,
To sweeten my imagination.
There’s money for thee.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Ay, every inch a king!
When I do stare, see how the subject quakes.
I pardon that man’s life. What was thy cause?
Adultery?
Thou shalt not die. Die for adultery? No.
The wren goes to ’t, and the small gilded fly
Does lecher in my sight.
Let copulation thrive, for Gloucester’s bastard son
Was kinder to his father than my daughters
Got ’tween the lawful sheets. To ’t, luxury, pell-mell!
For I lack soldiers. Behold yond simpering dame,
Whose face between her forks presages snow;
That minces virtue and does shake the head
To hear of pleasure’s name.
The fitchew, nor the soiled horse, goes to ’t

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
O ruined piece of nature! This great world
Shall so wear out to naught. Dost thou know me?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
I remember thine eyes well enough. Dost thou squiny at
me? No, do thy worst, blind Cupid. I’ll not love. Read
thou this challenge. Mark but the penning of it.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: What, with the case of eyes?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Oh ho, are you there with me? No eyes in your head, nor
no money in your purse? Your eyes are in a heavy case,
your purse in a light. Yet you see how this world goes.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: I see it feelingly.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
What, art mad? A man may see how this world goes with
no eyes. Look with thine ears. See how yon justice rails
upon yon simple thief. Hark in thine ear: change places
and, handy-dandy, which is the justice, which is the
thief? Thou hast seen a farmer’s dog bark at a beggar?

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Ay, sir.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
And the creature run from the cur? There thou mightst
behold the great image of authority: a dog’s obeyed in
office.
Thou rascal beadle, hold thy bloody hand.
Why dost thou lash that whore? Strip thine own back.
Thou hotly lust’st to use her in that kind
For which thou whipp’st her. The usurer hangs the
cozener.
Through tattered clothes great vices do appear;
Robes and furred gowns hide all. Plate sin with gold,
And the strong lance of justice hurtless breaks.
Arm it in rags, a pigmy’s straw does pierce it.
None does offend—none, I say, none. I’ll able 'em.
Take that of me, my friend, who have the power
To seal th' accuser’s lips. Get thee glass eyes,
And like a scurvy politician seem
To see the things thou dost not. Now, now, now, now,
Pull off my boots. Harder, harder. So.

For which thou whipp’st her. The usurer hangs the
cozener.
Through tattered clothes great vices do appear;
Robes and furred gowns hide all. Plate sin with gold,
And the strong lance of justice hurtless breaks.
Arm it in rags, a pigmy’s straw does pierce it.
None does offend—none, I say, none. I’ll able 'em.
Take that of me, my friend, who have the power
To seal th' accuser’s lips. Get thee glass eyes,
And like a scurvy politician seem
To see the things thou dost not. Now, now, now, now,
Pull off my boots. Harder, harder. So.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
And the creature run from the cur? There thou mightst
behold the great image of authority: a dog’s obeyed in
office.
Thou rascal beadle, hold thy bloody hand.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside] O matter and impertinency mixed! Reason in
madness!

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[aside] O matter and impertinency mixed! Reason in
madness!

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
If thou wilt weep my fortunes, take my eyes.
I know thee well enough. Thy name is Gloucester.
Thou must be patient. We came crying hither.
Thou know’st the first time that we smell the air
We wawl and cry. I will preach to thee. Mark me.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Alack, alack the day!

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
When we are born, we cry that we are come
To this great stage of fools. This a good block.
It were a delicate stratagem to shoe
A troop of horse with felt. I’ll put ’t in proof.
And when I have stol'n upon these sons-in-law,
Then, kill, kill, kill, kill, kill, kill!

Enter GENTLEMAN with two others

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Oh, here he is. Lay hand upon him.—Sir,
Your most dear daughter—

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Oh, here he is. Lay hand upon him.—Sir,
Your most dear daughter—

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
No rescue? What, a prisoner? I am even
The natural fool of fortune. Use me well.
You shall have ransom. Let me have surgeons.
I am cut to th' brains.

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: You shall have anything.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
No seconds? All myself?
Why, this would make a man a man of salt,
To use his eyes for garden water-pots,
Ay, and laying autumn’s dust.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Good sir—

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
I will die bravely, like a smug bridegroom.
What, I will be jovial. Come, come.
I am a king, my masters, know you that?

What, I will be jovial. Come, come.
I am a king, my masters, know you that?

What, I will be jovial. Come, come.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: You are a royal one, and we obey you.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Then there’s life in ’t. Come, an if you get it, you
shall get it by running. Sa, sa, sa, sa.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Then there’s life in ’t. Come, an if you get it, you
shall get it by running. Sa, sa, sa, sa.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:

shall get it by running. Sa, sa, sa, sa.
Exit LEAR running, followed by two gentlemen

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
A sight most pitiful in the meanest wretch,
Past speaking of in a king. Thou hast a daughter
Who redeems nature from the general curse
Which twain have brought her to.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
A sight most pitiful in the meanest wretch,
Past speaking of in a king. Thou hast a daughter
Who redeems nature from the general curse
Which twain have brought her to.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: Hail, gentle sir.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Sir, speed you. What’s your will?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Sir, speed you. What’s your will?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: Do you hear aught, sir, of a battle toward?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Most sure and vulgar. Everyone hears that
That can distinguish sound.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Most sure and vulgar. Everyone hears that
That can distinguish sound.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
But, by your favor,
How near’s the other army?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Near and on speedy foot. The main descry
Stands in the hourly thought.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Near and on speedy foot. The main descry
Stands in the hourly thought.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: I thank you, sir. That’s all.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: I thank you, sir. That’s all.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Though that the queen on special cause is here,
Her army is moved on.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: I thank you, sir.
Exit GENTLEMAN

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
You ever gentle gods, take my breath from me.
Let not my worser spirit tempt me again
To die before you please.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
You ever gentle gods, take my breath from me.
Let not my worser spirit tempt me again
To die before you please.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
You ever gentle gods, take my breath from me.
Let not my worser spirit tempt me again
To die before you please.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: Well pray you, father.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: Now, good sir, what are you?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
A most poor man made tame to fortune’s blows,
Who by the art of known and feeling sorrows
Am pregnant to good pity. Give me your hand,
I’ll lead you to some biding.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:

Who by the art of known and feeling sorrows
Am pregnant to good pity. Give me your hand,
I’ll lead you to some biding.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Hearty thanks.
The bounty and the benison of heaven
To boot and boot.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Hearty thanks.
The bounty and the benison of heaven
To boot and boot.
Enter OSWALD the steward

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
A proclaimed prize! Most happy!
That eyeless head of thine was first framed flesh
To raise my fortunes. Thou old unhappy traitor,
Briefly thyself remember. The sword is out
That must destroy thee.

sentences.text:

Briefly thyself remember. The sword is out
That must destroy thee.

Briefly thyself remember. The sword is out

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Now let thy friendly hand
Put strength enough to ’t.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
Now let thy friendly hand
Put strength enough to ’t.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
Wherefore, bold peasant,
Darest thou support a published traitor? Hence,
Lest that th' infection of his fortune take
Like hold on thee. Let go his arm.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
Wherefore, bold peasant,
Darest thou support a published traitor? Hence,
Lest that th' infection of his fortune take
Like hold on thee. Let go his arm.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text:
Wherefore, bold peasant,
Darest thou support a published traitor? Hence,
Lest that th' infection of his fortune take
Like hold on thee. Let go his arm.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text: 'Chill not let go, zir, without vurther 'casion.

sentences.speaker: OSWALD
sentences.text: Let go, slave, or thou diest!

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Good gentleman, go your gait, and let poor volk pass.
An 'chud ha' bin zwaggered out of my life, ’twould not
ha' bin zo long as ’tis by a vortnight. Nay, come not
near th' old man. Keep out, che vor' ye, or I’se try
whether your costard or my ballow be the harder. 'Chill
be plain with you.

ha' bin zo long as ’tis by a vortnight. Nay, come not
near th' old man. Keep out, che vor' ye, or I’se try
whether your costard or my ballow be the harder. 'Chill
be plain with you.

EDGAR and OSWALD fight

[falling] Slave, thou hast slain me. Villain, take my
purse.
If ever thou wilt thrive, bury my body.
And give the letters which thou find’st about me
To Edmund, Earl of Gloucester. Seek him out
Upon the British party. O untimely death! [ dies]

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
I know thee well—a serviceable villain,
As duteous to the vices of thy mistress
As badness would desire.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text: What, is he dead?

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Sit you down, father. Rest you.
Let’s see these pockets. The letters that he speaks of
May be my friends. He’s dead. I am only sorry
He had no other death’s-man. Let us see.
[takes letters out of OSWALD’s pocket and opens them]
Leave, gentle wax, and, manners, blame us not.
To know our enemies' minds, we rip their hearts.
Their papers is more lawful.
[reads]
“Let our reciprocal vows be remembered. You have many
opportunities to cut him off. If your will want not,
time and place will be fruitfully offered. There is
nothing done if he return the conqueror. Then am I the
prisoner and his bed my gaol, from the loathed warmth
whereof deliver me, and supply the place for your labor.
Your—wife, so I would say—affectionate servant,
Goneril.”
O indistinguished space of woman’s will!
A plot upon her virtuous husband’s life,
And the exchange my brother!— Here in the sands
Thee I’ll rake up, the post unsanctified
Of murderous lechers. And in the mature time
With this ungracious paper strike the sight
Of the death-practiced duke. For him ’tis well
That of thy death and business I can tell.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER
sentences.text:
The king is mad. How stiff is my vile sense,
That I stand up and have ingenious feeling
Of my huge sorrows. Better I were distract—
So should my thoughts be severed from my griefs,
And woes by wrong imaginations lose
The knowledge of themselves.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Give me your hand.
Far off methinks I hear the beaten drum.
Come, father, I’ll bestow you with a friend.

Enter CORDELIA, KENT disguised, GENTLEMAN, and DOCTOR

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
O thou good Kent, how shall I live and work
To match thy goodness? My life will be too short,
And every measure fail me.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
O thou good Kent, how shall I live and work
To match thy goodness? My life will be too short,
And every measure fail me.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
To be acknowledged, madam, is o'erpaid.
All my reports go with the modest truth,
Nor more, nor clipped, but so.

These weeds are memories of those worser hours.
I prithee, put them off.

Pardon, dear madam.
Yet to be known shortens my made intent.
My boon I make it that you know me not
Till time and I think meet.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Pardon, dear madam.
Yet to be known shortens my made intent.
My boon I make it that you know me not
Till time and I think meet.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
Then be ’t so, my good lord.—
How does the king?

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text: Madam, sleeps still.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
O you kind gods,
Cure this great breach in his abusèd nature,
Th' untuned and jarring senses, O, wind up,
Of this child-changèd father!

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
O you kind gods,
Cure this great breach in his abusèd nature,
Th' untuned and jarring senses, O, wind up,
Of this child-changèd father!

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text:
So please your majesty
That we may wake the king? He hath slept long.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text:
So please your majesty
That we may wake the king? He hath slept long.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
Be governed by your knowledge, and proceed
I' th' sway of your own will. Is he arrayed?

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
Be governed by your knowledge, and proceed
I' th' sway of your own will. Is he arrayed?
Enter LEAR asleep in a chair carried by servants

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Ay, madam. In the heaviness of his sleep
We put fresh garments on him.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Ay, madam. In the heaviness of his sleep
We put fresh garments on him.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text:
Be by, good madam, when we do awake him.
I doubt not of his temperance.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text:
Be by, good madam, when we do awake him.
I doubt not of his temperance.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text: Very well.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text: Please you, draw near.—Louder the music there!

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text: Please you, draw near.—Louder the music there!

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
[kisses LEAR] O my dear father, restoration hang
Thy medicine on my lips, and let this kiss
Repair those violent harms that my two sisters
Have in thy reverence made!

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Kind and dear princess!

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
Had you not been their father, these white flakes
Did challenge pity of them. Was this a face
To be opposed against the warring winds?
To stand against the deep dread-bolted thunder
In the most terrible and nimble stroke
Of quick cross lightning? To watch—poor perdu!—
With this thin helm? Mine enemy’s meanest dog,
Though he had bit me, should have stood that night
Against my fire. And wast thou fain, poor father,
To hovel thee with swine and rogues forlorn
In short and musty straw? Alack, alack!
'Tis wonder that thy life and wits at once
Had not concluded all.—He wakes. Speak to him.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text: Madam, do you. 'Tis fittest.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text: Madam, do you. 'Tis fittest.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text: How does my royal lord? How fares your majesty?

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text: How does my royal lord? How fares your majesty?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
You do me wrong to take me out o' th' grave.
Thou art a soul in bliss, but I am bound
Upon a wheel of fire, that mine own tears
Do scald like molten lead.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
You do me wrong to take me out o' th' grave.
Thou art a soul in bliss, but I am bound
Upon a wheel of fire, that mine own tears
Do scald like molten lead.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text: Sir, do you know me?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: You are a spirit, I know. Where did you die?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: You are a spirit, I know. Where did you die?

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text: [aside to DOCTOR] Still, still far wide!

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text: He’s scarce awake. Let him alone awhile.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text: He’s scarce awake. Let him alone awhile.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Where have I been? Where am I? Fair daylight?
I am mightily abused. I should ev'n die with pity
To see another thus. I know not what to say.
I will not swear these are my hands. Let’s see.
I feel this pinprick. Would I were assured
Of my condition.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
[kneels]
O, look upon me, sir,
And hold your hands in benediction o'er me.
No, sir, you must not kneel.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
[kneels]

And hold your hands in benediction o'er me.
No, sir, you must not kneel.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
[kneels]
O, look upon me, sir,
And hold your hands in benediction o'er me.
No, sir, you must not kneel.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Pray, do not mock me.
I am a very foolish fond old man,
Fourscore and upward, not an hour more nor less.
And to deal plainly
I fear I am not in my perfect mind.
Methinks I should know you, and know this man.
Yet I am doubtful, for I am mainly ignorant
What place this is, and all the skill I have
Remembers not these garments. Nor I know not
Where I did lodge last night. Do not laugh at me,
For as I am a man, I think this lady
To be my child Cordelia.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text: And so I am, I am.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Be your tears wet? Yes, faith. I pray, weep not.
If you have poison for me, I will drink it.
I know you do not love me, for your sisters
Have, as I do remember, done me wrong.
You have some cause; they have not.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text: No cause, no cause.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Am I in France?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: In your own kingdom, sir.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text: Do not abuse me.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text:
Be comforted, good madam. The great rage,
You see, is killed in him. And yet it is danger
To make him even o'er the time he has lost.
Desire him to go in. Trouble him no more
Till further settling.

sentences.speaker: DOCTOR
sentences.text:

Desire him to go in. Trouble him no more
Till further settling.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text: Will ’t please your highness walk?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
You must bear with me.
Pray you now, forget and forgive.
I am old and foolish.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
You must bear with me.
Pray you now, forget and forgive.
I am old and foolish.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
You must bear with me.
Pray you now, forget and forgive.
I am old and foolish.
Exeunt. Manent KENT and GENTLEMAN.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Holds it true, sir, that the Duke of Cornwall was so
slain?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: Most certain, sir.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Who is conductor of his people?

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text: As ’tis said, the bastard son of Gloucester.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
They say Edgar, his banished son, is with the Earl of
Kent in Germany.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Report is changeable. Tis time to look about. The
powers of the kingdom approach apace.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Report is changeable. Tis time to look about. The
powers of the kingdom approach apace.

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
Report is changeable. Tis time to look about. The
powers of the kingdom approach apace.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
The arbitrement is like to be bloody. Fare you well,
sir.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
The arbitrement is like to be bloody. Fare you well,
sir.
Exit GENTLEMAN

sentences.speaker: KENT
sentences.text:
My point and period will be throughly wrought,
Or well or ill, as this day’s battle’s fought.
Exit
Enter with drum and colors EDMUND, REGAN, gentlemen, and soldiers

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
[To a gentleman] Know of the duke if his last purpose
hold,
Or whether since he is advised by aught
To change the course. He’s full of alteration
And self-reproving. Bring his constant pleasure.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:

hold,
Or whether since he is advised by aught

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Our sister’s man is certainly miscarried.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: 'Tis to be doubted, madam.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Now, sweet lord,
You know the goodness I intend upon you.
Tell me but truly— but then speak the truth—
Do you not love my sister?

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
Now, sweet lord,
You know the goodness I intend upon you.
Tell me but truly— but then speak the truth—
Do you not love my sister?

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: In honored love.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
But have you never found my brother’s way
To the forfended place?

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: That thought abuses you.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I am doubtful that you have been conjunct
And bosomed with her as far as we call hers.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: No, by mine honor, madam.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: No, by mine honor, madam.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I never shall endure her. Dear my lord,
Be not familiar with her.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text:
I never shall endure her. Dear my lord,
Be not familiar with her.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
Fear me not.
She and the duke her husband!

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
Fear me not.
She and the duke her husband!
Enter with drum and colors ALBANY and GONERIL, with troops

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
[aside] I had rather lose the battle than that sister
Should loosen him and me.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
Our very loving sister, well be-met.
Sir, this I hear: the king is come to his daughter,
With others whom the rigor of our state
Forced to cry out. Where I could not be honest
I never yet was valiant. For this business,
It touches us as France invades our land,
Not bolds the king, with others whom I fear
Most just and heavy causes make oppose.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Sir, you speak nobly.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Why is this reasoned?

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:
Combine together 'gainst the enemy,
For these domestic and particular broils
Are not the question here.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text:

Are not the question here.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
Let’s then determine with the ancient of war
On our proceedings.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: I shall attend you presently at your tent.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: Sister, you’ll go with us?

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: No.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: 'Tis most convenient. Pray you, go with us.

sentences.speaker: REGAN
sentences.text: 'Tis most convenient. Pray you, go with us.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: [aside] Oh ho, I know the riddle.—I will go.

sentences.speaker: GONERIL
sentences.text: [aside] Oh ho, I know the riddle.—I will go.
Enter EDGAR disguised

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[to ALBANY] If e'er your grace had speech with man so
poor,
Hear me one word.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text:
[To EDMUND, REGAN, and GONERIL]
I’ll overtake you.—
Exeunt all but ALBANY and EDGAR

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: Speak.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
[Giving ALBANY a letter]
Before you fight the battle, ope this letter.
If you have victory, let the trumpet sound
For him that brought it. Wretched though I seem,
I can produce a champion that will prove
What is avouchèd there. If you miscarry,
Your business of the world hath so an end,
And machination ceases. Fortune love you.

sentences.text: Stay till I have read the letter.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
I was forbid it.
When time shall serve, let but the herald cry,
And I’ll appear again.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
I was forbid it.
When time shall serve, let but the herald cry,
And I’ll appear again.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: Why, fare thee well. I will o'erlook thy paper.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: Why, fare thee well. I will o'erlook thy paper.
Exit EDGAR
Enter EDMUND

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
The enemy’s in view. Draw up your powers.
[gives ALBANY a document]
Here is the guess of their true strength and forces
By diligent discovery, but your haste
Is now urged on you.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:

[gives ALBANY a document]
Here is the guess of their true strength and forces
By diligent discovery, but your haste
Is now urged on you.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
The enemy’s in view. Draw up your powers.
[gives ALBANY a document]
Here is the guess of their true strength and forces
By diligent discovery, but your haste
Is now urged on you.

sentences.speaker: ALBANY
sentences.text: We will greet the time.
Exit ALBANY

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
To both these sisters have I sworn my love,
Each jealous of the other as the stung
Are of the adder. Which of them shall I take?
Both? One? Or neither? Neither can be enjoyed
If both remain alive. To take the widow
Exasperates, makes mad her sister Goneril,
And hardly shall I carry out my side,
Her husband being alive. Now, then, we’ll use
His countenance for the battle, which being done,
Let her who would be rid of him devise
His speedy taking off. As for the mercy
Which he intends to Lear and to Cordelia,
The battle done and they within our power,
Shall never see his pardon, for my state
Stands on me to defend, not to debate.

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Here, father, take the shadow of this tree
For your good host. Pray that the right may thrive.
If ever I return to you again,
I’ll bring you comfort.

Alarum and retreat within
Enter EDGAR

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
Away, old man. Give me thy hand. Away!
King Lear hath lost, he and his daughter ta'en.
Give me thy hand. Come on.

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER

sentences.speaker: GLOUCESTER

sentences.speaker: EDGAR
sentences.text:
What, in ill thoughts again? Men must endure
Their going hence even as their coming hither.
Ripeness is all. Come on.

Enter in conquest with drum and colors EDMUND, with LEAR and CORDELIA as prisoners, and FIRST CAPTAIN with soldiers
Enter in conquest with drum and colors EDMUND, with LEAR and CORDELIA as prisoners, and FIRST CAPTAIN with soldiers

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
Some officers take them away. Good guard
Until their greater pleasures first be known
That are to censure them.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text:
Some officers take them away. Good guard
Until their greater pleasures first be known
That are to censure them.

sentences.speaker: CORDELIA
sentences.text:
[to LEAR ]
We are not the first
Who with best meaning have incurred the worst.
For thee, oppressèd King, I am cast down.
Myself could else outfrown false fortune’s frown.
Shall we not see these daughters and these sisters?

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
No, no, no, no! Come, let’s away to prison.
We two alone will sing like birds i' th' cage.
When thou dost ask me blessing, I’ll kneel down
And ask of thee forgiveness. So we’ll live,
And pray, and sing, and tell old tales, and laugh
At gilded butterflies, and hear poor rogues
Talk of court news, and we’ll talk with them too—
Who loses and who wins, who’s in, who’s out—
And take upon ’s the mystery of things
As if we were God’s spies. And we’ll wear out
In a walled prison packs and sects of great ones
That ebb and flow by the moon.

sentences.speaker: EDMUND
sentences.text: Take them away.

sentences.speaker: LEAR
sentences.text:
Upon such sacrifices, my Cordelia,
The gods themselves throw incense. Have I caught thee?
He that parts us shall bring a brand from heaven
And fire us hence like foxes. Wipe thine eyes.
The good years shall devour them, flesh and fell,
Ere they shall make us weep. We’ll see 'em starve
first.
Come.
